question_html: If A and B are invertible matrices, then which of the following is not correct?
question_text: If A and B are invertible matrices, then which of the following is not correct?

options.explanation: Since, A and B are invertible matrices. So, we can say that (AB) -1 = B -1 A -1 ...(i) We know that, A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{|A|}{/tex} (adj A) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} adj A = |A| {tex}\cdot{/tex} A -1 ...(ii) Also, det (A) -1 = [det (A)] -1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} det (A) -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{[\operatorname{det}(A)]}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} det (A) {tex}\cdot{/tex} det (A) -1 = 1 ...(iii) Which is true, So, only option d is incorrect.

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If A and B are invertible matrices, then which of the following is not correct?
question_text: If A and B are invertible matrices, then which of the following is not correct?

options.explanation: Given A and B are invertible matrices. Now, {tex}(A \cdot B) B^{-1} A^{-1}{/tex} = A(BB -1 ) A -1 = AIA -1 = (AI)A -1 = AA -1 = I {tex}\Rightarrow (A B)^{-1}=B^{-1} A^{-1}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A A -1 = I {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |A A -1 | = |I| {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |A||A -1 | = 1 {tex}\Rightarrow \left|A^{-1}\right|=\frac{1}{|A|}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \operatorname{det}(A)^{-1}=[\operatorname{det}(A)]^{-1}{/tex} Also we know that {tex}A^{-1}=\frac{adj \cdot A}{|A|}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \text { adj. } A=|A| \cdot A^{-1}{/tex} {tex}(A+B)^{-1}=\frac{1}{|A+B|} \operatorname{adj} .(A+B){/tex} But {tex}B^{-1}+A^{-1}=\frac{1}{|B|} \text { adj. } B+\frac{1}{|A|} \operatorname{adj} . A{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow (A+B)^{-1} \neq B^{-1}+A^{-1}{/tex}

explanation: Given A and B are invertible matrices. Now, {tex}(A \cdot B) B^{-1} A^{-1}{/tex} = A(BB -1 ) A -1 = AIA -1 = (AI)A -1 = AA -1 = I {tex}\Rightarrow (A B)^{-1}=B^{-1} A^{-1}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A A -1 = I {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |A A -1 | = |I| {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |A||A -1 | = 1 {tex}\Rightarrow \left|A^{-1}\right|=\frac{1}{|A|}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \operatorname{det}(A)^{-1}=[\operatorname{det}(A)]^{-1}{/tex} Also we know that {tex}A^{-1}=\frac{adj \cdot A}{|A|}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \text { adj. } A=|A| \cdot A^{-1}{/tex} {tex}(A+B)^{-1}=\frac{1}{|A+B|} \operatorname{adj} .(A+B){/tex} But {tex}B^{-1}+A^{-1}=\frac{1}{|B|} \text { adj. } B+\frac{1}{|A|} \operatorname{adj} . A{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow (A+B)^{-1} \neq B^{-1}+A^{-1}{/tex}

question_html:
If {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2&0 \\ { - 2}&{ - 1}&{ - 2} \\ 0&{ - 1}&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}, then find the value of A–1.

Using A–1, solve the system of linear equations: 
x - 2y = 10,
2x - y - z = 8,
-2y +z = 7
question_text: If {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2&0 \\ { - 2}&{ - 1}&{ - 2} \\ 0&{ - 1}&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} , then find the value of A –1 . Using A –1 , solve the system of linear equations: x - 2y = 10, 2x - y - z = 8, -2y +z = 7
explanation: We have, {tex}A = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2&0 \\ { - 2}&{ - 1}&{ - 2} \\ 0&{ - 1}&1 \end{array}} \right|{/tex} ...(i) {tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = 1\left( { - 3} \right) - 2\left( { - 2} \right) + 0 = 1 \ne 0{/tex} Now, {tex}{A_{11}} = - 3,{A_{12}} = 2,{A_{13}} = 2,{/tex} {tex}{A_{21}} = - 2,{A_{22}} = 1,{A_{23}} = 1,{/tex} {tex}{A_{31}} = - 4,{A_{32}} = 2{/tex} and {tex}{A_{33}} = 3{/tex} {tex}\therefore adj\left( A \right) = {\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&2&2 \\ { - 2}&1&1 \\ { - 4}&2&3 \end{array}} \right|^T} = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&{ - 2}&{ - 4} \\ 2&1&2 \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right|{/tex} {tex}\therefore {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{{adjA}}{{\left| A \right|}}{/tex} {tex} = \frac{1}{1}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&{ - 2}&{ - 4} \\ 2&1&2 \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {A^{ - 1}} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&{ - 2}&{ - 4} \\ 2&1&2 \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} ...(i) Also, we have the system of linear equations as {tex}x - 2y = 10{/tex} {tex}2x - y - z = 8{/tex} and {tex} - 2y+ z = 7{/tex} Now, the given system of equations can be rewritten in the form AX=B, where, {tex}A= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2&0 \\ 2&{ - 1}&{ - 1} \\ 0&{ - 2}&1 \end{array}} \right]X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {10} \\ 8 \\ 7 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}{/tex} Since A is non singular , therefore given system of equations has a unique solution given by, {tex}\therefore X = {A^{ - 1}}B{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&2&2 \\ { - 2}&1&1 \\ { - 4}&2&3 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {10} \\ 8 \\ 7 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 30 + 60 + 14} \\ { - 20 + 8 + 7} \\ { - 40 + 16 + 21} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0 \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore x = 0,y = - 5{/tex} and {tex}z = - 3{/tex}
solution: We have, {tex}A = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2&0 \\ { - 2}&{ - 1}&{ - 2} \\ 0&{ - 1}&1 \end{array}} \right|{/tex} ...(i) {tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = 1\left( { - 3} \right) - 2\left( { - 2} \right) + 0 = 1 \ne 0{/tex} Now, {tex}{A_{11}} = - 3,{A_{12}} = 2,{A_{13}} = 2,{/tex} {tex}{A_{21}} = - 2,{A_{22}} = 1,{A_{23}} = 1,{/tex} {tex}{A_{31}} = - 4,{A_{32}} = 2{/tex} and {tex}{A_{33}} = 3{/tex} {tex}\therefore adj\left( A \right) = {\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&2&2 \\ { - 2}&1&1 \\ { - 4}&2&3 \end{array}} \right|^T} = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&{ - 2}&{ - 4} \\ 2&1&2 \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right|{/tex} {tex}\therefore {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{{adjA}}{{\left| A \right|}}{/tex} {tex} = \frac{1}{1}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&{ - 2}&{ - 4} \\ 2&1&2 \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow {A^{ - 1}} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&{ - 2}&{ - 4} \\ 2&1&2 \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} ...(i) Also, we have the system of linear equations as {tex}x - 2y = 10{/tex} {tex}2x - y - z = 8{/tex} and {tex} - 2y+ z = 7{/tex} Now, the given system of equations can be rewritten in the form AX=B, where, {tex}A= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2&0 \\ 2&{ - 1}&{ - 1} \\ 0&{ - 2}&1 \end{array}} \right]X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {10} \\ 8 \\ 7 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}{/tex} Since A is non singular , therefore given system of equations has a unique solution given by, {tex}\therefore X = {A^{ - 1}}B{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&2&2 \\ { - 2}&1&1 \\ { - 4}&2&3 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {10} \\ 8 \\ 7 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 30 + 60 + 14} \\ { - 20 + 8 + 7} \\ { - 40 + 16 + 21} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0 \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore x = 0,y = - 5{/tex} and {tex}z = - 3{/tex}
solution_html:
We have, {tex}A = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2&0 \\ { - 2}&{ - 1}&{ - 2} \\ 0&{ - 1}&1 \end{array}} \right|{/tex}...(i)

{tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = 1\left( { - 3} \right) - 2\left( { - 2} \right) + 0 = 1 \ne 0{/tex}

Now, {tex}{A_{11}} = - 3,{A_{12}} = 2,{A_{13}} = 2,{/tex}{tex}{A_{21}} = - 2,{A_{22}} = 1,{A_{23}} = 1,{/tex}{tex}{A_{31}} = - 4,{A_{32}} = 2{/tex} and {tex}{A_{33}} = 3{/tex}

{tex}\therefore adj\left( A \right) = {\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&2&2 \\ { - 2}&1&1 \\ { - 4}&2&3 \end{array}} \right|^T} = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&{ - 2}&{ - 4} \\ 2&1&2 \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right|{/tex}

{tex}\therefore {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{{adjA}}{{\left| A \right|}}{/tex}

{tex} = \frac{1}{1}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&{ - 2}&{ - 4} \\ 2&1&2 \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow {A^{ - 1}} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&{ - 2}&{ - 4} \\ 2&1&2 \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} ...(i)

Also, we have the system of linear equations as

{tex}x - 2y = 10{/tex}

{tex}2x - y - z = 8{/tex}

and {tex} - 2y+ z = 7{/tex}

Now, the given system of equations can be rewritten in the form AX=B,

where, {tex}A= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2&0 \\ 2&{ - 1}&{ - 1} \\ 0&{ - 2}&1 \end{array}} \right]X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]{/tex}and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {10} \\ 8 \\ 7 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}{/tex}Since A is non singular , therefore given system of equations has a unique solution given by,

{tex}\therefore X = {A^{ - 1}}B{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&2&2 \\ { - 2}&1&1 \\ { - 4}&2&3 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {10} \\ 8 \\ 7 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 30 + 60 + 14} \\ { - 20 + 8 + 7} \\ { - 40 + 16 + 21} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0 \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore x = 0,y = - 5{/tex} and {tex}z = - 3{/tex}

question_html:
If {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} find A-1, using A-1 solve the system of equations

{tex}2x - 3y + 5z = 11{/tex}

{tex}3x + 2y - 4z = - 5{/tex}

{tex}x + y - 2z = - 3{/tex}
question_text: If {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} find A -1 , using A -1 solve the system of equations {tex}2x - 3y + 5z = 11{/tex} {tex}3x + 2y - 4z = - 5{/tex} {tex}x + y - 2z = - 3{/tex}
explanation: {tex}\left| A \right| = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\left( A \right) = - 1 \ne 0{/tex} {tex}{A^{ - 1}}exists{/tex} Here, {tex}A_{11}=0,A_{12}=2,A_{13}=1\\ A_{21}=-1,A_{22}=-9,A_{23}=-5\\ A_{31}=2,A_{32}=23,A_{33}=13{/tex} {tex}{A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}\left( {adjA} \right){/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{{ - 1}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 1}&2 \\ 2&{ - 9}&{23} \\ 1&{ - 5}&{13} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 1}&2 \\ { - 2}&9&{ - 23} \\ { - 1}&5&{ - 13} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} The given system of equation can be written is Ax = B X= A -1 B {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&5&2 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = {\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&5&2 \end{array}} \right]^{ - 1}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 1}&{ - 1} \\ { - 2}&9&{ - 23} \\ { - 1}&5&{ - 13} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} x = 1 y = 2 z = 3
solution: {tex}\left| A \right| = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\left( A \right) = - 1 \ne 0{/tex} {tex}{A^{ - 1}}exists{/tex} Here, {tex}A_{11}=0,A_{12}=2,A_{13}=1\\ A_{21}=-1,A_{22}=-9,A_{23}=-5\\ A_{31}=2,A_{32}=23,A_{33}=13{/tex} {tex}{A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}\left( {adjA} \right){/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{{ - 1}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 1}&2 \\ 2&{ - 9}&{23} \\ 1&{ - 5}&{13} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 1}&2 \\ { - 2}&9&{ - 23} \\ { - 1}&5&{ - 13} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} The given system of equation can be written is Ax = B X= A -1 B {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&5&2 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = {\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&5&2 \end{array}} \right]^{ - 1}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 1}&{ - 1} \\ { - 2}&9&{ - 23} \\ { - 1}&5&{ - 13} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} x = 1 y = 2 z = 3
solution_html:
{tex}\left| A \right| = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\left( A \right) = - 1 \ne 0{/tex}

{tex}{A^{ - 1}}exists{/tex}

Here, {tex}A_{11}=0,A_{12}=2,A_{13}=1\\ A_{21}=-1,A_{22}=-9,A_{23}=-5\\ A_{31}=2,A_{32}=23,A_{33}=13{/tex}

{tex}{A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}\left( {adjA} \right){/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{{ - 1}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 1}&2 \\ 2&{ - 9}&{23} \\ 1&{ - 5}&{13} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 1}&2 \\ { - 2}&9&{ - 23} \\ { - 1}&5&{ - 13} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

The given system of equation can be written is Ax = B 

   X= A-1B

{tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&5&2 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = {\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&5&2 \end{array}} \right]^{ - 1}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 1}&{ - 1} \\ { - 2}&9&{ - 23} \\ { - 1}&5&{ - 13} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

x = 1

y = 2

z = 3

question_html: The area of a triangle with vertices {tex}(4,-1),(1, {k}){/tex} and ({tex}-2,-1{/tex}) is 9 sq units. The value of {tex}k{/tex} is:
question_text: The area of a triangle with vertices {tex}(4,-1),(1, {k}){/tex} and ( {tex}-2,-1{/tex} ) is 9 sq units. The value of {tex}k{/tex} is:

options.explanation: The area of a triangle with vertices (x1,y1), (x2,y2), and (x3,y3) is given by the formula: Area=12∣∣x1(y2−y3)+x2(y3−y1)+x3(y1−y2)∣∣ Substituting the given points (4,−1), (1,k), and (−2,−1): Area=12|4(k+1)+1(0)+(−2)(−1−k)|=12|6k+6|=3|k+1| Setting the area equal to 9: 3|k+1|=9⟹|k+1|=3⟹k+1=±3⟹k=2 or k=−4 Among the given options, only k=2 is present.

explanation: The area of a triangle with vertices (x1,y1), (x2,y2), and (x3,y3) is given by the formula: Area=12∣∣x1(y2−y3)+x2(y3−y1)+x3(y1−y2)∣∣ Substituting the given points (4,−1), (1,k), and (−2,−1): Area=12|4(k+1)+1(0)+(−2)(−1−k)|=12|6k+6|=3|k+1| Setting the area equal to 9: 3|k+1|=9⟹|k+1|=3⟹k+1=±3⟹k=2 or k=−4 Among the given options, only k=2 is present.

question_html: If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{rrr}3 & 2 & 1 \\ 4 & -1 & 2 \\ 7 & 3 & -3\end{array}\right]{/tex}, find {tex}A^{-1}{/tex}. Using {tex}A^{-1}{/tex}, solve the given system of equations {tex}3 {x}+4 {y}+7 {z}=14 ; 2 {x}-{y}+3 {z}{/tex} {tex}=4 ; x+2 {y}-3 {z}=0{/tex}.
question_text: If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{rrr}3 & 2 & 1 \\ 4 & -1 & 2 \\ 7 & 3 & -3\end{array}\right]{/tex} , find {tex}A^{-1}{/tex} . Using {tex}A^{-1}{/tex} , solve the given system of equations {tex}3 {x}+4 {y}+7 {z}=14 ; 2 {x}-{y}+3 {z}{/tex} {tex}=4 ; x+2 {y}-3 {z}=0{/tex} .
explanation: {tex}|A|=3(-3)-2(-26)+1(19)=62 \neq 0 \Rightarrow A^{-1}{/tex} exists. cofactor Matrix {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-3 & 26 & 19 \\ 9 & -16 & 5 \\ 5 & -2 & -11\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\operatorname{adj} A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-3 & 9 & 5 \\ 26 & -16 & -2 \\ 19 & 5 & -11\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}A^{-1}=\frac{1}{62}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-3 & 9 & 5 \\ 26 & -16 & -2 \\ 19 & 5 & -11\end{array}\right]{/tex} Given system of equations can be written as {tex}A^{\prime} \cdot X=B{/tex} where {tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right], B=\left[\begin{array}{c}14 \\ 4 \\ 0\end{array}\right]{/tex} Now, {tex}A^{\prime} . X=B \Rightarrow X=\left(A^{\prime}\right)^{-1} . B{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow X=\left(A^{-1}\right)^{\prime} \cdot B=\frac{1}{62}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-3 & 26 & 19 \\ 9 & -16 & 5 \\ 5 & -2 & -11\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}14 \\ 4 \\ 0\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{62}\left[\begin{array}{l}62 \\ 62 \\ 62\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l}1 \\ 1 \\ 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x=1, y=1, z=1{/tex}
solution: {tex}|A|=3(-3)-2(-26)+1(19)=62 \neq 0 \Rightarrow A^{-1}{/tex} exists. cofactor Matrix {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-3 & 26 & 19 \\ 9 & -16 & 5 \\ 5 & -2 & -11\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\operatorname{adj} A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-3 & 9 & 5 \\ 26 & -16 & -2 \\ 19 & 5 & -11\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}A^{-1}=\frac{1}{62}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-3 & 9 & 5 \\ 26 & -16 & -2 \\ 19 & 5 & -11\end{array}\right]{/tex} Given system of equations can be written as {tex}A^{\prime} \cdot X=B{/tex} where {tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right], B=\left[\begin{array}{c}14 \\ 4 \\ 0\end{array}\right]{/tex} Now, {tex}A^{\prime} . X=B \Rightarrow X=\left(A^{\prime}\right)^{-1} . B{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow X=\left(A^{-1}\right)^{\prime} \cdot B=\frac{1}{62}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-3 & 26 & 19 \\ 9 & -16 & 5 \\ 5 & -2 & -11\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}14 \\ 4 \\ 0\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{62}\left[\begin{array}{l}62 \\ 62 \\ 62\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l}1 \\ 1 \\ 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow x=1, y=1, z=1{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}|A|=3(-3)-2(-26)+1(19)=62 \neq 0 \Rightarrow A^{-1}{/tex} exists.
cofactor Matrix {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-3 & 26 & 19 \\ 9 & -16 & 5 \\ 5 & -2 & -11\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\operatorname{adj} A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-3 & 9 & 5 \\ 26 & -16 & -2 \\ 19 & 5 & -11\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}A^{-1}=\frac{1}{62}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-3 & 9 & 5 \\ 26 & -16 & -2 \\ 19 & 5 & -11\end{array}\right]{/tex}
Given system of equations can be written as {tex}A^{\prime} \cdot X=B{/tex}
where {tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right], B=\left[\begin{array}{c}14 \\ 4 \\ 0\end{array}\right]{/tex}
Now, {tex}A^{\prime} . X=B \Rightarrow X=\left(A^{\prime}\right)^{-1} . B{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow X=\left(A^{-1}\right)^{\prime} \cdot B=\frac{1}{62}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-3 & 26 & 19 \\ 9 & -16 & 5 \\ 5 & -2 & -11\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}14 \\ 4 \\ 0\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{62}\left[\begin{array}{l}62 \\ 62 \\ 62\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l}1 \\ 1 \\ 1\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow x=1, y=1, z=1{/tex}

question_html:
If A = {tex} \left[\begin{array}{rrr} 1 & -2 & 0 \\ 2 & -1 & -1 \\ 0 & -2 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, find A-1 and use it to solve the following system of equations:
x - 2y = 10, 2x - y - z = 8, -2y + z = 7
question_text: If A = {tex} \left[\begin{array}{rrr} 1 & -2 & 0 \\ 2 & -1 & -1 \\ 0 & -2 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , find A -1 and use it to solve the following system of equations: x - 2y = 10, 2x - y - z = 8, -2y + z = 7
explanation: |A| = 1 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 hence A -1 exists. {tex} \operatorname{Adj} A=\left[\begin{array}{lll} -3 & 2 & 2 \\ -2 & 1 & 1 \\ -4 & 2 & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}A^{-1}=\left[\begin{array}{lll} -3 & 2 & 2 \\ -2 & 1 & 1 \\ -4 & 2 & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex} {AX}={B} \Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{lll} 1 & -2 & 0 \\ 2 & -1 & -1 \\ 0 & -2 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 10 \\ 8 \\ 7 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} X=A^{-1} B \Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -3 & 2 & 2 \\ -2 & 1 & 1 \\ -4 & 2 & 3 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 10 \\ 8 \\ 7 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 0 \\ -5 \\ -3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 0, y = -5, z = -3
solution: |A| = 1 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 hence A -1 exists. {tex} \operatorname{Adj} A=\left[\begin{array}{lll} -3 & 2 & 2 \\ -2 & 1 & 1 \\ -4 & 2 & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}A^{-1}=\left[\begin{array}{lll} -3 & 2 & 2 \\ -2 & 1 & 1 \\ -4 & 2 & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex} {AX}={B} \Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{lll} 1 & -2 & 0 \\ 2 & -1 & -1 \\ 0 & -2 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 10 \\ 8 \\ 7 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} X=A^{-1} B \Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -3 & 2 & 2 \\ -2 & 1 & 1 \\ -4 & 2 & 3 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 10 \\ 8 \\ 7 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 0 \\ -5 \\ -3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 0, y = -5, z = -3
solution_html:
|A| = 1 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 hence A-1 exists.
{tex} \operatorname{Adj} A=\left[\begin{array}{lll} -3 & 2 & 2 \\ -2 & 1 & 1 \\ -4 & 2 & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}A^{-1}=\left[\begin{array}{lll} -3 & 2 & 2 \\ -2 & 1 & 1 \\ -4 & 2 & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex} {AX}={B} \Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{lll} 1 & -2 & 0 \\ 2 & -1 & -1 \\ 0 & -2 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 10 \\ 8 \\ 7 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
{tex} X=A^{-1} B \Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -3 & 2 & 2 \\ -2 & 1 & 1 \\ -4 & 2 & 3 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 10 \\ 8 \\ 7 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 0 \\ -5 \\ -3 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 0, y = -5, z = -3

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
Given A = {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{lll} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 2 & 3 & 2 \\ 1 & 1 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, find A-1. Use it to solve the following system of equations:
{tex} x+y+z=1 {/tex}
{tex} 2 x+3 y+2 z=2 {/tex}
{tex} x+y+2 z=4 {/tex}
question_text: Given A = {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{lll} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 2 & 3 & 2 \\ 1 & 1 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , find A -1 . Use it to solve the following system of equations: {tex} x+y+z=1 {/tex} {tex} 2 x+3 y+2 z=2 {/tex} {tex} x+y+2 z=4 {/tex}
explanation: {tex} |A|=1(4)-1(2)+1(-1)=1{/tex} Cofactors of the elements of A are: {tex} A_{11}=4, A_{12}=-2, A_{13}=-1 {/tex} {tex} A_{21}=-1, A_{22}=1, A_{23}=0 {/tex} {tex} A_{31}=-1, A_{32}=0, A_{33}=1 {/tex} {tex}\therefore \operatorname{adj} A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}4 & -1 & -1 \\ -2 & 1 & 0 \\ -1 & 0 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex} {A}^{-1}=\frac{\operatorname{adj} A}{|A|}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & -1 & -1 \\ -2 & 1 & 0 \\ -1 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Given system of equations can be written as {tex}{AX}={B}{/tex} , where {tex} {X}=\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right], {B}=\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 2 \\ 4 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} X={A}^{-1} {~B}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & -1 & -1 \\ -2 & 1 & 0 \\ -1 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 2 \\ 4 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} -2 \\ 0 \\ 3 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} x=-2, y=0, z=3{/tex}
solution: {tex} |A|=1(4)-1(2)+1(-1)=1{/tex} Cofactors of the elements of A are: {tex} A_{11}=4, A_{12}=-2, A_{13}=-1 {/tex} {tex} A_{21}=-1, A_{22}=1, A_{23}=0 {/tex} {tex} A_{31}=-1, A_{32}=0, A_{33}=1 {/tex} {tex}\therefore \operatorname{adj} A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}4 & -1 & -1 \\ -2 & 1 & 0 \\ -1 & 0 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex} {A}^{-1}=\frac{\operatorname{adj} A}{|A|}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & -1 & -1 \\ -2 & 1 & 0 \\ -1 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Given system of equations can be written as {tex}{AX}={B}{/tex} , where {tex} {X}=\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right], {B}=\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 2 \\ 4 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} X={A}^{-1} {~B}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & -1 & -1 \\ -2 & 1 & 0 \\ -1 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 2 \\ 4 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} -2 \\ 0 \\ 3 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} x=-2, y=0, z=3{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex} |A|=1(4)-1(2)+1(-1)=1{/tex}
Cofactors of the elements of A are:
{tex} A_{11}=4, A_{12}=-2, A_{13}=-1 {/tex}
{tex} A_{21}=-1, A_{22}=1, A_{23}=0 {/tex}
{tex} A_{31}=-1, A_{32}=0, A_{33}=1 {/tex}
{tex}\therefore \operatorname{adj} A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}4 & -1 & -1 \\ -2 & 1 & 0 \\ -1 & 0 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex}

{tex} {A}^{-1}=\frac{\operatorname{adj} A}{|A|}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & -1 & -1 \\ -2 & 1 & 0 \\ -1 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Given system of equations can be written as {tex}{AX}={B}{/tex}, where
{tex} {X}=\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right], {B}=\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 2 \\ 4 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
{tex} X={A}^{-1} {~B}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & -1 & -1 \\ -2 & 1 & 0 \\ -1 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 2 \\ 4 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} -2 \\ 0 \\ 3 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
{tex} x=-2, y=0, z=3{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
The month of September is celebrated as the Rashtriya Poshan Maah across the country. Following a healthy and well-balanced diet is crucial in order to supply the body with the proper nutrients it needs. A balanced diet also keeps us mentally fit and promotes improved level of energy.

Figure - 1

Figure - 2

A dietician wishes to minimize the cost of a diet involving two types of foods, food {tex}{X}({x} {kg}){/tex} and food {tex}{Y}({y} {kg}){/tex} which are available at the rate of ₹ {tex}16 / {kg}{/tex} and ₹ {tex}20 / {kg}{/tex} respectively. The feasible region satisfying the constraints is shown in Figure-2.

On the basis of the above information, answer the following questions:

  1. Identify and write all the constraints which determine the given feasible region in Figure-2. (2)
  2. If the objective is to minimize cost Z = 16x + 20y, find the values of x and y at which cost is minimum. Also, find minimum cost assuming that minimum cost is possible for the given unbounded region. (2)
question_text: The month of September is celebrated as the Rashtriya Poshan Maah across the country. Following a healthy and well-balanced diet is crucial in order to supply the body with the proper nutrients it needs. A balanced diet also keeps us mentally fit and promotes improved level of energy. Figure - 1 Figure - 2 A dietician wishes to minimize the cost of a diet involving two types of foods, food {tex}{X}({x} {kg}){/tex} and food {tex}{Y}({y} {kg}){/tex} which are available at the rate of ₹ {tex}16 / {kg}{/tex} and ₹ {tex}20 / {kg}{/tex} respectively. The feasible region satisfying the constraints is shown in Figure-2. On the basis of the above information, answer the following questions: Identify and write all the constraints which determine the given feasible region in Figure-2. (2) If the objective is to minimize cost Z = 16x + 20y, find the values of x and y at which cost is minimum. Also, find minimum cost assuming that minimum cost is possible for the given unbounded region. (2)
explanation: {tex} \text { (Constraints are } x+2 y \geq 10 {/tex} {tex} x+y \geq 6 {/tex} {tex} 3 x+y \geq 8 {/tex} {tex} x \geq 0 {/tex} {tex} y \geq 0 {/tex} Corner points Value of {tex}Z=16 x+20 y{/tex} {tex}{~A}(10,0){/tex} 160 {tex}{~B}(2,4){/tex} 112 {tex}{C}(1,5){/tex} 116 {tex}{D}(0,8){/tex} 160 The minimum cost is ₹ 112
solution: {tex} \text { (Constraints are } x+2 y \geq 10 {/tex} {tex} x+y \geq 6 {/tex} {tex} 3 x+y \geq 8 {/tex} {tex} x \geq 0 {/tex} {tex} y \geq 0 {/tex} Corner points Value of {tex}Z=16 x+20 y{/tex} {tex}{~A}(10,0){/tex} 160 {tex}{~B}(2,4){/tex} 112 {tex}{C}(1,5){/tex} 116 {tex}{D}(0,8){/tex} 160 The minimum cost is ₹ 112
solution_html:
1.  {tex} \text { (Constraints are } x+2 y \geq 10 {/tex}
    {tex} x+y \geq 6 {/tex}
    {tex} 3 x+y \geq 8 {/tex}
    {tex} x \geq 0 {/tex}
    {tex} y \geq 0 {/tex}
  2. Corner points Value of {tex}Z=16 x+20 y{/tex}
    {tex}{~A}(10,0){/tex} 160
    {tex}{~B}(2,4){/tex} 112
    {tex}{C}(1,5){/tex} 116
    {tex}{D}(0,8){/tex} 160
    The minimum cost is ₹ 112

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Solve the following system of equations, using matrices: {tex}\frac{2}{x}+\frac{3}{y}+\frac{10}{z}=4,{/tex} {tex}\frac{4}{x}-\frac{6}{y}+\frac{5}{z}=1, {/tex} {tex}\frac{6}{x}+\frac{9}{y}-\frac{20}{z}=2{/tex} where x, y, z {tex} \neq {/tex} 0.
question_text: Solve the following system of equations, using matrices: {tex}\frac{2}{x}+\frac{3}{y}+\frac{10}{z}=4,{/tex} {tex}\frac{4}{x}-\frac{6}{y}+\frac{5}{z}=1, {/tex} {tex}\frac{6}{x}+\frac{9}{y}-\frac{20}{z}=2{/tex} where x, y, z {tex} \neq {/tex} 0.
explanation: Given system of linear equations is equivalent to {tex}{AX}={B}{/tex} , where {tex} A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 3 & 10 \\ 4 & -6 & 5 \\ 6 & 9 & -20 \end{array}\right], X=\left[\begin{array}{c} \frac{1}{x} \\ \frac{1}{y} \\ \frac{1}{z} \end{array}\right], B=\left[\begin{array}{l} 4 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} |A|=1200 \neq 0{/tex} Cofactors of the elements of A are {tex} A_{11}=75, A_{12}=110, A_{13}=72 {/tex} {tex} A_{21}=150, A_{22}=-100, A_{23}=0 {/tex} {tex} A_{31}=75, A_{32}=30, A_{33}=-24 {/tex} {tex} \operatorname{adj} A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 75 & 150 & 75 \\ 110 & -100 & 30 \\ 72 & 0 & -24 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} A^{-1}=\frac{\operatorname{adj} A}{|A|}=\frac{1}{1200}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 75 & 150 & 75 \\ 110 & -100 & 30 \\ 72 & 0 & -24 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} X=A^{-1} B=\frac{1}{1200}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 75 & 150 & 75 \\ 110 & -100 & 30 \\ 72 & 0 & -24 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 4 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} =\frac{1}{1200}\left[\begin{array}{l} 600 \\ 400 \\ 240 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} \frac{1}{2} \\ \frac{1}{3} \\ \frac{1}{5} \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} \therefore {x}=2, {y}=3, {z}=5{/tex}
solution: Given system of linear equations is equivalent to {tex}{AX}={B}{/tex} , where {tex} A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 3 & 10 \\ 4 & -6 & 5 \\ 6 & 9 & -20 \end{array}\right], X=\left[\begin{array}{c} \frac{1}{x} \\ \frac{1}{y} \\ \frac{1}{z} \end{array}\right], B=\left[\begin{array}{l} 4 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} |A|=1200 \neq 0{/tex} Cofactors of the elements of A are {tex} A_{11}=75, A_{12}=110, A_{13}=72 {/tex} {tex} A_{21}=150, A_{22}=-100, A_{23}=0 {/tex} {tex} A_{31}=75, A_{32}=30, A_{33}=-24 {/tex} {tex} \operatorname{adj} A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 75 & 150 & 75 \\ 110 & -100 & 30 \\ 72 & 0 & -24 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} A^{-1}=\frac{\operatorname{adj} A}{|A|}=\frac{1}{1200}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 75 & 150 & 75 \\ 110 & -100 & 30 \\ 72 & 0 & -24 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} X=A^{-1} B=\frac{1}{1200}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 75 & 150 & 75 \\ 110 & -100 & 30 \\ 72 & 0 & -24 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 4 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} =\frac{1}{1200}\left[\begin{array}{l} 600 \\ 400 \\ 240 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} \frac{1}{2} \\ \frac{1}{3} \\ \frac{1}{5} \end{array}\right] {/tex} {tex} \therefore {x}=2, {y}=3, {z}=5{/tex}
solution_html:
Given system of linear equations is equivalent to {tex}{AX}={B}{/tex}, where
{tex} A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 3 & 10 \\ 4 & -6 & 5 \\ 6 & 9 & -20 \end{array}\right], X=\left[\begin{array}{c} \frac{1}{x} \\ \frac{1}{y} \\ \frac{1}{z} \end{array}\right], B=\left[\begin{array}{l} 4 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
{tex} |A|=1200 \neq 0{/tex}
Cofactors of the elements of A are
{tex} A_{11}=75, A_{12}=110, A_{13}=72 {/tex}
{tex} A_{21}=150, A_{22}=-100, A_{23}=0 {/tex}
{tex} A_{31}=75, A_{32}=30, A_{33}=-24 {/tex}
{tex} \operatorname{adj} A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 75 & 150 & 75 \\ 110 & -100 & 30 \\ 72 & 0 & -24 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
{tex} A^{-1}=\frac{\operatorname{adj} A}{|A|}=\frac{1}{1200}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 75 & 150 & 75 \\ 110 & -100 & 30 \\ 72 & 0 & -24 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
{tex} X=A^{-1} B=\frac{1}{1200}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 75 & 150 & 75 \\ 110 & -100 & 30 \\ 72 & 0 & -24 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 4 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
{tex} =\frac{1}{1200}\left[\begin{array}{l} 600 \\ 400 \\ 240 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} \frac{1}{2} \\ \frac{1}{3} \\ \frac{1}{5} \end{array}\right] {/tex}
{tex} \therefore {x}=2, {y}=3, {z}=5{/tex}

question_html:
If {tex}{A}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 2 & -3 \\ 2 & 0 & -3 \\ 1 & 2 & 0\end{array}\right]{/tex}, then find {tex}{A}^{-1}{/tex} and hence solve the following system of equations :
{tex}x+2 y-3 z=1{/tex}
{tex}2 x-3 z=2{/tex}
{tex}x+2 y=3{/tex}
question_text: If {tex}{A}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 2 & -3 \\ 2 & 0 & -3 \\ 1 & 2 & 0\end{array}\right]{/tex} , then find {tex}{A}^{-1}{/tex} and hence solve the following system of equations : {tex}x+2 y-3 z=1{/tex} {tex}2 x-3 z=2{/tex} {tex}x+2 y=3{/tex}
explanation: {tex}\|A\|=1(6)-2(3)-3(4)=-12 \neq 0, \therefore A^{-1}{/tex} exist {tex} \operatorname{adj} A =\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 6 & -6 & -6 \\ -3 & 3 & -3 \\ 4 & 0 & -4 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex} \therefore A^{-1} =-\frac{1}{12}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 6 & -6 & -6 \\ -3 & 3 & -3 \\ 4 & 0 & -4 \end{array}\right] {/tex} The given system of equations can be written as {tex}{A X}={B}, {X}=\left[\begin{array}{l}{x} \\ {y} \\ {z}\end{array}\right], {B}=\left[\begin{array}{l}1 \\ 2 \\ 3\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}X=A^{-1} B \Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=-\frac{1}{12}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 6 & -6 & -6 \\ -3 & 3 & -3 \\ 4 & 0 & -4 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 2 \\ 1 / 2 \\ 2 / 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} The solution of the given system of equations is: {tex}x=2, y=\frac{1}{2}, z=\frac{2}{3}{/tex}
solution: {tex}\|A\|=1(6)-2(3)-3(4)=-12 \neq 0, \therefore A^{-1}{/tex} exist {tex} \operatorname{adj} A =\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 6 & -6 & -6 \\ -3 & 3 & -3 \\ 4 & 0 & -4 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex} \therefore A^{-1} =-\frac{1}{12}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 6 & -6 & -6 \\ -3 & 3 & -3 \\ 4 & 0 & -4 \end{array}\right] {/tex} The given system of equations can be written as {tex}{A X}={B}, {X}=\left[\begin{array}{l}{x} \\ {y} \\ {z}\end{array}\right], {B}=\left[\begin{array}{l}1 \\ 2 \\ 3\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}X=A^{-1} B \Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=-\frac{1}{12}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 6 & -6 & -6 \\ -3 & 3 & -3 \\ 4 & 0 & -4 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 2 \\ 1 / 2 \\ 2 / 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} The solution of the given system of equations is: {tex}x=2, y=\frac{1}{2}, z=\frac{2}{3}{/tex}
solution_html:
{tex}\|A\|=1(6)-2(3)-3(4)=-12 \neq 0, \therefore A^{-1}{/tex} exist
{tex} \operatorname{adj} A =\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 6 & -6 & -6 \\ -3 & 3 & -3 \\ 4 & 0 & -4 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex} \therefore A^{-1} =-\frac{1}{12}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 6 & -6 & -6 \\ -3 & 3 & -3 \\ 4 & 0 & -4 \end{array}\right] {/tex}
The given system of equations can be written as {tex}{A X}={B}, {X}=\left[\begin{array}{l}{x} \\ {y} \\ {z}\end{array}\right], {B}=\left[\begin{array}{l}1 \\ 2 \\ 3\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}X=A^{-1} B \Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=-\frac{1}{12}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 6 & -6 & -6 \\ -3 & 3 & -3 \\ 4 & 0 & -4 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 2 \\ 1 / 2 \\ 2 / 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex}The solution of the given system of equations is: {tex}x=2, y=\frac{1}{2}, z=\frac{2}{3}{/tex}

question_html:
If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 0 & 2 \\ 3 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, find A–1 and hence solve the following system of linear equations:
x + y + z = 6
x + 2z = 7
3x + y + z = 12
question_text: If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 0 & 2 \\ 3 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , find A –1 and hence solve the following system of linear equations: x + y + z = 6 x + 2z = 7 3x + y + z = 12
explanation: Ist we will find the value of determinent i,e |A| = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{lll} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 0 & 2 \\ 3 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 1(0 - 2) - 1(1 - 6) + 1(1 - 0) = -2 + 5 + 1 = 4 Let C ij be the cofactor of the elements aij in A[a ij ]. Then, C 11 = {tex}(-1)^{1+1}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 0 & 2 \\ 1 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = -2 , C 12 = {tex}(-1)^{1+2}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 2 \\ 3 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 5, C 13 = {tex}(-1)^{1+3}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 0 \\ 3 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 1 C 21 = {tex}(-1)^{2+1}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 0, C 22 = {tex}(-1)^{2+2}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \\ 3 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = -2, C 23 = {tex}(-1)^{2+3}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \\ 3 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 2 C 31 = {tex}(-1)^{3+1}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \\ 0 & 2 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 2, C 32 = {tex}(-1)^{3+2}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 2 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = -1, C 33 = {tex}(-1)^{3+3}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 0 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = -1 adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & 5 & 1 \\ 0 & -2 & 2 \\ 2 & -1 & -1 \end{array}\right]^{T}{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & 0 & 2 \\ 5 & -2 & -1 \\ 1 & 2 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{|A|} a d j A{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & 0 & 2 \\ 5 & -2 & -1 \\ 1 & 2 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} X = A -1 B {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & 0 & 2 \\ 5 & -2 & -1 \\ 1 & 2 & -1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 6 \\ 7 \\ 12 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{c} -12+0+24 \\ 30-14-12 \\ 6+14-12 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{c} 12 \\ 4 \\ 8\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{12}{4}{/tex} , y = {tex}\frac{4}{4}{/tex} and z = {tex}\frac{8}{4}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 3, y = 1 and z = 2
solution: Ist we will find the value of determinent i,e |A| = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{lll} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 0 & 2 \\ 3 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 1(0 - 2) - 1(1 - 6) + 1(1 - 0) = -2 + 5 + 1 = 4 Let C ij be the cofactor of the elements aij in A[a ij ]. Then, C 11 = {tex}(-1)^{1+1}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 0 & 2 \\ 1 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = -2 , C 12 = {tex}(-1)^{1+2}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 2 \\ 3 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 5, C 13 = {tex}(-1)^{1+3}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 0 \\ 3 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 1 C 21 = {tex}(-1)^{2+1}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 0, C 22 = {tex}(-1)^{2+2}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \\ 3 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = -2, C 23 = {tex}(-1)^{2+3}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \\ 3 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 2 C 31 = {tex}(-1)^{3+1}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \\ 0 & 2 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 2, C 32 = {tex}(-1)^{3+2}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 2 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = -1, C 33 = {tex}(-1)^{3+3}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 0 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = -1 adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & 5 & 1 \\ 0 & -2 & 2 \\ 2 & -1 & -1 \end{array}\right]^{T}{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & 0 & 2 \\ 5 & -2 & -1 \\ 1 & 2 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{|A|} a d j A{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & 0 & 2 \\ 5 & -2 & -1 \\ 1 & 2 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} X = A -1 B {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & 0 & 2 \\ 5 & -2 & -1 \\ 1 & 2 & -1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 6 \\ 7 \\ 12 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{c} -12+0+24 \\ 30-14-12 \\ 6+14-12 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{c} 12 \\ 4 \\ 8\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{12}{4}{/tex} , y = {tex}\frac{4}{4}{/tex} and z = {tex}\frac{8}{4}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 3, y = 1 and z = 2
solution_html:
Ist we will find the value of determinent  i,e

|A| = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{lll} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 0 & 2 \\ 3 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} 
= 1(0 - 2) - 1(1 - 6) + 1(1 - 0)
= -2 + 5 + 1 = 4
Let Cij be the cofactor of the elements aij in A[aij]. Then,
C11= {tex}(-1)^{1+1}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 0 & 2 \\ 1 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = -2 , C12 = {tex}(-1)^{1+2}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 2 \\ 3 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 5, C13 = {tex}(-1)^{1+3}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 0 \\ 3 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 1
C21 = {tex}(-1)^{2+1}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 0, C22 = {tex}(-1)^{2+2}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \\ 3 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = -2, C23 = {tex}(-1)^{2+3}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \\ 3 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 2
C31 = {tex}(-1)^{3+1}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \\ 0 & 2 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 2, C32 = {tex}(-1)^{3+2}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 2 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = -1, C33 = {tex}(-1)^{3+3}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 0 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = -1
adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & 5 & 1 \\ 0 & -2 & 2 \\ 2 & -1 & -1 \end{array}\right]^{T}{/tex} 
= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & 0 & 2 \\ 5 & -2 & -1 \\ 1 & 2 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A-1 = {tex}\frac{1}{|A|} a d j A{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & 0 & 2 \\ 5 & -2 & -1 \\ 1 & 2 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} 
X = A-1B
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & 0 & 2 \\ 5 & -2 & -1 \\ 1 & 2 & -1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 6 \\ 7 \\ 12 \end{array}\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{c} -12+0+24 \\ 30-14-12 \\ 6+14-12 \end{array}\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{c} 12 \\ 4 \\ 8\end{array}\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{12}{4}{/tex}, y = {tex}\frac{4}{4}{/tex} and z = {tex}\frac{8}{4}{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 3, y = 1 and z = 2

Solve the following system of equations by matrix method:
x + 2y + 3z = 6
2x - y + z = 2
3x + 2y - 2z = 3
question_text: Solve the following system of equations by matrix method: x + 2y + 3z = 6 2x - y + z = 2 3x + 2y - 2z = 3
explanation: Here, AX = B i.e. {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {3} & {2} & {-2} \\ {1} & {2} & {3} \\ {2} & {-1} & {1} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} {3} \\ {6} \\ {2} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} X = A -1 B For A -1 , Co-factors are A 11 = 5, A 12 = 5, A 13 = -5, A 21 = 0, A 22 = 7, A 23 = 7, A 31 = 10, A 3 2 = 11, A 33 = 4 {tex}\therefore \quad \operatorname{adj} A=\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {5} & {5} & {-5} \\ {0} & {7} & {7} \\ {10} & {-11} & {4} \end{array}\right]^{T}=\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {5} & {0} & {10} \\ {5} & {7} & {-11} \\ {-5} & {7} & {4} \end{array}\right]{/tex} Also, |A| = 3(5) + 2(5) + (-2)(-5) = 35 {tex}\therefore \ A^{-1}=\frac{\operatorname{adj} A}{|A|}=\frac{1}{35}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{5} & {0} & {10} \\ {5} & {7} & {-11} \\ {-5} & {7} & {4}\end{array}\right]{/tex} Now X = A -1 B {tex}\Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right]=\frac{1}{35}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {5} & {0} & {10} \\ {5} & {7} & {-11} \\ {-5} & {7} & {4} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} {3} \\ {6} \\ {2} \end{array}\right]=\frac{1}{35}\left[\begin{array}{c} {15+20} \\ {15+42-22} \\ {-15+42+8} \end{array}\right]=\frac{1}{35}\left[\begin{array}{c} {35} \\ {35} \\ {35} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} {1} \\ {1} \\ {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 1, y = 1 and z = 1
solution: Here, AX = B i.e. {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {3} & {2} & {-2} \\ {1} & {2} & {3} \\ {2} & {-1} & {1} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} {3} \\ {6} \\ {2} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} X = A -1 B For A -1 , Co-factors are A 11 = 5, A 12 = 5, A 13 = -5, A 21 = 0, A 22 = 7, A 23 = 7, A 31 = 10, A 3 2 = 11, A 33 = 4 {tex}\therefore \quad \operatorname{adj} A=\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {5} & {5} & {-5} \\ {0} & {7} & {7} \\ {10} & {-11} & {4} \end{array}\right]^{T}=\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {5} & {0} & {10} \\ {5} & {7} & {-11} \\ {-5} & {7} & {4} \end{array}\right]{/tex} Also, |A| = 3(5) + 2(5) + (-2)(-5) = 35 {tex}\therefore \ A^{-1}=\frac{\operatorname{adj} A}{|A|}=\frac{1}{35}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{5} & {0} & {10} \\ {5} & {7} & {-11} \\ {-5} & {7} & {4}\end{array}\right]{/tex} Now X = A -1 B {tex}\Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right]=\frac{1}{35}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {5} & {0} & {10} \\ {5} & {7} & {-11} \\ {-5} & {7} & {4} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} {3} \\ {6} \\ {2} \end{array}\right]=\frac{1}{35}\left[\begin{array}{c} {15+20} \\ {15+42-22} \\ {-15+42+8} \end{array}\right]=\frac{1}{35}\left[\begin{array}{c} {35} \\ {35} \\ {35} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} {1} \\ {1} \\ {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 1, y = 1 and z = 1
solution_html:
Here, AX = B
i.e. {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {3} & {2} & {-2} \\ {1} & {2} & {3} \\ {2} & {-1} & {1} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} {3} \\ {6} \\ {2} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} X = A-1 B
For A-1,
Co-factors are
A11 = 5, A12 = 5, A13 = -5,
A21 = 0, A22 = 7, A23 = 7,
A31 = 10, A32 = 11, A33 = 4
{tex}\therefore \quad \operatorname{adj} A=\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {5} & {5} & {-5} \\ {0} & {7} & {7} \\ {10} & {-11} & {4} \end{array}\right]^{T}=\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {5} & {0} & {10} \\ {5} & {7} & {-11} \\ {-5} & {7} & {4} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Also, |A| = 3(5) + 2(5) + (-2)(-5) = 35
{tex}\therefore \ A^{-1}=\frac{\operatorname{adj} A}{|A|}=\frac{1}{35}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}{5} & {0} & {10} \\ {5} & {7} & {-11} \\ {-5} & {7} & {4}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
Now X = A-1 B
{tex}\Rightarrow\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right]=\frac{1}{35}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {5} & {0} & {10} \\ {5} & {7} & {-11} \\ {-5} & {7} & {4} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} {3} \\ {6} \\ {2} \end{array}\right]=\frac{1}{35}\left[\begin{array}{c} {15+20} \\ {15+42-22} \\ {-15+42+8} \end{array}\right]=\frac{1}{35}\left[\begin{array}{c} {35} \\ {35} \\ {35} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} {1} \\ {1} \\ {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 1, y = 1 and z = 1

question_html: Prove that the determinant {tex}\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x&{\sin \theta }&{\cos \theta } \\ { - \sin \theta }&{ - x}&1 \\ {\cos \theta }&1&x \end{array}} \right|{/tex} is independent of {tex}\theta {/tex}.
question_text: Prove that the determinant {tex}\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x&{\sin \theta }&{\cos \theta } \\ { - \sin \theta }&{ - x}&1 \\ {\cos \theta }&1&x \end{array}} \right|{/tex} is independent of {tex}\theta {/tex} .
explanation: Let {tex}\Delta = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x&{\sin \theta }&{\cos \theta } \\ { - \sin \theta }&{ - x}&1 \\ {\cos \theta }&1&x \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Expanding along first row, {tex}\Delta = x\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - x}&1 \\ 1&x \end{array}} \right| - \sin \theta \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - \sin \theta }&1 \\ {\cos \theta }&x \end{array}} \right| {/tex} {tex}+ \cos \theta \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - \sin \theta }&{ - x} \\ {\cos \theta }&1 \end{array}} \right|{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \Delta = x\left( { - {x^2} - 1} \right) - \sin \theta \left( { - x\sin \theta - \cos \theta } \right) {/tex} {tex}+ \cos \theta \left( { - \sin \theta + x\cos \theta } \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \Delta = - {x^3} - x + x{\sin ^2}\theta {/tex} {tex}+ \sin \theta \cos \theta - \sin \theta \cos \theta + x{\cos ^2}\theta{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \Delta = - {x^3} - x + x\left( {{{\sin }^2}\theta + {{\cos }^2}\theta } \right) {/tex} = -x 3 - x + x = - x 3 which is independent of {tex}\theta {/tex}
solution: Let {tex}\Delta = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x&{\sin \theta }&{\cos \theta } \\ { - \sin \theta }&{ - x}&1 \\ {\cos \theta }&1&x \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Expanding along first row, {tex}\Delta = x\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - x}&1 \\ 1&x \end{array}} \right| - \sin \theta \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - \sin \theta }&1 \\ {\cos \theta }&x \end{array}} \right| {/tex} {tex}+ \cos \theta \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - \sin \theta }&{ - x} \\ {\cos \theta }&1 \end{array}} \right|{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \Delta = x\left( { - {x^2} - 1} \right) - \sin \theta \left( { - x\sin \theta - \cos \theta } \right) {/tex} {tex}+ \cos \theta \left( { - \sin \theta + x\cos \theta } \right){/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \Delta = - {x^3} - x + x{\sin ^2}\theta {/tex} {tex}+ \sin \theta \cos \theta - \sin \theta \cos \theta + x{\cos ^2}\theta{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \Delta = - {x^3} - x + x\left( {{{\sin }^2}\theta + {{\cos }^2}\theta } \right) {/tex} = -x 3 - x + x = - x 3 which is independent of {tex}\theta {/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}\Delta = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x&{\sin \theta }&{\cos \theta } \\ { - \sin \theta }&{ - x}&1 \\ {\cos \theta }&1&x \end{array}} \right]{/tex} 

Expanding along first row,

{tex}\Delta = x\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - x}&1 \\ 1&x \end{array}} \right| - \sin \theta \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - \sin \theta }&1 \\ {\cos \theta }&x \end{array}} \right| {/tex} {tex}+ \cos \theta \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - \sin \theta }&{ - x} \\ {\cos \theta }&1 \end{array}} \right|{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \Delta = x\left( { - {x^2} - 1} \right) - \sin \theta \left( { - x\sin \theta - \cos \theta } \right) {/tex} {tex}+ \cos \theta \left( { - \sin \theta + x\cos \theta } \right){/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \Delta = - {x^3} - x + x{\sin ^2}\theta {/tex} {tex}+ \sin \theta \cos \theta - \sin \theta \cos \theta + x{\cos ^2}\theta{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \Delta = - {x^3} - x + x\left( {{{\sin }^2}\theta + {{\cos }^2}\theta } \right) {/tex} = -x3 - x + x = - x3 which is independent of {tex}\theta {/tex}

question_html: If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 1 & 2 & -2 \\ -1 & 3 & 0 \\ 0 & -2 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} and B-1 = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 3 & -1 & 1 \\ -15 & 6 & -5 \\ 5 & -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, find (AB)-1.
question_text: If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 1 & 2 & -2 \\ -1 & 3 & 0 \\ 0 & -2 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} and B -1 = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 3 & -1 & 1 \\ -15 & 6 & -5 \\ 5 & -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , find (AB) -1 .
solution_html:
A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 1 & 2 & -2 \\ -1 & 3 & 0 \\ 0 & -2 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, B-1 = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 3 & -1 & 1 \\ -15 & 6 & -5 \\ 5 & -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
(AB)-1 = B-1 A-1
|A| = 1(3) - 2(-1) - 2(2) = 3 + 2 - 4 = 1 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0
adj(A) = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 3 & 2 & 6 \\ 1 & 1 & 2 \\ 2 & 2 & 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
A11= (-1)1+1 M11 = (-1)2 3 = 3
A12= (-1)1+2 M12 = (-1)3 (-1) = 1
A13=  (-1)1+3M13 = (-1)4 2 = 2
A21=  (-1)2+1 M21 = (-1)3 (-2) = 2
A22=  (-1)2+2 M22 = (-1)4 1 = 1
A23=  (-1)2+3 M23 = (-1)3 2 = 2
A31=  (-1)3+1 M31 = (-1)4 6 = 6
A32=  (-1)3+2 M32 = (-1)5 (-2) = 2
A33=  (-1)3+3 M33 = (-1)6 5 = 5
A-1 = {tex}\frac{1}{1}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 3 & 2 & 6 \\ 1 & 1 & 2 \\ 2 & 2 & 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} (AB)-1=B-1 A-1
= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 3 & -1 & 1 \\ -15 & 6 & -5 \\ 5 & -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{lll} 3 & 2 & 6 \\ 1 & 1 & 2 \\ 2 & 2 & 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex}

={tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 9+(-1)+2 & 6+(-1)+2 & 18+(-2)+5 \\ -45+6+(-10) & -30+6+(-10) & -90+12+(-25) \\ 15+(-2)+4 & 10+(-2)+4 & 30+(-4)+10 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
={tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 11+(-1) & 8+(-1) & 23+(-2) \\ -55+6 & -40+6 & -115+12 \\ 19+(-2) & 14+(-2) & 40+(-4) \end{array}\right]{/tex}

= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 10 & 7 & 21 \\ -49 & -34 & -103 \\ 17 & 12 & 36 \end{array}\right]{/tex}

question_text: If A {tex}\cdot{/tex} (adj A) = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 3 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 3 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , then the value of |A| + |adj A| is equal to:

options.explanation: 12 Explaination: A {tex}\cdot{/tex} (adj A) = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 3 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 3 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} we know that A.(AdjA) = I.|A| {tex}\implies{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 3 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 3 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = |A| I {tex}\implies{/tex} 3 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = |A| I {tex}\implies{/tex} 3I = |A| I {tex}\implies{/tex} |A|=3 ---(1) |Adj A| = |A| 3-1 [ Since order n=3] |Adj A| = (3) 2 =9 |adj(A)| = 9 ------(2) Now, |A| + |adj A| = 3 + 9 =12

explanation: 12 Explaination: A {tex}\cdot{/tex} (adj A) = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 3 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 3 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} we know that A.(AdjA) = I.|A| {tex}\implies{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 3 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 3 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = |A| I {tex}\implies{/tex} 3 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = |A| I {tex}\implies{/tex} 3I = |A| I {tex}\implies{/tex} |A|=3 ---(1) |Adj A| = |A| 3-1 [ Since order n=3] |Adj A| = (3) 2 =9 |adj(A)| = 9 ------(2) Now, |A| + |adj A| = 3 + 9 =12

question_text: Let A be the area of a triangle having vertices (x 1 , y 1 ), (x 2 , y 2 ) and (x 3 , y 3 ). Which of the following is correct?

question_html:
If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & 2 \\ 5 & -7 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, then find A-1 and use it to solve the following system of equations:
3x + 5y = 11, 2x - 7y = -3.
question_text: If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & 2 \\ 5 & -7 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , then find A -1 and use it to solve the following system of equations: 3x + 5y = 11, 2x - 7y = -3.
explanation: A= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & 2 \\ 5 & -7 \end{array}\right]{/tex} |A| = (-21-10) = -31 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 Adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rr} -7 & -2 \\ -5 & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} A -1 = {tex}\frac{-1}{31}\left[\begin{array}{rr} -7 & -2 \\ -5 & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Given system of equations is {tex}\left\lfloor\begin{array}{rr} 3 & 5 \\ 2 & -7 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} \text x \\ \text y \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c} 11 \\ -3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} which is A'X = B, where X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} \text x \\ \text y \end{array}\right]{/tex} , B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c} 11 \\ -3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} X = (A') -1 B {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} X = (A -1 )'B = {tex}\frac{-1}{31}\left[\begin{array}{rr} -7 & -5 \\ -2 & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c} 11 \\ -3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 2 \\ 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 2, y = 1
solution: A= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & 2 \\ 5 & -7 \end{array}\right]{/tex} |A| = (-21-10) = -31 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 Adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rr} -7 & -2 \\ -5 & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} A -1 = {tex}\frac{-1}{31}\left[\begin{array}{rr} -7 & -2 \\ -5 & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Given system of equations is {tex}\left\lfloor\begin{array}{rr} 3 & 5 \\ 2 & -7 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} \text x \\ \text y \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c} 11 \\ -3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} which is A'X = B, where X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} \text x \\ \text y \end{array}\right]{/tex} , B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c} 11 \\ -3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} X = (A') -1 B {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} X = (A -1 )'B = {tex}\frac{-1}{31}\left[\begin{array}{rr} -7 & -5 \\ -2 & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c} 11 \\ -3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 2 \\ 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 2, y = 1
solution_html:
A={tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & 2 \\ 5 & -7 \end{array}\right]{/tex}

|A| = (-21-10) = -31{tex}\neq{/tex}0

Adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rr} -7 & -2 \\ -5 & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex}

A-1 = {tex}\frac{-1}{31}\left[\begin{array}{rr} -7 & -2 \\ -5 & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Given system of equations is
{tex}\left\lfloor\begin{array}{rr} 3 & 5 \\ 2 & -7 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} \text x \\ \text y \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c} 11 \\ -3 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
which is A'X = B, where X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} \text x \\ \text y \end{array}\right]{/tex}, B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c} 11 \\ -3 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} X = (A')-1B
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} X = (A-1)'B
= {tex}\frac{-1}{31}\left[\begin{array}{rr} -7 & -5 \\ -2 & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex}{tex}\left[\begin{array}{c} 11 \\ -3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 2 \\ 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 2, y = 1

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 0 & 1 & 1 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} \text x \\ \text y \\ \text z \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 6 \\ 3 \\ 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, then the value of (2x + y - z) is:
question_text: If {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 0 & 1 & 1 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} \text x \\ \text y \\ \text z \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 6 \\ 3 \\ 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , then the value of (2x + y - z) is:

options.explanation: 5 Explanation {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 0 & 1 & 1 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} \text x \\ \text y \\ \text z \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 6 \\ 3 \\ 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x+y+z \\ y+z\\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 6 \\ 3 \\ 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x+y+z =6 ------(i) y+z = 3 -----------(ii) z=2 --------------(iii) from (ii) and (iii) y + 2 = 3 y = 1 x+ y+ z = 6 x + 3 = 6 x=3 (2x + y - z) = 2(3)+1-2 = 6+1-2 = 7-2 =5

explanation: 5 Explanation {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 0 & 1 & 1 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} \text x \\ \text y \\ \text z \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 6 \\ 3 \\ 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x+y+z \\ y+z\\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 6 \\ 3 \\ 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x+y+z =6 ------(i) y+z = 3 -----------(ii) z=2 --------------(iii) from (ii) and (iii) y + 2 = 3 y = 1 x+ y+ z = 6 x + 3 = 6 x=3 (2x + y - z) = 2(3)+1-2 = 6+1-2 = 7-2 =5

question_html: Using determinants, find the area of {tex}\triangle {/tex}PQR with vertices P(3, 1), Q(9, 3) and R(5, 7). Also, find the equation of line PQ using determinants.
question_text: Using determinants, find the area of {tex}\triangle {/tex} PQR with vertices P(3, 1), Q(9, 3) and R(5, 7). Also, find the equation of line PQ using determinants.
explanation: Area = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left|\begin{array}{lll} 3 & 1 & 1 \\ 9 & 3 & 1 \\ 5 & 7 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = {tex}\frac 12{/tex} {3(-4) - 1(4) + 1(48)} = 16 sq. unit Equation of PQ is {tex}\left|\begin{array}{lll} \text x & \text y & 1 \\ 3 & 1 & 1 \\ 9 & 3 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 0 -2x + 6y = 0 or x - 3y = 0
solution: Area = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left|\begin{array}{lll} 3 & 1 & 1 \\ 9 & 3 & 1 \\ 5 & 7 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = {tex}\frac 12{/tex} {3(-4) - 1(4) + 1(48)} = 16 sq. unit Equation of PQ is {tex}\left|\begin{array}{lll} \text x & \text y & 1 \\ 3 & 1 & 1 \\ 9 & 3 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 0 -2x + 6y = 0 or x - 3y = 0
solution_html:
Area = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left|\begin{array}{lll} 3 & 1 & 1 \\ 9 & 3 & 1 \\ 5 & 7 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex}
= {tex}\frac 12{/tex}{3(-4) - 1(4) + 1(48)} = 16 sq. unit
Equation of PQ is {tex}\left|\begin{array}{lll} \text x & \text y & 1 \\ 3 & 1 & 1 \\ 9 & 3 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 0
-2x + 6y = 0 or x - 3y = 0

question_html:
If matrix A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 3 & 2 & 1 \\ 4 & 1 & 3 \\ 1 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, find A-1 and hence solve the following system of linear equations:
3x + 2y + z = 2000
4x + y + 3z = 2500
x + y + z = 900
question_text: If matrix A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 3 & 2 & 1 \\ 4 & 1 & 3 \\ 1 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , find A -1 and hence solve the following system of linear equations: 3x + 2y + z = 2000 4x + y + 3z = 2500 x + y + z = 900
explanation: |A| = 3(-2) - 4(1) + 1(5) = -5 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A -1 exists. A 11 = -2, A 12 = -1, A 13 = 3 A 21 = -1, A 22 = 2, A 23 = -1 A 31 = 5, A 32 = -5, A 33 = -5 adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -1 & 5 \\ -1 & 2 & -5 \\ 3 & -1 & -5 \end{array}\right]{/tex} A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{|A|}{/tex} adj A = {tex}-\frac{1}{5}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -1 & 5 \\ -1 & 2 & -5 \\ 3 & -1 & -5 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Given system of equations can be written as, where B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c} 2000 \\ 2500 \\ 900 \end{array}\right]{/tex} X = A -1 B = {tex}-\frac{1}{5}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -1 & 5 \\ -1 & 2 & -5 \\ 3 & -1 & -5 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 2000 \\ 2500 \\ 900 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{5}\left[\begin{array}{l} -2000 \\ -1500 \\ -1000 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 400 \\ 300 \\ 200 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 400, y = 300 and z = 200
solution: |A| = 3(-2) - 4(1) + 1(5) = -5 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A -1 exists. A 11 = -2, A 12 = -1, A 13 = 3 A 21 = -1, A 22 = 2, A 23 = -1 A 31 = 5, A 32 = -5, A 33 = -5 adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -1 & 5 \\ -1 & 2 & -5 \\ 3 & -1 & -5 \end{array}\right]{/tex} A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{|A|}{/tex} adj A = {tex}-\frac{1}{5}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -1 & 5 \\ -1 & 2 & -5 \\ 3 & -1 & -5 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Given system of equations can be written as, where B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c} 2000 \\ 2500 \\ 900 \end{array}\right]{/tex} X = A -1 B = {tex}-\frac{1}{5}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -1 & 5 \\ -1 & 2 & -5 \\ 3 & -1 & -5 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 2000 \\ 2500 \\ 900 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{5}\left[\begin{array}{l} -2000 \\ -1500 \\ -1000 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 400 \\ 300 \\ 200 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 400, y = 300 and z = 200
solution_html:
|A| = 3(-2) - 4(1) + 1(5) = -5 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A-1 exists.
A11 = -2, A12 = -1, A13 = 3
A21 = -1, A22 = 2, A23 = -1
A31 = 5, A32 = -5, A33 = -5
adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -1 & 5 \\ -1 & 2 & -5 \\ 3 & -1 & -5 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
A-1 = {tex}\frac{1}{|A|}{/tex} adj A = {tex}-\frac{1}{5}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -1 & 5 \\ -1 & 2 & -5 \\ 3 & -1 & -5 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Given system of equations can be written as, where B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c} 2000 \\ 2500 \\ 900 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
X = A-1B
= {tex}-\frac{1}{5}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -1 & 5 \\ -1 & 2 & -5 \\ 3 & -1 & -5 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 2000 \\ 2500 \\ 900 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{5}\left[\begin{array}{l} -2000 \\ -1500 \\ -1000 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 400 \\ 300 \\ 200 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 400, y = 300 and z = 200

question_html:
Let A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 6 & 7 & 8 \\ 6 & 7 & -8 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, find A-1 and hence solve the following system of linear equations:
x + y + z = 5000
6x + 7y + 8z = 35800
6x + 7y - 8z = 7000
question_text: Let A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 6 & 7 & 8 \\ 6 & 7 & -8 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , find A -1 and hence solve the following system of linear equations: x + y + z = 5000 6x + 7y + 8z = 35800 6x + 7y - 8z = 7000
explanation: |A| = (-56 - 56) - 6(-8 -7) + 6(8 - 7) = -16 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A -1 exists. A 11 = -112, A 12 = 96, A 13 = 0 A 21 = 15, A 22 = -14, A 23 = -1 A 31 = 1, A 32 = -2, A 33 = 1 adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -112 & 15 & 1 \\ 96 & -14 & -2 \\ 0 & -1 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{|A|}{/tex} adj A = {tex}-\frac{1}{16}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -112 & 15 & 1 \\ 96 & -14 & -2 \\ 0 & -1 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Given system of equations can be written as AX = B, where B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c} 5000 \\ 35800 \\ 7000\\ \end{array}\right]{/tex} X = A -1 B = {tex}-\frac{1}{16}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -112 & 15 & 1 \\ 96 & -14 & -2 \\ 0 & -1 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 5000 \\ 35800 \\ 7000 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{16}\left[\begin{array}{l} -16000 \\ -35200 \\ -28800 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 1000 \\ 2200 \\ 1800 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 1000, y = 2200, z = 1800
solution: |A| = (-56 - 56) - 6(-8 -7) + 6(8 - 7) = -16 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A -1 exists. A 11 = -112, A 12 = 96, A 13 = 0 A 21 = 15, A 22 = -14, A 23 = -1 A 31 = 1, A 32 = -2, A 33 = 1 adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -112 & 15 & 1 \\ 96 & -14 & -2 \\ 0 & -1 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{|A|}{/tex} adj A = {tex}-\frac{1}{16}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -112 & 15 & 1 \\ 96 & -14 & -2 \\ 0 & -1 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Given system of equations can be written as AX = B, where B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c} 5000 \\ 35800 \\ 7000\\ \end{array}\right]{/tex} X = A -1 B = {tex}-\frac{1}{16}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -112 & 15 & 1 \\ 96 & -14 & -2 \\ 0 & -1 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 5000 \\ 35800 \\ 7000 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}-\frac{1}{16}\left[\begin{array}{l} -16000 \\ -35200 \\ -28800 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 1000 \\ 2200 \\ 1800 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 1000, y = 2200, z = 1800
solution_html:
|A| = (-56 - 56) - 6(-8 -7) + 6(8 - 7) = -16 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A-1 exists.
A11 = -112, A12 = 96, A13 = 0
A21 = 15, A22 = -14, A23 = -1
A31 = 1, A32 = -2, A33 = 1
adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -112 & 15 & 1 \\ 96 & -14 & -2 \\ 0 & -1 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
A-1 = {tex}\frac{1}{|A|}{/tex} adj A = {tex}-\frac{1}{16}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -112 & 15 & 1 \\ 96 & -14 & -2 \\ 0 & -1 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Given system of equations can be written as AX = B, where B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c} 5000 \\ 35800 \\ 7000\\ \end{array}\right]{/tex}
X = A-1B
= {tex}-\frac{1}{16}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -112 & 15 & 1 \\ 96 & -14 & -2 \\ 0 & -1 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 5000 \\ 35800 \\ 7000 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}-\frac{1}{16}\left[\begin{array}{l} -16000 \\ -35200 \\ -28800 \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 1000 \\ 2200 \\ 1800 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 1000, y = 2200, z = 1800

question_html:
If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}-3 & -2 & -4 \\ 2 & 1 & 2 \\ 2 & 1 & 3\end{array}\right]{/tex}, B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}1 & 2 & 0 \\ -2 & -1 & -2 \\ 0 & -1 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex}, then find AB and use it to solve the following system of equations:
x - 2y = 3
2x - y - z = 2
-2y + z = 3
question_text: If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}-3 & -2 & -4 \\ 2 & 1 & 2 \\ 2 & 1 & 3\end{array}\right]{/tex} , B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}1 & 2 & 0 \\ -2 & -1 & -2 \\ 0 & -1 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} , then find AB and use it to solve the following system of equations: x - 2y = 3 2x - y - z = 2 -2y + z = 3
explanation: Given, If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}-3 & -2 & -4 \\ 2 & 1 & 2 \\ 2 & 1 & 3\end{array}\right]{/tex} , B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}1 & 2 & 0 \\ -2 & -1 & -2 \\ 0 & -1 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} , AB = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}-3 & -2 & -4 \\ 2 & 1 & 2 \\ 2 & 1 & 3\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}1 & 2 & 0 \\ -2 & -1 & -2 \\ 0 & -1 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}-3+4+0 & -6+2+4& 0+4-4 \\ 2-2+0 & 4-1+2 &0-2+ 2 \\ 2-2+0 & 4-1-3 & 0-2+3\end{array}\right]{/tex} AB = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll}1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\because{/tex} AB = I (AB) B -1 = (I)B -1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B -1 = A ......(i) The given system of equations can be written as: x - 2y = 3 2x - y - z = 2 -2y + z = 3 C= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}1 & 2 & 0 \\ -2 & -1 & -2 \\ 0 & -1 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} \text x \\ \text y \\ \text z \end{array}\right]{/tex} , D = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 3 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} CX = D and also,C=B T .......{ii} C -1 (CX)=C -1 D I X = C -1 D X = C -1 D X = {tex}\left(\text B^{-T}\right)^{\mathrm{1}} \cdot \text D{/tex} X= {tex}\left(\text B^{-1}\right)^{\mathrm{T}} \cdot \text D{/tex} X= A T {tex}\cdot{/tex} D X= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} -3 & -2 & -4 \\ 2 & 1 & 2 \\ 2 & 1 & 3 \end{array}\right]^T \left [\begin{array}{l} 3 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} -3 & 2 & 2 \\ -2 & 1 & 1 \\ -4 & 2 & 3 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 3 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} X= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} -9+4+6 \\ -6+2+3 \\ -12+4+9 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} \text x \\ \text y \\ \text z \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c} 1 \\ -1 \\ 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 1, y = -1, z = 1
solution: Given, If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}-3 & -2 & -4 \\ 2 & 1 & 2 \\ 2 & 1 & 3\end{array}\right]{/tex} , B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}1 & 2 & 0 \\ -2 & -1 & -2 \\ 0 & -1 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} , AB = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}-3 & -2 & -4 \\ 2 & 1 & 2 \\ 2 & 1 & 3\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}1 & 2 & 0 \\ -2 & -1 & -2 \\ 0 & -1 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}-3+4+0 & -6+2+4& 0+4-4 \\ 2-2+0 & 4-1+2 &0-2+ 2 \\ 2-2+0 & 4-1-3 & 0-2+3\end{array}\right]{/tex} AB = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll}1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\because{/tex} AB = I (AB) B -1 = (I)B -1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B -1 = A ......(i) The given system of equations can be written as: x - 2y = 3 2x - y - z = 2 -2y + z = 3 C= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}1 & 2 & 0 \\ -2 & -1 & -2 \\ 0 & -1 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} \text x \\ \text y \\ \text z \end{array}\right]{/tex} , D = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 3 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} CX = D and also,C=B T .......{ii} C -1 (CX)=C -1 D I X = C -1 D X = C -1 D X = {tex}\left(\text B^{-T}\right)^{\mathrm{1}} \cdot \text D{/tex} X= {tex}\left(\text B^{-1}\right)^{\mathrm{T}} \cdot \text D{/tex} X= A T {tex}\cdot{/tex} D X= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} -3 & -2 & -4 \\ 2 & 1 & 2 \\ 2 & 1 & 3 \end{array}\right]^T \left [\begin{array}{l} 3 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} -3 & 2 & 2 \\ -2 & 1 & 1 \\ -4 & 2 & 3 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 3 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} X= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} -9+4+6 \\ -6+2+3 \\ -12+4+9 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} \text x \\ \text y \\ \text z \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c} 1 \\ -1 \\ 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 1, y = -1, z = 1
solution_html:
Given, If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}-3 & -2 & -4 \\ 2 & 1 & 2 \\ 2 & 1 & 3\end{array}\right]{/tex}, B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}1 & 2 & 0 \\ -2 & -1 & -2 \\ 0 & -1 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex},
AB = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}-3 & -2 & -4 \\ 2 & 1 & 2 \\ 2 & 1 & 3\end{array}\right]{/tex}{tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}1 & 2 & 0 \\ -2 & -1 & -2 \\ 0 & -1 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}-3+4+0 & -6+2+4& 0+4-4 \\ 2-2+0 & 4-1+2 &0-2+ 2 \\ 2-2+0 & 4-1-3 & 0-2+3\end{array}\right]{/tex}
AB = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll}1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\because{/tex} AB = I
(AB) B-1 = (I)B-1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} B-1 = A ......(i)
The given system of equations can be written as:
x - 2y = 3
2x - y - z = 2
-2y + z = 3
C= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}1 & 2 & 0 \\ -2 & -1 & -2 \\ 0 & -1 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} \text x \\ \text y \\ \text z \end{array}\right]{/tex}, D = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 3 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
CX = D and also,C=BT .......{ii}
C-1(CX)=C-1D
I X = C-1D
X = C-1D
X = {tex}\left(\text B^{-T}\right)^{\mathrm{1}} \cdot \text D{/tex}
X={tex}\left(\text B^{-1}\right)^{\mathrm{T}} \cdot \text D{/tex}
X= AT{tex}\cdot{/tex}D

X={tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} -3 & -2 & -4 \\ 2 & 1 & 2 \\ 2 & 1 & 3 \end{array}\right]^T \left [\begin{array}{l} 3 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} -3 & 2 & 2 \\ -2 & 1 & 1 \\ -4 & 2 & 3 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 3 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} X= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} -9+4+6 \\ -6+2+3 \\ -12+4+9 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} \text x \\ \text y \\ \text z \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c} 1 \\ -1 \\ 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 1, y = -1, z = 1

question_html:
Find the inverse of the matrix A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 1 & -1 & 2 \\ 0 & 2 & -3 \\ 3 & -2 & 4 \end{array}\right]{/tex}. Using the inverse A-1, solve the system of linear equations
x - y + 2z = 1; 2y - 3z = 1; 3x - 2y + 4z = 3.
question_text: Find the inverse of the matrix A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 1 & -1 & 2 \\ 0 & 2 & -3 \\ 3 & -2 & 4 \end{array}\right]{/tex} . Using the inverse A -1 , solve the system of linear equations x - y + 2z = 1; 2y - 3z = 1; 3x - 2y + 4z = 3.
explanation: A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 1 & -1 & 2 \\ 0 & 2 & -3 \\ 3 & -2 & 4 \end{array}\right]{/tex} |A| = 1(8 - 6) + 1(0 + 9) + 2(0 - 6) = -1 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 {tex}\therefore{/tex} A is invertible. adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 2 & 0 & -1 \\ -9 & -2 & 3 \\ -6 & -1 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{|A|}{/tex} adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} -2 & 0 & 1 \\ 9 & 2 & -3 \\ 6 & 1 & -2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} The given system of equation can be written as AX = B, when A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & -1 & 2 \\ 0 & 2 & -3 \\ 3 & -2 & 4 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]{/tex} , B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 1 \\ 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} X = A -1 B {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} -2 & 0 & 1 \\ 9 & 2 & -3 \\ 6 & 1 & -2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 1 \\ 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 2 \\ 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 1, y = 2, z = 1
solution: A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 1 & -1 & 2 \\ 0 & 2 & -3 \\ 3 & -2 & 4 \end{array}\right]{/tex} |A| = 1(8 - 6) + 1(0 + 9) + 2(0 - 6) = -1 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 {tex}\therefore{/tex} A is invertible. adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 2 & 0 & -1 \\ -9 & -2 & 3 \\ -6 & -1 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{|A|}{/tex} adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} -2 & 0 & 1 \\ 9 & 2 & -3 \\ 6 & 1 & -2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} The given system of equation can be written as AX = B, when A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & -1 & 2 \\ 0 & 2 & -3 \\ 3 & -2 & 4 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]{/tex} , B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 1 \\ 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} X = A -1 B {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} -2 & 0 & 1 \\ 9 & 2 & -3 \\ 6 & 1 & -2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 1 \\ 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 2 \\ 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 1, y = 2, z = 1
solution_html:
A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 1 & -1 & 2 \\ 0 & 2 & -3 \\ 3 & -2 & 4 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
|A| = 1(8 - 6) + 1(0 + 9) + 2(0 - 6) = -1 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0
{tex}\therefore{/tex} A is invertible.
adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 2 & 0 & -1 \\ -9 & -2 & 3 \\ -6 & -1 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A-1 = {tex}\frac{1}{|A|}{/tex} adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} -2 & 0 & 1 \\ 9 & 2 & -3 \\ 6 & 1 & -2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
The given system of equation can be written as AX = B, when
A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & -1 & 2 \\ 0 & 2 & -3 \\ 3 & -2 & 4 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]{/tex}, B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 1 \\ 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} X = A-1 B
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} -2 & 0 & 1 \\ 9 & 2 & -3 \\ 6 & 1 & -2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 1 \\ 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 \\ 2 \\ 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 1, y = 2, z = 1

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
Assertion (A): If a line makes angles {tex}\alpha{/tex}, {tex}\beta{/tex}, {tex}\gamma{/tex} with positive direction of the coordinate axes, then sin2 {tex}\alpha{/tex} + sin2 {tex}\beta{/tex} + sin2 {tex}\gamma{/tex} = 2.
Reason (R): The sum of squares of the direction cosines of a line is 1.
question_text: Assertion (A): If a line makes angles {tex}\alpha{/tex} , {tex}\beta{/tex} , {tex}\gamma{/tex} with positive direction of the coordinate axes, then sin 2 {tex}\alpha{/tex} + sin 2 {tex}\beta{/tex} + sin 2 {tex}\gamma{/tex} = 2. Reason (R): The sum of squares of the direction cosines of a line is 1.

options.text: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
options.html: Both A and R are true and R is the correct explanation of A.
options.explanation: {tex}\because{/tex} cos 2 {tex}\alpha{/tex} + cos 2 {tex}\beta{/tex} + cos 2 {tex}\gamma{/tex} = 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (1 - sin 2 {tex}\alpha{/tex} ) + (1 - sin 2 {tex}\beta{/tex} ) + (1 - sin 2 {tex}\gamma{/tex} ) = 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3 - (sin 2 {tex}\alpha{/tex} + sin 2 {tex}\beta{/tex} + sin 2 {tex}\gamma{/tex} ) = 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} sin 2 {tex}\alpha{/tex} + sin 2 {tex}\beta{/tex} + sin 2 {tex}\gamma{/tex} = 2
