subject: Mathematics
question_html:
Read the following text carefully and answer the questions that follow:
Gautam buys 5 pens, 3 bags and 1 instrument box and pays a sum of ₹ 160. From the same shop, Vikram buys 2 pens, 1 bag and 3 instrument boxes and pays a sum of ₹ 190. Also Ankur buys 1 pen, 2 bags and 4 instrument boxes and pays a sum of ₹ 250.

  1. Convert the given above situation into a matrix equation of the form AX = B. (1)
  2. Find |A|. (1)
  3. Find A-1. (2)
    OR
    Determine P = A2 - 5A. (2)
question_text: Read the following text carefully and answer the questions that follow: Gautam buys 5 pens, 3 bags and 1 instrument box and pays a sum of ₹ 160. From the same shop, Vikram buys 2 pens, 1 bag and 3 instrument boxes and pays a sum of ₹ 190. Also Ankur buys 1 pen, 2 bags and 4 instrument boxes and pays a sum of ₹ 250. Convert the given above situation into a matrix equation of the form AX = B. (1) Find |A|. (1) Find A -1 . (2) OR Determine P = A 2 - 5A. (2)
explanation: Matrix equation is AX = B, where A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 5 & 3 & 1 \\ 2 & 1 & 3 \\ 1 & 2 & 4 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} \mathrm{x} \\ \mathrm{y} \\ \mathrm{z} \end{array}\right]{/tex} , B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 160 \\ 190 \\ 250 \end{array}\right]{/tex} where x is the number of pens bought, y the number of bags and z the number of instrument boxes. |A| = 5(4 - 6) - 3(8 - 3) + 1(4 - 1) = -22 adj (A) = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -5 & 3 \\ -10 & 19 & -7 \\ 8 & -13 & -1 \end{array}\right]^{\prime}{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -10 & 8 \\ -5 & 19 & -13 \\ 3 & -7 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{(-22)}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} -2 & -10 & 8 \\ -5 & 19 & -13 \\ 3 & -7 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} OR P = A 2 - 5A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 32 & 20 & 18 \\ 15 & 13 & 17 \\ 13 & 13 & 23 \end{array}\right]{/tex} - {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 25 & 15 & 5 \\ 10 & 5 & 15 \\ 5 & 10 & 20 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 7 & 5 & 13 \\ 5 & 8 & 2 \\ 8 & 3 & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
solution: Matrix equation is AX = B, where A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 5 & 3 & 1 \\ 2 & 1 & 3 \\ 1 & 2 & 4 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} \mathrm{x} \\ \mathrm{y} \\ \mathrm{z} \end{array}\right]{/tex} , B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 160 \\ 190 \\ 250 \end{array}\right]{/tex} where x is the number of pens bought, y the number of bags and z the number of instrument boxes. |A| = 5(4 - 6) - 3(8 - 3) + 1(4 - 1) = -22 adj (A) = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -5 & 3 \\ -10 & 19 & -7 \\ 8 & -13 & -1 \end{array}\right]^{\prime}{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -10 & 8 \\ -5 & 19 & -13 \\ 3 & -7 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{(-22)}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} -2 & -10 & 8 \\ -5 & 19 & -13 \\ 3 & -7 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} OR P = A 2 - 5A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 32 & 20 & 18 \\ 15 & 13 & 17 \\ 13 & 13 & 23 \end{array}\right]{/tex} - {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 25 & 15 & 5 \\ 10 & 5 & 15 \\ 5 & 10 & 20 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 7 & 5 & 13 \\ 5 & 8 & 2 \\ 8 & 3 & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
solution_html:
1. Matrix equation is AX = B, where
    A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 5 & 3 & 1 \\ 2 & 1 & 3 \\ 1 & 2 & 4 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} \mathrm{x} \\ \mathrm{y} \\ \mathrm{z} \end{array}\right]{/tex}, B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 160 \\ 190 \\ 250 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
    where x is the number of pens bought, y the number of bags and z the number of instrument boxes.

  2. |A| = 5(4 - 6) - 3(8 - 3) + 1(4 - 1) = -22

  3. adj (A) = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -5 & 3 \\ -10 & 19 & -7 \\ 8 & -13 & -1 \end{array}\right]^{\prime}{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -10 & 8 \\ -5 & 19 & -13 \\ 3 & -7 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
    {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A-1 = {tex}\frac{1}{(-22)}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} -2 & -10 & 8 \\ -5 & 19 & -13 \\ 3 & -7 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
    OR
    P = A2 - 5A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 32 & 20 & 18 \\ 15 & 13 & 17 \\ 13 & 13 & 23 \end{array}\right]{/tex} - {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 25 & 15 & 5 \\ 10 & 5 & 15 \\ 5 & 10 & 20 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
    = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 7 & 5 & 13 \\ 5 & 8 & 2 \\ 8 & 3 & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Three points P(2x, x + 3), Q(0, x) and R(x + 3, x + 6) are collinear, then x is equal to:
question_text: Three points P(2x, x + 3), Q(0, x) and R(x + 3, x + 6) are collinear, then x is equal to:

options.explanation: As points are collinear {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Area of triangle formed by 3 points is zero. {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\begin{equation} \frac{1}{2}\left|\begin{array}{ll} \left(x_1-x_2\right) & \left(x_2-x_3\right) \\ \left(y_1-y_2\right) & \left(y_2-y_3\right) \end{array}\right|=0 \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\begin{equation} \frac{1}{2}\left|\begin{array}{cc} (2 x-0) & \{0-(x+3)\} \\ (x+3-x) & \{x-(x+6)\} \end{array}\right|=0 \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\begin{equation} \left|\begin{array}{cc} 2 x & -(x+3) \\ 3 & -6 \end{array}\right|=0 \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -12x + 3(x + 3) = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -12x + 3x + 9 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -9x = -9 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 1

explanation: As points are collinear {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Area of triangle formed by 3 points is zero. {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\begin{equation} \frac{1}{2}\left|\begin{array}{ll} \left(x_1-x_2\right) & \left(x_2-x_3\right) \\ \left(y_1-y_2\right) & \left(y_2-y_3\right) \end{array}\right|=0 \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\begin{equation} \frac{1}{2}\left|\begin{array}{cc} (2 x-0) & \{0-(x+3)\} \\ (x+3-x) & \{x-(x+6)\} \end{array}\right|=0 \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\begin{equation} \left|\begin{array}{cc} 2 x & -(x+3) \\ 3 & -6 \end{array}\right|=0 \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -12x + 3(x + 3) = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -12x + 3x + 9 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -9x = -9 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 1

question_text: The system of linear equations 5x + ky = 5, 3x + 3y = 5; will be consistent if:

options.explanation: We have, 5x + ky - 5 = 0 and 3x + 3y - 5 = 0 For consistent system {tex}\begin{equation} \frac{5}{3} \neq \frac{k}{3} \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} k {tex}\neq{/tex} 5

question_html: If {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}, find A-1. Using A-1 solve the system of equations 2x - 3y + 5z = 11; 3x + 2y - 4z = -5; x + y - 2z = -3
question_text: If {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} , find A -1 . Using A -1 solve the system of equations 2x - 3y + 5z = 11; 3x + 2y - 4z = -5; x + y - 2z = -3
explanation: Given: Matrix {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} |A| = {tex}\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right|{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |A| = 2(-4 + 4) - (-3)(-6 + 4) + 5(3 - 2) = 0 - 6 + 5 = -1 {tex} \ne{/tex} 0 {tex}\therefore{/tex} A -1 exists and A -1 = {tex} \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}{/tex} (adj. A)...(i) Now, A 11 = 0, A 12 = 2, A 13 = 1 and A 21 = -1, A 22 = -9, A 23 = -5 and A 31 = 2, A 32 = 23, A 33 = 13 {tex}\therefore{/tex} adj. A = {tex} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&2&1 \\ { - 1}&{ - 9}&{ - 5} \\ 2&{23}&{13} \end{array}} \right]' = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 1}&2 \\ 2&{ - 9}&{23} \\ 1&{ - 5}&{13} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} From eq. (i), A -1 = {tex} \frac{1}{{ - 1}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 1}&2 \\ 2&{ - 9}&{23} \\ 1&{ - 5}&{13} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&1&{ - 2} \\ { - 2}&9&{ - 23} \\ { - 1}&5&{ - 13} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Now, Matrix form of given equations is AX = B {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Here A = {tex} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} , X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]{/tex} and B = {tex} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Therefore, solution is unique and X = A -1 B {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&1&{ - 2} \\ { - 2}&9&{ - 23} \\ { - 1}&5&{ - 13} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {0 - 5 + 6} \\ { - 22 - 45 + 69} \\ { - 11 - 25 + 39} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Therefore, x = 1, y = 2 and z = 3
solution: Given: Matrix {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} |A| = {tex}\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right|{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |A| = 2(-4 + 4) - (-3)(-6 + 4) + 5(3 - 2) = 0 - 6 + 5 = -1 {tex} \ne{/tex} 0 {tex}\therefore{/tex} A -1 exists and A -1 = {tex} \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}{/tex} (adj. A)...(i) Now, A 11 = 0, A 12 = 2, A 13 = 1 and A 21 = -1, A 22 = -9, A 23 = -5 and A 31 = 2, A 32 = 23, A 33 = 13 {tex}\therefore{/tex} adj. A = {tex} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&2&1 \\ { - 1}&{ - 9}&{ - 5} \\ 2&{23}&{13} \end{array}} \right]' = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 1}&2 \\ 2&{ - 9}&{23} \\ 1&{ - 5}&{13} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} From eq. (i), A -1 = {tex} \frac{1}{{ - 1}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 1}&2 \\ 2&{ - 9}&{23} \\ 1&{ - 5}&{13} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&1&{ - 2} \\ { - 2}&9&{ - 23} \\ { - 1}&5&{ - 13} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Now, Matrix form of given equations is AX = B {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Here A = {tex} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} , X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]{/tex} and B = {tex} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Therefore, solution is unique and X = A -1 B {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&1&{ - 2} \\ { - 2}&9&{ - 23} \\ { - 1}&5&{ - 13} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {0 - 5 + 6} \\ { - 22 - 45 + 69} \\ { - 11 - 25 + 39} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Therefore, x = 1, y = 2 and z = 3
solution_html:
Given: Matrix {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore{/tex} |A| = {tex}\left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right|{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |A| = 2(-4 + 4) - (-3)(-6 + 4) + 5(3 - 2) = 0 - 6 + 5 = -1 {tex} \ne{/tex} 0
{tex}\therefore{/tex} A-1 exists and A-1 = {tex} \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}{/tex} (adj. A)...(i)
Now, A11 = 0, A12 = 2, A13 = 1 and A21 = -1, A22 = -9, A23 = -5 and A31 = 2, A32 = 23, A33 = 13
{tex}\therefore{/tex} adj. A ={tex} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&2&1 \\ { - 1}&{ - 9}&{ - 5} \\ 2&{23}&{13} \end{array}} \right]' = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 1}&2 \\ 2&{ - 9}&{23} \\ 1&{ - 5}&{13} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
From eq. (i),
A-1 = {tex} \frac{1}{{ - 1}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 1}&2 \\ 2&{ - 9}&{23} \\ 1&{ - 5}&{13} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&1&{ - 2} \\ { - 2}&9&{ - 23} \\ { - 1}&5&{ - 13} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
Now, Matrix form of given equations is AX = B
{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
Here A ={tex} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}, X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]{/tex} and B ={tex} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
Therefore, solution is unique and X = A-1B
{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&1&{ - 2} \\ { - 2}&9&{ - 23} \\ { - 1}&5&{ - 13} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} 
{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {0 - 5 + 6} \\ { - 22 - 45 + 69} \\ { - 11 - 25 + 39} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} 
{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} 
Therefore, x = 1, y = 2 and z = 3

=2[-4+4] + 3[-6+4] + 5[3-2] = 0 + 3(-2) + 5(1) = -6 + 5 = -1 {tex} \neq0{/tex}

{tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} \begin{gathered} x \hfill \\ y \hfill \\ \end{gathered} \\ z \end{array}} \right] = {\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&5&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]^{ - 1}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}|A|=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}1 & 2 & -3 \\ 3 & 2 & -2 \\ 2^3 & -1 & 1\end{array}\right|{/tex}
{tex}=1\left|\begin{array}{cc}2 & -2 \\ -1 & 1\end{array}\right|-2\left|\begin{array}{cc}3 & -2 \\ 2 & 1\end{array}\right|-3\left|\begin{array}{cc}3 & 2 \\ 2 & -1\end{array}\right|{/tex}
{tex}=1(2-2)-2(3+4)-3(-3-4){/tex}
{tex}=0-14+21=7 \neq 0{/tex}.
{tex}A^{-1}{/tex} Exist
{tex}A_{11}=\left|\begin{array}{cc}2 & -2 \\ -1 & 1\end{array}\right|=0{/tex}
{tex}A_{12}=-\left|\begin{array}{cc}3 & -2 \\ 2 & 1\end{array}\right|=-(3+4)=-7{/tex}
{tex}A_{13}=\left|\begin{array}{cc}3 & 2 \\ 2 & -1\end{array}\right|=-3-4=-7{/tex}.
{tex}A_{21}=-\left|\begin{array}{cc}2 & -3 \\ -1 & 1\end{array}\right|=-(2-3) =1{/tex}
{tex}A_{22}=\left|\begin{array}{cc}1 & -3 \\ 2 & 1\end{array}\right|=1+6=7{/tex}
{tex}A_{23}=-\left|\begin{array}{cc}1 & 2 \\ 2 & -1\end{array}\right|=-(-1-4)=5{/tex}
{tex} A_{31}=\left|\begin{array}{ll}2 & -3 \\ 2 & -2\end{array}\right|=-4+6=2 {/tex}
{tex} A_{32}=-\left|\begin{array}{ll}1 & -3 \\ 3 & -2\end{array}\right|=-(-2+9)=-7{/tex}
{tex}A_{33}=\left|\begin{array}{ll}1 & 2 \\ 3 & 2\end{array}\right|=-4{/tex}
{tex}\operatorname{Adj} A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}0 & -7 & -7 \\ 1 & 7 & 5 \\ 2 & -7 & -4\end{array}\right]'{/tex}
{tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}0 & 1 & 2 \\ -7 & 7 & -7 \\ -7 & 5 & -4\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore A^{-1} =\frac{1}{A} \text { adj } A . {/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{7}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}0 & 1 & 2 \\ -7 & 7 & -7 \\ -7 & 5 & -4\end{array}\right]{/tex}
Now the systeem of eq. cans be reduced in the matrix from:
{tex}A x=B{/tex} as.
{tex} x_0=\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right], B=\left[\begin{array}{l}6 \\ 3 \\ 2\end{array}\right] {/tex}
{tex} A x=B \Rightarrow x=A^{-1} B .{/tex}
{tex} {\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right] } =\frac{1}{7}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}0 & 1 & 2 \\ -7 & 7 & -7 \\ -7 & 5 & -4\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l}6 \\ 3 \\ 2\end{array}\right] {/tex}
{tex} =\frac{1}{7}\left[\begin{array}{c}7 \\ -35 \\ -35\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c}1 \\ -5 \\ -5\end{array}\right]{/tex}
x = 1
y = -5
z = -5

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
Using properties of determinants, show that
{tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc} 3 a & -a+b & -a+c \\ -b+a & 3 b & -b+c \\ -c+a & -c+b & 3 c \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 3(a + b + c)(ab + bc + ca)
question_text: Using properties of determinants, show that {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc} 3 a & -a+b & -a+c \\ -b+a & 3 b & -b+c \\ -c+a & -c+b & 3 c \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 3(a + b + c)(ab + bc + ca)
explanation: L.H.S = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc} {3 a} & {-a+b} & {-a+c} \\ {-b+a} & {3 b} & {-b+c} \\ {-c+a} & {-c+b} & {3 c} \end{array}\right|{/tex} On applying operation C 1 {tex}\to{/tex} C 1 + C 2 + C 3 We have L.H.S = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc}{3 a-a+b-a+c} & {-a+b} & {-a+c} \\ {-b+a+3 b-b+c} & {3 b} & {-b+c} \\ {-c+a-c+b+3 c} & {-c+b} & {3 c}\end{array}\right|{/tex} = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc}{a+b+c} & {-a+b} & {-a+c} \\ {a+b+c} & {3 b} & {-b+c} \\ {a+b+c} & {-c+b} & {3 c}\end{array}\right|{/tex} = {tex}(a+b+c)\left|\begin{array}{ccc}{1} & {-a+b} & {-a+c} \\ {1} & {3 b} & {-b+c} \\ {1} & {-c+b} & {3 c}\end{array}\right|{/tex} {tex}\text { Applying } \mathrm{R}_{2} \rightarrow \mathrm{R}_{2}-\mathrm{R}_{1} \text { and } \mathrm{R}_{3} \rightarrow \mathrm{R}_{3}-\mathrm{R}_{1}, \text { we have: }{/tex} L.H.S = {tex}(a+b+c)\left|\begin{array}{ccc} {1} & {-a+b} & {-a+c} \\ {0} & {2 b+a} & {a-b} \\ {0} & {a-c} & {2 c+a} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = {tex}(a+b+c)\left|\begin{array}{ccc}{0} & {-a+c} & {-a-2 c} \\ {0} & {2 b+a} & {-b+a} \\ {1} & {-c+b} & {3 c}\end{array}\right|{/tex} Expanding along C 1 , we have {tex}\begin{aligned} \text L.\text H.\text S &=(a+b+c)[(2 b+a)(2 c+a)-(a-b)(a-c)] \\ &=(a+b+c)\left[4 b c+2 a b+2 a c+a^{2}-a^{2}+a c+b a-b c\right] \\ &=(a+b+c)(3 a b+3 b c+3 a c) \\ &=3(a+b+c)(a b+b c+c a) \end{aligned}{/tex} Hence proved.
solution: L.H.S = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc} {3 a} & {-a+b} & {-a+c} \\ {-b+a} & {3 b} & {-b+c} \\ {-c+a} & {-c+b} & {3 c} \end{array}\right|{/tex} On applying operation C 1 {tex}\to{/tex} C 1 + C 2 + C 3 We have L.H.S = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc}{3 a-a+b-a+c} & {-a+b} & {-a+c} \\ {-b+a+3 b-b+c} & {3 b} & {-b+c} \\ {-c+a-c+b+3 c} & {-c+b} & {3 c}\end{array}\right|{/tex} = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc}{a+b+c} & {-a+b} & {-a+c} \\ {a+b+c} & {3 b} & {-b+c} \\ {a+b+c} & {-c+b} & {3 c}\end{array}\right|{/tex} = {tex}(a+b+c)\left|\begin{array}{ccc}{1} & {-a+b} & {-a+c} \\ {1} & {3 b} & {-b+c} \\ {1} & {-c+b} & {3 c}\end{array}\right|{/tex} {tex}\text { Applying } \mathrm{R}_{2} \rightarrow \mathrm{R}_{2}-\mathrm{R}_{1} \text { and } \mathrm{R}_{3} \rightarrow \mathrm{R}_{3}-\mathrm{R}_{1}, \text { we have: }{/tex} L.H.S = {tex}(a+b+c)\left|\begin{array}{ccc} {1} & {-a+b} & {-a+c} \\ {0} & {2 b+a} & {a-b} \\ {0} & {a-c} & {2 c+a} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = {tex}(a+b+c)\left|\begin{array}{ccc}{0} & {-a+c} & {-a-2 c} \\ {0} & {2 b+a} & {-b+a} \\ {1} & {-c+b} & {3 c}\end{array}\right|{/tex} Expanding along C 1 , we have {tex}\begin{aligned} \text L.\text H.\text S &=(a+b+c)[(2 b+a)(2 c+a)-(a-b)(a-c)] \\ &=(a+b+c)\left[4 b c+2 a b+2 a c+a^{2}-a^{2}+a c+b a-b c\right] \\ &=(a+b+c)(3 a b+3 b c+3 a c) \\ &=3(a+b+c)(a b+b c+c a) \end{aligned}{/tex} Hence proved.
solution_html:
L.H.S = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc} {3 a} & {-a+b} & {-a+c} \\ {-b+a} & {3 b} & {-b+c} \\ {-c+a} & {-c+b} & {3 c} \end{array}\right|{/tex}
On applying operation C1 {tex}\to{/tex} C1 + C2 + C3
We have
L.H.S = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc}{3 a-a+b-a+c} & {-a+b} & {-a+c} \\ {-b+a+3 b-b+c} & {3 b} & {-b+c} \\ {-c+a-c+b+3 c} & {-c+b} & {3 c}\end{array}\right|{/tex}
= {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ccc}{a+b+c} & {-a+b} & {-a+c} \\ {a+b+c} & {3 b} & {-b+c} \\ {a+b+c} & {-c+b} & {3 c}\end{array}\right|{/tex}
= {tex}(a+b+c)\left|\begin{array}{ccc}{1} & {-a+b} & {-a+c} \\ {1} & {3 b} & {-b+c} \\ {1} & {-c+b} & {3 c}\end{array}\right|{/tex}
{tex}\text { Applying } \mathrm{R}_{2} \rightarrow \mathrm{R}_{2}-\mathrm{R}_{1} \text { and } \mathrm{R}_{3} \rightarrow \mathrm{R}_{3}-\mathrm{R}_{1}, \text { we have: }{/tex}
L.H.S = {tex}(a+b+c)\left|\begin{array}{ccc} {1} & {-a+b} & {-a+c} \\ {0} & {2 b+a} & {a-b} \\ {0} & {a-c} & {2 c+a} \end{array}\right|{/tex}
= {tex}(a+b+c)\left|\begin{array}{ccc}{0} & {-a+c} & {-a-2 c} \\ {0} & {2 b+a} & {-b+a} \\ {1} & {-c+b} & {3 c}\end{array}\right|{/tex}
Expanding along C1, we have
{tex}\begin{aligned} \text L.\text H.\text S &=(a+b+c)[(2 b+a)(2 c+a)-(a-b)(a-c)] \\ &=(a+b+c)\left[4 b c+2 a b+2 a c+a^{2}-a^{2}+a c+b a-b c\right] \\ &=(a+b+c)(3 a b+3 b c+3 a c) \\ &=3(a+b+c)(a b+b c+c a) \end{aligned}{/tex}
Hence proved.

question_html:
If A = {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} find A-1, using A-1 solve the system of equations
2x - 3y + 5z = 11
3x + 2y - 4z = -5
x + y - 2z = -3
question_text: If A = {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} find A -1 , using A -1 solve the system of equations 2x - 3y + 5z = 11 3x + 2y - 4z = -5 x + y - 2z = -3
explanation: A = {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} |A| = 2(-4 + 4) + 3(-6 +4)+ 5(3 - 2) = 0 -6 +5 = -1 Now, A 11 = 0, A 12 = 2, A 13 = 1 A 21 = -1, A 22 = -9, A 23 = -5 A 31 = 2, A 32 = 23,A 33 = 13 {tex}\therefore {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{|A|}}(adjA) = - \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 1}&2 \\ 2&{ - 9}&{23} \\ 1&{ - 5}&{13} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&1&{ - 2} \\ { - 2}&9&{ - 23} \\ { - 1}&5&{ - 13} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} ....(1) Now, the given system of equations can be written in the form of AX = B, where {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]\;,X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]\;and\;B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} The solution of the system of equations is given by X = A -1 B {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&1&{ - 2} \\ { - 2}&9&{ - 23} \\ { - 1}&5&{ - 13} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} [Using (1) ] {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {0 - 5 + 6} \\ { - 22 - 45 + 69} \\ { - 11 - 25 + 39} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Hence, x = 1, y = 2 and z = 3.​​​​​​
solution: A = {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} |A| = 2(-4 + 4) + 3(-6 +4)+ 5(3 - 2) = 0 -6 +5 = -1 Now, A 11 = 0, A 12 = 2, A 13 = 1 A 21 = -1, A 22 = -9, A 23 = -5 A 31 = 2, A 32 = 23,A 33 = 13 {tex}\therefore {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{|A|}}(adjA) = - \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 1}&2 \\ 2&{ - 9}&{23} \\ 1&{ - 5}&{13} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&1&{ - 2} \\ { - 2}&9&{ - 23} \\ { - 1}&5&{ - 13} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} ....(1) Now, the given system of equations can be written in the form of AX = B, where {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]\;,X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]\;and\;B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} The solution of the system of equations is given by X = A -1 B {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&1&{ - 2} \\ { - 2}&9&{ - 23} \\ { - 1}&5&{ - 13} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} [Using (1) ] {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {0 - 5 + 6} \\ { - 22 - 45 + 69} \\ { - 11 - 25 + 39} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Hence, x = 1, y = 2 and z = 3.​​​​​​
solution_html:
A = {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} 

{tex}\therefore{/tex} |A| = 2(-4 + 4) + 3(-6 +4)+ 5(3 - 2) = 0 -6 +5 = -1
Now, A11 = 0, A12= 2, A13= 1
A21= -1, A22 = -9, A23= -5
A31= 2, A32= 23,A33 = 13

 {tex}\therefore {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{|A|}}(adjA) = - \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&{ - 1}&2 \\ 2&{ - 9}&{23} \\ 1&{ - 5}&{13} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&1&{ - 2} \\ { - 2}&9&{ - 23} \\ { - 1}&5&{ - 13} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}....(1)

Now, the given system of equations can be written in the form of AX = B, where

{tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&{ - 3}&5 \\ 3&2&{ - 4} \\ 1&1&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]\;,X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]\;and\;B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

The solution of the system of equations is given by X = A-1B

{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&1&{ - 2} \\ { - 2}&9&{ - 23} \\ { - 1}&5&{ - 13} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11} \\ { - 5} \\ { - 3} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} [Using (1) ]

{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {0 - 5 + 6} \\ { - 22 - 45 + 69} \\ { - 11 - 25 + 39} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Hence, x = 1, y = 2 and z = 3.​​​​​​

question_html: If A is a square matrix of order 3 with |A| = 4, then write the value of |-2A|.
question_text: If A is a square matrix of order 3 with |A| = 4, then write the value of |-2A|.
explanation: Given, Order of the square matrix is, n = 3 |A| = 4 |-2A| = (-2) n |A| ...[For a square matrix of order n and constant k], {tex}|k A|=k^{n}|A|{/tex} ] |-2A| = (-2) 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 |-2A| = -32 Therefore, the value of |-2A| is -32.
solution: Given, Order of the square matrix is, n = 3 |A| = 4 |-2A| = (-2) n |A| ...[For a square matrix of order n and constant k], {tex}|k A|=k^{n}|A|{/tex} ] |-2A| = (-2) 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 |-2A| = -32 Therefore, the value of |-2A| is -32.
solution_html:
Given, Order of the square matrix is, n = 3
|A| = 4
|-2A| = (-2)n|A|   ...[For a square matrix of order n and constant k], {tex}|k A|=k^{n}|A|{/tex}]
|-2A| = (-2)3 {tex}\times{/tex} 4
|-2A| = -32
Therefore, the value of |-2A| is -32.

question_html: If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{lll} {1} & {3} & {4} \\ {2} & {1} & {2} \\ {5} & {1} & {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex}, Find A-1. Hence solve the system of equations x + 3y + 4z = 8, 2x + y + 2x = 5 and 5x + y + z = 7
question_text: If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{lll} {1} & {3} & {4} \\ {2} & {1} & {2} \\ {5} & {1} & {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex} , Find A -1 . Hence solve the system of equations x + 3y + 4z = 8, 2x + y + 2x = 5 and 5x + y + z = 7
explanation: If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{lll}{1} & {3} & {4} \\ {2} & {1} & {2} \\ {5} & {1} & {1}\end{array}\right]{/tex} then |A| = 1(1 - 2) - 3(2 - 10) + 4(2 - 5) = 11 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 Then cofactors of A are A 11 = -1, A 12 = 8, A 13 = -3, A 21 = 1, A 22 = -19, A 23 = 14, A 31 = 2, A 32 = 6, and A 33 = -5 Then, adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll}{\mathrm{A}_{11}} & {\mathrm{A}_{12}} & {\mathrm{A}_{13}} \\ {\mathrm{A}_{21}} & {\mathrm{A}_{22}} & {\mathrm{A}_{23}} \\ {\mathrm{A}_{31}} & {\mathrm{A}_{32}} & {\mathrm{A}^{33}}\end{array}\right]^{\mathrm{T}}{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{-1} & {8} & {-3} \\ {1} & {-19} & {14} \\ {2} & {6} & {-5}\end{array}\right]^{T}{/tex} Adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{-1} & {1} & {2} \\ {8} & {-19} & {6} \\ {-3} & {14} & {-5}\end{array}\right]{/tex} Now, A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{|\mathrm{A}|}{/tex} adjA = {tex}\frac{1}{11}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{-1} & {1} & {2} \\ {8} & {-19} & {6} \\ {-3} & {14} & {-5}\end{array}\right]{/tex} ...(ii) Given system of equations are x + 3y + 4z = 8 2x + y + 2z = 5 5x + y + z = 7 Let, A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll}{1} & {3} & {4} \\ {2} & {1} & {2} \\ {5} & {1} & {1}\end{array}\right]{/tex} , B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}{8} \\ {5} \\ {7}\end{array}\right]{/tex} , and X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}{x} \\ {y} \\ {z}\end{array}\right]{/tex} Then AX = B {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll}{1} & {3} & {4} \\ {2} & {1} & {2} \\ {5} & {1} & {1}\end{array}\right] X=\left[\begin{array}{l}{8} \\ {5} \\ {7}\end{array}\right]{/tex} X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll}{1} & {3} & {4} \\ {2} & {1} & {2} \\ {5} & {1} & {1}\end{array}\right]^{-1}\left[\begin{array}{l}{8} \\ {5} \\ {7}\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{11}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{-1} & {1} & {2} \\ {8} & {-19} & {6} \\ {-3} & {14} & {-5}\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l}{8} \\ {5} \\ {7}\end{array}\right]{/tex} [using (ii) equation] = {tex}\frac{1}{11}\left[\begin{array}{r}{-8+5+14} \\ {64-95+42} \\ {-24+70-35}\end{array}\right]=\frac{1}{11}\left[\begin{array}{c}{11} \\ {11} \\ {11}\end{array}\right]{/tex} X = {tex}\frac{1}{11}\left[\begin{array}{l}{11} \\ {11} \\ {11}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}{x} \\ {y} \\ {z}\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l}{1} \\ {1} \\ {1}\end{array}\right]{/tex} Hence, x = 1, y = 1, z = 1
solution: If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{lll}{1} & {3} & {4} \\ {2} & {1} & {2} \\ {5} & {1} & {1}\end{array}\right]{/tex} then |A| = 1(1 - 2) - 3(2 - 10) + 4(2 - 5) = 11 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 Then cofactors of A are A 11 = -1, A 12 = 8, A 13 = -3, A 21 = 1, A 22 = -19, A 23 = 14, A 31 = 2, A 32 = 6, and A 33 = -5 Then, adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll}{\mathrm{A}_{11}} & {\mathrm{A}_{12}} & {\mathrm{A}_{13}} \\ {\mathrm{A}_{21}} & {\mathrm{A}_{22}} & {\mathrm{A}_{23}} \\ {\mathrm{A}_{31}} & {\mathrm{A}_{32}} & {\mathrm{A}^{33}}\end{array}\right]^{\mathrm{T}}{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{-1} & {8} & {-3} \\ {1} & {-19} & {14} \\ {2} & {6} & {-5}\end{array}\right]^{T}{/tex} Adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{-1} & {1} & {2} \\ {8} & {-19} & {6} \\ {-3} & {14} & {-5}\end{array}\right]{/tex} Now, A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{|\mathrm{A}|}{/tex} adjA = {tex}\frac{1}{11}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{-1} & {1} & {2} \\ {8} & {-19} & {6} \\ {-3} & {14} & {-5}\end{array}\right]{/tex} ...(ii) Given system of equations are x + 3y + 4z = 8 2x + y + 2z = 5 5x + y + z = 7 Let, A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll}{1} & {3} & {4} \\ {2} & {1} & {2} \\ {5} & {1} & {1}\end{array}\right]{/tex} , B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}{8} \\ {5} \\ {7}\end{array}\right]{/tex} , and X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}{x} \\ {y} \\ {z}\end{array}\right]{/tex} Then AX = B {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll}{1} & {3} & {4} \\ {2} & {1} & {2} \\ {5} & {1} & {1}\end{array}\right] X=\left[\begin{array}{l}{8} \\ {5} \\ {7}\end{array}\right]{/tex} X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll}{1} & {3} & {4} \\ {2} & {1} & {2} \\ {5} & {1} & {1}\end{array}\right]^{-1}\left[\begin{array}{l}{8} \\ {5} \\ {7}\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{11}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{-1} & {1} & {2} \\ {8} & {-19} & {6} \\ {-3} & {14} & {-5}\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l}{8} \\ {5} \\ {7}\end{array}\right]{/tex} [using (ii) equation] = {tex}\frac{1}{11}\left[\begin{array}{r}{-8+5+14} \\ {64-95+42} \\ {-24+70-35}\end{array}\right]=\frac{1}{11}\left[\begin{array}{c}{11} \\ {11} \\ {11}\end{array}\right]{/tex} X = {tex}\frac{1}{11}\left[\begin{array}{l}{11} \\ {11} \\ {11}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}{x} \\ {y} \\ {z}\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l}{1} \\ {1} \\ {1}\end{array}\right]{/tex} Hence, x = 1, y = 1, z = 1
solution_html:
If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{lll}{1} & {3} & {4} \\ {2} & {1} & {2} \\ {5} & {1} & {1}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
then |A| = 1(1 - 2) - 3(2 - 10) + 4(2 - 5)
= 11 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0
Then cofactors of A are
A11 = -1, A12 = 8, A13 = -3,
A21 = 1, A22 = -19, A23 = 14, A31 = 2, A32 = 6, and A33 = -5
Then, adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll}{\mathrm{A}_{11}} & {\mathrm{A}_{12}} & {\mathrm{A}_{13}} \\ {\mathrm{A}_{21}} & {\mathrm{A}_{22}} & {\mathrm{A}_{23}} \\ {\mathrm{A}_{31}} & {\mathrm{A}_{32}} & {\mathrm{A}^{33}}\end{array}\right]^{\mathrm{T}}{/tex}
= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{-1} & {8} & {-3} \\ {1} & {-19} & {14} \\ {2} & {6} & {-5}\end{array}\right]^{T}{/tex}
Adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{-1} & {1} & {2} \\ {8} & {-19} & {6} \\ {-3} & {14} & {-5}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
Now, A-1 = {tex}\frac{1}{|\mathrm{A}|}{/tex}adjA
= {tex}\frac{1}{11}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{-1} & {1} & {2} \\ {8} & {-19} & {6} \\ {-3} & {14} & {-5}\end{array}\right]{/tex} ...(ii)
Given system of equations are
x + 3y + 4z = 8
2x + y + 2z = 5
5x + y + z = 7
Let, A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll}{1} & {3} & {4} \\ {2} & {1} & {2} \\ {5} & {1} & {1}\end{array}\right]{/tex}, B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}{8} \\ {5} \\ {7}\end{array}\right]{/tex}, and X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}{x} \\ {y} \\ {z}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
Then AX = B
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll}{1} & {3} & {4} \\ {2} & {1} & {2} \\ {5} & {1} & {1}\end{array}\right] X=\left[\begin{array}{l}{8} \\ {5} \\ {7}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll}{1} & {3} & {4} \\ {2} & {1} & {2} \\ {5} & {1} & {1}\end{array}\right]^{-1}\left[\begin{array}{l}{8} \\ {5} \\ {7}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\frac{1}{11}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}{-1} & {1} & {2} \\ {8} & {-19} & {6} \\ {-3} & {14} & {-5}\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l}{8} \\ {5} \\ {7}\end{array}\right]{/tex} [using (ii) equation]
= {tex}\frac{1}{11}\left[\begin{array}{r}{-8+5+14} \\ {64-95+42} \\ {-24+70-35}\end{array}\right]=\frac{1}{11}\left[\begin{array}{c}{11} \\ {11} \\ {11}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
X = {tex}\frac{1}{11}\left[\begin{array}{l}{11} \\ {11} \\ {11}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}{x} \\ {y} \\ {z}\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l}{1} \\ {1} \\ {1}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
Hence, x = 1, y = 1, z = 1

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {1} & {1} & {1} \\ {0} & {1} & {3} \\ {1} & {-2} & {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex}, find A-1.
Hence, solve the following system of equations:
x + y + z = 6,
y + 3z = 11
and x - 2y + z = 0
question_text: If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {1} & {1} & {1} \\ {0} & {1} & {3} \\ {1} & {-2} & {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex} , find A -1 . Hence, solve the following system of equations: x + y + z = 6, y + 3z = 11 and x - 2y + z = 0
explanation: The given matrix is, {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {1} & {1} & {1} \\ {0} & {1} & {3} \\ {1} & {-2} & {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex} Clearly, the Co-factors are A 11 = 7, A 12 = 3, A 13 = -1 A 21 = -3. A 22 = 0, A 23 = 3 A 31 = 2, A 32 = -3, A 33 = 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}(\operatorname{adj} A)=\left[ \begin{array}{ccc}{7} & {-3} & {2} \\ {3} & {0} & {-3} \\ {-1} & {3} & {1}\end{array}\right]{/tex} Also Determinant |A| = 1(7) - 1(-3) + 1(-1) = 9 {tex}\therefore ~~\mathrm{A}^{-1}=\frac{1}{9} \left[ \begin{array}{ccc}{7} & {-3} & {2} \\ {3} & {0} & {-3} \\ {-1} & {3} & {1}\end{array}\right]{/tex} Given equations can be written as {tex}\left[ \begin{array}{ccc}{1} & {1} & {1} \\ {0} & {1} & {3} \\ {1} & {-2} & {1}\end{array}\right] \left[ \begin{array}{l}{x} \\ {y} \\ {z}\end{array}\right]=\left[ \begin{array}{c}{6} \\ {11} \\ {0}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} AX = B {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} X = A -1 B {tex}\therefore \left[ \begin{array}{l}{x} \\ {y} \\ {z}\end{array}\right]=\frac{1}{9} \left[ \begin{array}{ccc}{7} & {-3} & {2} \\ {3} & {0} & {-3} \\ {-1} & {3} & {1}\end{array}\right] \left[ \begin{array}{c}{6} \\ {11} \\ {0}\end{array}\right]=\left[ \begin{array}{c}{1} \\ {2} \\ {3}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 1, y = 2, z = 3
solution: The given matrix is, {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {1} & {1} & {1} \\ {0} & {1} & {3} \\ {1} & {-2} & {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex} Clearly, the Co-factors are A 11 = 7, A 12 = 3, A 13 = -1 A 21 = -3. A 22 = 0, A 23 = 3 A 31 = 2, A 32 = -3, A 33 = 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}(\operatorname{adj} A)=\left[ \begin{array}{ccc}{7} & {-3} & {2} \\ {3} & {0} & {-3} \\ {-1} & {3} & {1}\end{array}\right]{/tex} Also Determinant |A| = 1(7) - 1(-3) + 1(-1) = 9 {tex}\therefore ~~\mathrm{A}^{-1}=\frac{1}{9} \left[ \begin{array}{ccc}{7} & {-3} & {2} \\ {3} & {0} & {-3} \\ {-1} & {3} & {1}\end{array}\right]{/tex} Given equations can be written as {tex}\left[ \begin{array}{ccc}{1} & {1} & {1} \\ {0} & {1} & {3} \\ {1} & {-2} & {1}\end{array}\right] \left[ \begin{array}{l}{x} \\ {y} \\ {z}\end{array}\right]=\left[ \begin{array}{c}{6} \\ {11} \\ {0}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} AX = B {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} X = A -1 B {tex}\therefore \left[ \begin{array}{l}{x} \\ {y} \\ {z}\end{array}\right]=\frac{1}{9} \left[ \begin{array}{ccc}{7} & {-3} & {2} \\ {3} & {0} & {-3} \\ {-1} & {3} & {1}\end{array}\right] \left[ \begin{array}{c}{6} \\ {11} \\ {0}\end{array}\right]=\left[ \begin{array}{c}{1} \\ {2} \\ {3}\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 1, y = 2, z = 3
solution_html:
The given matrix is, {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {1} & {1} & {1} \\ {0} & {1} & {3} \\ {1} & {-2} & {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Clearly, the Co-factors are
A11 = 7, A12 = 3, A13 = -1
A21 = -3. A22 = 0, A23 = 3
A31 = 2, A32 = -3, A33 = 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}(\operatorname{adj} A)=\left[ \begin{array}{ccc}{7} & {-3} & {2} \\ {3} & {0} & {-3} \\ {-1} & {3} & {1}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
Also Determinant |A| = 1(7) - 1(-3) + 1(-1) = 9
{tex}\therefore ~~\mathrm{A}^{-1}=\frac{1}{9} \left[ \begin{array}{ccc}{7} & {-3} & {2} \\ {3} & {0} & {-3} \\ {-1} & {3} & {1}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
Given equations can be written as {tex}\left[ \begin{array}{ccc}{1} & {1} & {1} \\ {0} & {1} & {3} \\ {1} & {-2} & {1}\end{array}\right] \left[ \begin{array}{l}{x} \\ {y} \\ {z}\end{array}\right]=\left[ \begin{array}{c}{6} \\ {11} \\ {0}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} AX = B 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} X = A-1B
{tex}\therefore \left[ \begin{array}{l}{x} \\ {y} \\ {z}\end{array}\right]=\frac{1}{9} \left[ \begin{array}{ccc}{7} & {-3} & {2} \\ {3} & {0} & {-3} \\ {-1} & {3} & {1}\end{array}\right] \left[ \begin{array}{c}{6} \\ {11} \\ {0}\end{array}\right]=\left[ \begin{array}{c}{1} \\ {2} \\ {3}\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 1, y = 2, z = 3

question_html: If A and B are square matrices of the same order 3, such that |A| = 2 and AB = 2I, write the value of |B|.
question_text: If A and B are square matrices of the same order 3, such that |A| = 2 and AB = 2I, write the value of |B|.
explanation: Here,it is given AB = 2I, |A| = 2 Formula used : |AB| = |A|.|B| and |kA| = k n .|A|, where n is the order of matrix. As, AB = 2I Taking determinant, we obtain |AB| = |2I| |A|.|B| = 2 3 .|I| 2.|B| = 8.1 |B| = 4
solution: Here,it is given AB = 2I, |A| = 2 Formula used : |AB| = |A|.|B| and |kA| = k n .|A|, where n is the order of matrix. As, AB = 2I Taking determinant, we obtain |AB| = |2I| |A|.|B| = 2 3 .|I| 2.|B| = 8.1 |B| = 4
solution_html:
Here,it is given AB = 2I, |A| = 2
Formula used: |AB| = |A|.|B| and |kA| = kn.|A|, where n is the order of matrix.
As, AB = 2I
Taking determinant, we obtain
|AB| = |2I|
|A|.|B| = 23.|I|
2.|B| = 8.1
|B| = 4

question_html: Find the cofactor of the element a23 of the determinant {tex}\mathrm{}\left|\begin{array}{lll} 5 & 3 & 8 \\ 2 & 0 & 1 \\ 1 & 2 & 3 \end{array}\right|{/tex}.
question_text: Find the cofactor of the element a 23 of the determinant {tex}\mathrm{}\left|\begin{array}{lll} 5 & 3 & 8 \\ 2 & 0 & 1 \\ 1 & 2 & 3 \end{array}\right|{/tex} .
explanation: Here, A = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{lll} 5 & 3 & 8 \\ 2 & 0 & 1 \\ 1 & 2 & 3 \end{array}\right|{/tex} First we will find minor of second row. M 21 = 9 - 16 = - 7 M 22 = 15 - 8 = 7 M 23 = 10 - 3 = 7 Now, we will find the cofactors using these minors. A 21 = ( - 1) 2+1 M 21 = - 1( - 7) = 7 A 22 = ( - 1) 2+2 M 22 = 1(7) = 7 A 23 = ( - 1) 2+3 M 23 = - 1(7) = - 7 Now, a 21 = 2 a 22 = 0 a 23 = 1 Therefore value of {tex}\Delta{/tex} = a 21 A 21 + a 22 A 22 + a 23 A 23 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\Delta{/tex} = 2(7) + 0(7) + 1(−7) = 14 + 0 − 7 = 7 Thus we have {tex}\Delta{/tex} = 7.
solution: Here, A = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{lll} 5 & 3 & 8 \\ 2 & 0 & 1 \\ 1 & 2 & 3 \end{array}\right|{/tex} First we will find minor of second row. M 21 = 9 - 16 = - 7 M 22 = 15 - 8 = 7 M 23 = 10 - 3 = 7 Now, we will find the cofactors using these minors. A 21 = ( - 1) 2+1 M 21 = - 1( - 7) = 7 A 22 = ( - 1) 2+2 M 22 = 1(7) = 7 A 23 = ( - 1) 2+3 M 23 = - 1(7) = - 7 Now, a 21 = 2 a 22 = 0 a 23 = 1 Therefore value of {tex}\Delta{/tex} = a 21 A 21 + a 22 A 22 + a 23 A 23 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\Delta{/tex} = 2(7) + 0(7) + 1(−7) = 14 + 0 − 7 = 7 Thus we have {tex}\Delta{/tex} = 7.
solution_html:
Here, A =  {tex}\left|\begin{array}{lll} 5 & 3 & 8 \\ 2 & 0 & 1 \\ 1 & 2 & 3 \end{array}\right|{/tex} 
First we will find minor of second row.
M21 = 9 - 16 = - 7
M22 = 15 - 8 = 7
M23 = 10 - 3 = 7
Now, we will find the cofactors using these minors.
A21 = ( - 1)2+1 M21 = - 1( - 7) = 7
A22 = ( - 1)2+2 M22 = 1(7) = 7
A23 = ( - 1)2+3 M23 = - 1(7) = - 7
Now, a21 = 2
a22 = 0
a23 = 1
Therefore value of  {tex}\Delta{/tex}  = a21A21 + a22A22 + a23A23 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\Delta{/tex} = 2(7) + 0(7) + 1(−7) = 14 + 0 − 7 = 7
Thus we have  {tex}\Delta{/tex} = 7.

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 5 & -3 \\ -3 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} and {tex}B^{-1}=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & 2 \\ 0 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, find A-1 and hence find {tex}(\mathrm{AB})^{-1}{/tex}
question_text: If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 5 & -3 \\ -3 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} and {tex}B^{-1}=\left[\begin{array}{cc} 3 & 2 \\ 0 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , find A -1 and hence find {tex}(\mathrm{AB})^{-1}{/tex}
explanation: We have |A| = 10 - 9 = 1 also c 11 = 2 , c 12 = 3, c 21 = 3, c 22 = 5, Therefore , A -1 = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}2 & 3 \\ 3 & 5\end{array}\right]{/tex} Also , (AB) -1 = B -1 A -1 = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rr}3 & 2 \\ 0 & -1\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ll}2 & 3 \\ 3 & 5\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}12 & 19 \\ -3 & -5\end{array}\right]{/tex}
solution: We have |A| = 10 - 9 = 1 also c 11 = 2 , c 12 = 3, c 21 = 3, c 22 = 5, Therefore , A -1 = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}2 & 3 \\ 3 & 5\end{array}\right]{/tex} Also , (AB) -1 = B -1 A -1 = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rr}3 & 2 \\ 0 & -1\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ll}2 & 3 \\ 3 & 5\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}12 & 19 \\ -3 & -5\end{array}\right]{/tex}
solution_html:
We have  |A| = 10 - 9 = 1

also  c11 = 2 , c12 = 3, c21 = 3, c22 = 5,

Therefore , A-1 = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll}2 & 3 \\ 3 & 5\end{array}\right]{/tex}
Also , (AB)-1 = B-1A-1
= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rr}3 & 2 \\ 0 & -1\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ll}2 & 3 \\ 3 & 5\end{array}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc}12 & 19 \\ -3 & -5\end{array}\right]{/tex}

explanation: We can write {tex}|2.AA'|{/tex} = 4 {tex}|A|{/tex} {tex}|A'|{/tex} = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 = 64

question_html: If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 0 & 1 & 2 \\ 1 & 2 & 3 \\ 3 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, find A-1. Using A-1, solve the system of equations y + 2z = 5, x + 2y + 3z = 10, 3x + y + z = 9
question_text: If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 0 & 1 & 2 \\ 1 & 2 & 3 \\ 3 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , find A -1 . Using A -1 , solve the system of equations y + 2z = 5, x + 2y + 3z = 10, 3x + y + z = 9
explanation: {tex}|A|{/tex} = {tex}\begin{vmatrix}0&1&2\\1&2&3\\3&1&1\end{vmatrix}{/tex} = -1(1-9)+2(1-6) = 8-10 {tex}|A|{/tex} = -2 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A -1 exists Now, A 11 = -1, A 12 = 8, A 13 = -5 A 21 = 1, A 22 = -6, A 23 = 3 A 31 = -1 , A 32 = 2, A 33 = -1 adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}-1 & 1 & -1 \\ 8 & -6 & 2 \\ -5 & 3 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex} A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{|A|}{/tex} adjA = {tex}\frac{-1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}-1 & 1 & -1 \\ 8 & -6 & 2 \\ -5 & 3 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex} Given system of equations are y + 2z = 5, x + 2y + 3z = 10, 3x + y + z = 9 It can be written as {tex}\begin{bmatrix}0&1&2\\1&2&3\\3&1&1\end{bmatrix}{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c}5 \\ 10 \\ 9\end{array}\right]{/tex} or AX = B, where A = {tex}\begin{bmatrix}0&1&2\\1&2&3\\3&1&1\end{bmatrix}{/tex} X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex} and B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c}5 \\ 10 \\ 9\end{array}\right]{/tex} Now AX = B ⇒ X = A -1 B {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}-1 & 1 & -1 \\ 8 & -6 & 2 \\ -5 & 3 & -1\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{r}5 \\ 10 \\ 9\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{l}-4 \\ -2 \\ -4\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l}2 \\ 1 \\ 2\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 2, y = 1, z = 2
solution: {tex}|A|{/tex} = {tex}\begin{vmatrix}0&1&2\\1&2&3\\3&1&1\end{vmatrix}{/tex} = -1(1-9)+2(1-6) = 8-10 {tex}|A|{/tex} = -2 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A -1 exists Now, A 11 = -1, A 12 = 8, A 13 = -5 A 21 = 1, A 22 = -6, A 23 = 3 A 31 = -1 , A 32 = 2, A 33 = -1 adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}-1 & 1 & -1 \\ 8 & -6 & 2 \\ -5 & 3 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex} A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{|A|}{/tex} adjA = {tex}\frac{-1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}-1 & 1 & -1 \\ 8 & -6 & 2 \\ -5 & 3 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex} Given system of equations are y + 2z = 5, x + 2y + 3z = 10, 3x + y + z = 9 It can be written as {tex}\begin{bmatrix}0&1&2\\1&2&3\\3&1&1\end{bmatrix}{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c}5 \\ 10 \\ 9\end{array}\right]{/tex} or AX = B, where A = {tex}\begin{bmatrix}0&1&2\\1&2&3\\3&1&1\end{bmatrix}{/tex} X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex} and B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c}5 \\ 10 \\ 9\end{array}\right]{/tex} Now AX = B ⇒ X = A -1 B {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}-1 & 1 & -1 \\ 8 & -6 & 2 \\ -5 & 3 & -1\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{r}5 \\ 10 \\ 9\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{-1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{l}-4 \\ -2 \\ -4\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l}2 \\ 1 \\ 2\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 2, y = 1, z = 2
solution_html:
{tex}|A|{/tex}= {tex}\begin{vmatrix}0&1&2\\1&2&3\\3&1&1\end{vmatrix}{/tex} = -1(1-9)+2(1-6) = 8-10 

{tex}|A|{/tex} = -2 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A-1 exists
Now, A11 = -1, A12 = 8, A13 = -5
A21 = 1, A22 = -6, A23 = 3
A31 = -1 , A32 = 2, A33 = -1
adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}-1 & 1 & -1 \\ 8 & -6 & 2 \\ -5 & 3 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex}
A-1 = {tex}\frac{1}{|A|}{/tex}adjA = {tex}\frac{-1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}-1 & 1 & -1 \\ 8 & -6 & 2 \\ -5 & 3 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex}
Given system of equations are

y + 2z = 5, x + 2y + 3z = 10, 3x + y + z = 9

It can be written as {tex}\begin{bmatrix}0&1&2\\1&2&3\\3&1&1\end{bmatrix}{/tex}{tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex}= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c}5 \\ 10 \\ 9\end{array}\right]{/tex} or AX = B,
where A ={tex}\begin{bmatrix}0&1&2\\1&2&3\\3&1&1\end{bmatrix}{/tex} X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex}and B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c}5 \\ 10 \\ 9\end{array}\right]{/tex}
Now AX = B ⇒ X = A-1B
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex}= {tex}\frac{-1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{rrr}-1 & 1 & -1 \\ 8 & -6 & 2 \\ -5 & 3 & -1\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{r}5 \\ 10 \\ 9\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{l}x \\ y \\ z\end{array}\right]{/tex}= {tex}\frac{-1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{l}-4 \\ -2 \\ -4\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l}2 \\ 1 \\ 2\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 2, y = 1, z = 2

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
Using matrices, solve the following system of linear equations:
x + 2y -3z = -4
2x + 3y + 2z = 2
3x - 3y - 3z = 11
question_text: Using matrices, solve the following system of linear equations: x + 2y -3z = -4 2x + 3y + 2z = 2 3x - 3y - 3z = 11
explanation: Given system of linear equations are x + 2y -3z = -4 2x + 3y + 2z = 2 3x - 3y - 3z = 11 It can be written as {tex}\begin{bmatrix}1&2&-3\\2&3&2\\3&-3&-3\end{bmatrix}\begin{bmatrix}x\\y\\z\end{bmatrix}\;=\;\begin{bmatrix}-4\\2\\11\end{bmatrix}{/tex} or AX = B where A = {tex}\begin{bmatrix}1&2&-3\\2&3&2\\3&-3&-3\end{bmatrix}{/tex} , X = {tex}\begin{bmatrix}x\\y\\z\end{bmatrix}{/tex} and B = {tex}\begin{bmatrix}-4\\2\\11\end{bmatrix}{/tex} |A| = {tex}\begin{vmatrix}1&2&-3\\2&3&2\\3&-3&-3\end{vmatrix}{/tex} = 1(-9 + 6) -2(-6 - 6) -3(-6 - 9) = -3 +24 +45 = 66 {tex}|A|{/tex} = 66 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 {tex}\therefore{/tex} X = A -1 B A 11= -3 , A 12 ​= 12 , ​​​​​​A 13 ​ = -15 ,​​​​​​ A 2 1 ​= 15 , ​​​​​​A 22 ​= 6, ​​​​​​A 23 ​= 9, ​​​​​​A 31 ​ = 13, ​​​​​​A 32 ​ = -8, ​​​​​​A 33 = -1 adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -3 & 15 & 13 \\ 12 & 6 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{66}​​​​{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -3 & 15 & 13 \\ 12 & 6 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} So X = {tex}\frac{1}{66}​​​​{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -3 & 15 & 13 \\ 12 & 6 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\begin{bmatrix}-4\\2\\11\end{bmatrix}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{66}​​​​{/tex} {tex}\begin{bmatrix}185\\-124\\67\end{bmatrix}{/tex} = {tex}\begin{bmatrix}\frac{185}{66}\\\frac{-62}{33}\\\frac{67}{66}\end{bmatrix}{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{185}{66}{/tex} , y = - {tex}\frac{62}{33}{/tex} , z = {tex}\frac{67}{66}{/tex}
solution: Given system of linear equations are x + 2y -3z = -4 2x + 3y + 2z = 2 3x - 3y - 3z = 11 It can be written as {tex}\begin{bmatrix}1&2&-3\\2&3&2\\3&-3&-3\end{bmatrix}\begin{bmatrix}x\\y\\z\end{bmatrix}\;=\;\begin{bmatrix}-4\\2\\11\end{bmatrix}{/tex} or AX = B where A = {tex}\begin{bmatrix}1&2&-3\\2&3&2\\3&-3&-3\end{bmatrix}{/tex} , X = {tex}\begin{bmatrix}x\\y\\z\end{bmatrix}{/tex} and B = {tex}\begin{bmatrix}-4\\2\\11\end{bmatrix}{/tex} |A| = {tex}\begin{vmatrix}1&2&-3\\2&3&2\\3&-3&-3\end{vmatrix}{/tex} = 1(-9 + 6) -2(-6 - 6) -3(-6 - 9) = -3 +24 +45 = 66 {tex}|A|{/tex} = 66 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 {tex}\therefore{/tex} X = A -1 B A 11= -3 , A 12 ​= 12 , ​​​​​​A 13 ​ = -15 ,​​​​​​ A 2 1 ​= 15 , ​​​​​​A 22 ​= 6, ​​​​​​A 23 ​= 9, ​​​​​​A 31 ​ = 13, ​​​​​​A 32 ​ = -8, ​​​​​​A 33 = -1 adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -3 & 15 & 13 \\ 12 & 6 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{66}​​​​{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -3 & 15 & 13 \\ 12 & 6 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} So X = {tex}\frac{1}{66}​​​​{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -3 & 15 & 13 \\ 12 & 6 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\begin{bmatrix}-4\\2\\11\end{bmatrix}{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{66}​​​​{/tex} {tex}\begin{bmatrix}185\\-124\\67\end{bmatrix}{/tex} = {tex}\begin{bmatrix}\frac{185}{66}\\\frac{-62}{33}\\\frac{67}{66}\end{bmatrix}{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{185}{66}{/tex} , y = - {tex}\frac{62}{33}{/tex} , z = {tex}\frac{67}{66}{/tex}
solution_html:
Given system of linear equations are
x + 2y -3z = -4
2x + 3y + 2z = 2
3x - 3y - 3z = 11

It can be written as {tex}\begin{bmatrix}1&2&-3\\2&3&2\\3&-3&-3\end{bmatrix}\begin{bmatrix}x\\y\\z\end{bmatrix}\;=\;\begin{bmatrix}-4\\2\\11\end{bmatrix}{/tex} 
or AX = B where A = {tex}\begin{bmatrix}1&2&-3\\2&3&2\\3&-3&-3\end{bmatrix}{/tex}, X = {tex}\begin{bmatrix}x\\y\\z\end{bmatrix}{/tex}and B ={tex}\begin{bmatrix}-4\\2\\11\end{bmatrix}{/tex} 

|A| = {tex}\begin{vmatrix}1&2&-3\\2&3&2\\3&-3&-3\end{vmatrix}{/tex} = 1(-9 + 6) -2(-6 - 6) -3(-6 - 9) = -3 +24 +45 = 66

{tex}|A|{/tex} = 66 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0
{tex}\therefore{/tex} X = A-1 B
A11= -3 , A12​= 12 , ​​​​​​A13​ = -15 ,​​​​​​ A21​= 15 , ​​​​​​A22​= 6, ​​​​​​A23​= 9, ​​​​​​A31​ = 13, ​​​​​​A32​ = -8, ​​​​​​A33 = -1

adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -3 & 15 & 13 \\ 12 & 6 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}

A-1 = {tex}\frac{1}{66}​​​​{/tex}{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -3 & 15 & 13 \\ 12 & 6 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}

So X ={tex}\frac{1}{66}​​​​{/tex}{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -3 & 15 & 13 \\ 12 & 6 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\begin{bmatrix}-4\\2\\11\end{bmatrix}{/tex}

 = {tex}\frac{1}{66}​​​​{/tex}{tex}\begin{bmatrix}185\\-124\\67\end{bmatrix}{/tex}

= {tex}\begin{bmatrix}\frac{185}{66}\\\frac{-62}{33}\\\frac{67}{66}\end{bmatrix}{/tex}

x = {tex}\frac{185}{66}{/tex}, y = -{tex}\frac{62}{33}{/tex}, z = {tex}\frac{67}{66}{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Find |AB|, if A = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{cc} 0 & -1 \\ 0 & 2 \end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} and B = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{ll} 3 & 5 \\ 0 & 0 \end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex}
question_text: Find |AB|, if A = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{cc} 0 & -1 \\ 0 & 2 \end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} and B = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{ll} 3 & 5 \\ 0 & 0 \end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex}
explanation: Given, A = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{cc} 0 & -1 \\ 0 & 2 \end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} and B = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{ll} 3 & 5 \\ 0 & 0 \end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} AB = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{cc} 0 & -1 \\ 0 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ll} 3 & 5 \\ 0 & 0 \end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} or AB = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{ll} 0+0 & 0+0 \\ 0+0 & 0+0 \end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} or AB = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{ll} 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 \end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} = 0 Hence, |AB| = 0
solution: Given, A = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{cc} 0 & -1 \\ 0 & 2 \end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} and B = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{ll} 3 & 5 \\ 0 & 0 \end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} AB = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{cc} 0 & -1 \\ 0 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ll} 3 & 5 \\ 0 & 0 \end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} or AB = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{ll} 0+0 & 0+0 \\ 0+0 & 0+0 \end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} or AB = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{ll} 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 \end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} = 0 Hence, |AB| = 0
solution_html:
Given, A = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{cc} 0 & -1 \\ 0 & 2 \end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} and B = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{ll} 3 & 5 \\ 0 & 0 \end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} AB = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{cc} 0 & -1 \\ 0 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ll} 3 & 5 \\ 0 & 0 \end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex}
or AB = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{ll} 0+0 & 0+0 \\ 0+0 & 0+0 \end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex}
or AB = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{ll} 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 \end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} = 0
Hence, |AB| = 0

subject: Mathematics
question_html: For the matrix {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ 1&2&{ - 3} \\ 2&{ - 1}&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}, show that A3 - 6A2 + 5A + 11I = 0. Hence find A-1.
question_text: For the matrix {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ 1&2&{ - 3} \\ 2&{ - 1}&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} , show that A 3 - 6A 2 + 5A + 11I = 0. Hence find A -1 .
solution_html:
Given: {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ 1&2&{ - 3} \\ 2&{ - 1}&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore {A^2} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ 1&2&{ - 3} \\ 2&{ - 1}&3 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ 1&2&{ - 3} \\ 2&{ - 1}&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow {A^2} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {1 + 1 + 2}&{1 + 2 - 1}&{1 - 3 + 3} \\ {1 + 2 - 6}&{1 + 4 + 3}&{1 - 6 - 9} \\ {2 - 1 + 6}&{2 - 2 - 3}&{2 + 3 + 9} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&2&1 \\ { - 3}&8&{ - 14} \\ 7&{ - 3}&{14} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Now {tex}{A^3} = {A^2}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&2&1 \\ { - 3}&8&{ - 14} \\ 7&{ - 3}&{14} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ 1&2&{ - 3} \\ 2&{ - 1}&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} 

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {4 + 2 + 2}&{4 + 4 + 1}&{4 - 6 + 3} \\ { - 3 + 8 - 28}&{ - 3 + 16 + 14}&{ - 3 - 24 - 42} \\ {7 - 3 + 28}&{7 - 6 - 14}&{7 + 9 + 42} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 8&7&1 \\ { - 23}&{27}&{ - 69} \\ {32}&{ - 13}&{58} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

L.H.S. {tex} = {A^3} - 6{A^2} + 5A + 11I{/tex} 

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 8&7&1 \\ { - 23}&{27}&{ - 69} \\ {32}&{ - 13}&{58} \end{array}} \right] - 6\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&2&1 \\ { - 3}&8&{ - 14} \\ 7&{ - 3}&{14} \end{array}} \right] + {/tex}{tex}5\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ 1&2&{ - 3} \\ 2&{ - 1}&3 \end{array}} \right] + 11\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0&0 \\ 0&1&0 \\ 0&0&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 8&7&1 \\ { - 23}&{27}&{ - 69} \\ {32}&{ - 13}&{58} \end{array}} \right] - \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {24}&{12}&6 \\ { - 18}&{48}&{ - 84} \\ {42}&{ - 18}&{84} \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}+ \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5&5&5 \\ 5&{10}&{ - 15} \\ {10}&{ - 5}&{15} \end{array}} \right] + \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11}&0&0 \\ 0&{11}&0 \\ 0&0&{11} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {8 - 24 + 5}&{7 - 12 + 5}&{1 - 6 + 5} \\ { - 23 + 18 + 5}&{27 - 48 + 10}&{ - 69 + 84 - 15} \\ {32 - 42 + 10}&{ - 13 + 18 - 5}&{58 + 84 + 15} \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}+ \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11}&0&0 \\ 0&{11}&0 \\ 0&0&{11} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 11}&0&0 \\ 0&{ - 11}&0 \\ 0&0&{ - 11} \end{array}} \right] + \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {11}&0&0 \\ 0&{11}&0 \\ 0&0&{11} \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 0&0&0 \\ 0&0&0 \\ 0&0&0 \end{array}} \right] = 0{/tex}= R.H.S.

Now,   to find {tex}{A^{ - 1}}{/tex}, multiplying {tex}{A^3} - 6{A^2} + 5A + 11I = 0{/tex} by {tex}{A^{ - 1}}{/tex} 

{tex}\Rightarrow {A^3}{A^{ - 1}} - 6{A^2}{A^{ - 1}} + 5A{A^{ - 1}} + 11I.{A^{ - 1}} = 0.{A^{ - 1}}{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow {A^2} - 6A + 5I + 11{A^{ - 1}} = 0{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow 11{A^{ - 1}} = 6A - 5I - {A^2}{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow 11{A^{ - 1}} = 6\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&1&1 \\ 1&2&{ - 3} \\ 2&{ - 1}&3 \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}- 5\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&0&0 \\ 0&1&0 \\ 0&0&1 \end{array}} \right] - \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&2&1 \\ { - 3}&8&{ - 14} \\ 7&{ - 3}&{14} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow 11{A^{ - 1}} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 6&6&6 \\ 6&{12}&{ - 18} \\ {12}&{ - 6}&{18} \end{array}} \right] {/tex} {tex}- \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5&0&0 \\ 0&5&0 \\ 0&0&5 \end{array}} \right] - \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4&2&1 \\ { - 3}&8&{ - 14} \\ 7&{ - 3}&{14} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow 11{A^{ - 1}} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {6 - 5 - 4}&{6 - 2}&{6 - 1} \\ {6 + 3}&{12 - 5 - 8}&{ - 18 + 14} \\ {12 - 7}&{ - 6 + 3}&{18 - 5 - 14} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow 11{A^{ - 1}} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&4&5 \\ 9&{ - 1}&{ - 4} \\ 5&{ - 3}&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{11}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 3}&4&5 \\ 9&{ - 1}&{ - 4} \\ 5&{ - 3}&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
Solve the following system of equations by matrix method:
3x - 2y + 3z = 8
2x + y - z = 1
4x - 3y + 2z = 4
question_text: Solve the following system of equations by matrix method: 3x - 2y + 3z = 8 2x + y - z = 1 4x - 3y + 2z = 4
explanation: The system of equations can be written in the form AX = B, where {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {3} & {-2} & {3} \\ {2} & {1} & {-1} \\ {4} & {-3} & {2} \end{array}\right], X=\left[\begin{array}{c} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right] \text { and } B=\left[\begin{array}{l} {8} \\ {1} \\ {4} \end{array}\right]{/tex} We see that {tex}|A|{/tex} = 3 (2 – 3) + 2(4 + 4) + 3 (– 6 – 4) = – 17 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 Hence, A is non-singular and so its inverse exists. Now A 11 = –1, A 12 = – 8, A 13 = –10 A 21 = –5, A 22 = – 6, A 23 = 1 A 31 = –1, A 32 = 9, A 33 = 7 Therefore {tex}A^{-1}=-\frac{1}{17}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-1} & {-5} & {-1} \\ {-8} & {-6} & {9} \\ {-10} & {1} & {7} \end{array}\right]{/tex} So, {tex}X=A^{-1} B=-\frac{1}{17}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-1} & {-5} & {-1} \\ {-8} & {-6} & {9} \\ {-10} & {1} & {7} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} {8} \\ {1} \\ {4} \end{array}\right]{/tex} i.e. {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right]=-\frac{1}{17}\left[\begin{array}{c} {-17} \\ {-34} \\ {-51} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} {1} \\ {2} \\ {3} \end{array}\right]{/tex} Hence x = 1, y = 2 and z = 3.
solution: The system of equations can be written in the form AX = B, where {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {3} & {-2} & {3} \\ {2} & {1} & {-1} \\ {4} & {-3} & {2} \end{array}\right], X=\left[\begin{array}{c} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right] \text { and } B=\left[\begin{array}{l} {8} \\ {1} \\ {4} \end{array}\right]{/tex} We see that {tex}|A|{/tex} = 3 (2 – 3) + 2(4 + 4) + 3 (– 6 – 4) = – 17 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 Hence, A is non-singular and so its inverse exists. Now A 11 = –1, A 12 = – 8, A 13 = –10 A 21 = –5, A 22 = – 6, A 23 = 1 A 31 = –1, A 32 = 9, A 33 = 7 Therefore {tex}A^{-1}=-\frac{1}{17}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-1} & {-5} & {-1} \\ {-8} & {-6} & {9} \\ {-10} & {1} & {7} \end{array}\right]{/tex} So, {tex}X=A^{-1} B=-\frac{1}{17}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-1} & {-5} & {-1} \\ {-8} & {-6} & {9} \\ {-10} & {1} & {7} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} {8} \\ {1} \\ {4} \end{array}\right]{/tex} i.e. {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right]=-\frac{1}{17}\left[\begin{array}{c} {-17} \\ {-34} \\ {-51} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} {1} \\ {2} \\ {3} \end{array}\right]{/tex} Hence x = 1, y = 2 and z = 3.
solution_html:
The system of equations can be written in the form AX = B, where
{tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {3} & {-2} & {3} \\ {2} & {1} & {-1} \\ {4} & {-3} & {2} \end{array}\right], X=\left[\begin{array}{c} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right] \text { and } B=\left[\begin{array}{l} {8} \\ {1} \\ {4} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
We see that
{tex}|A|{/tex} = 3 (2 – 3) + 2(4 + 4) + 3 (– 6 – 4) = – 17 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0
Hence, A is non-singular and so its inverse exists. Now
A11 = –1, A12 = – 8, A13 = –10
A21 = –5, A22 = – 6, A23 = 1
A31 = –1, A32 = 9, A33 = 7
Therefore {tex}A^{-1}=-\frac{1}{17}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-1} & {-5} & {-1} \\ {-8} & {-6} & {9} \\ {-10} & {1} & {7} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
So, {tex}X=A^{-1} B=-\frac{1}{17}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-1} & {-5} & {-1} \\ {-8} & {-6} & {9} \\ {-10} & {1} & {7} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} {8} \\ {1} \\ {4} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
i.e. {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right]=-\frac{1}{17}\left[\begin{array}{c} {-17} \\ {-34} \\ {-51} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} {1} \\ {2} \\ {3} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Hence x = 1, y = 2 and z = 3.

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
Using matrices, solve the following system of linear equations:
2x + 3y + 10z = 4
4x - 6y + 5z = 1
6x + 9y - 20z = 2
question_text: Using matrices, solve the following system of linear equations: 2x + 3y + 10z = 4 4x - 6y + 5z = 1 6x + 9y - 20z = 2
explanation: A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 3 & 10 \\ 4 & -6 & 5 \\ 6 & 9 & -20 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]{/tex} , B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 4 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{|A|}{/tex} adj A |A| = 2(120 - 45) - 3(-80 - 30) + 10(36 + 36) = 150 + 330 + 720 = 1200 adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 75 & 110 & 72 \\ 150 & -100 & 0 \\ 75 & 30 & -24 \end{array}\right]^{T}{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 75 & 150 & 25 \\ 110 & -100 & 30 \\ 72 & 0 & -24 \end{array}\right]{/tex} X = A T B = {tex}\frac{1}{1200}{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 75 & 150 & 75 \\ 110 & -100 & 30 \\ 72 & 0 & -24 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 4 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{1200}\left[\begin{array}{l} 600 \\ 400 \\ 240 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 / 2 \\ 1 / 3 \\ 1 / 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} , y = {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} , z = {tex}\frac{1}{5}{/tex}
solution: A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 3 & 10 \\ 4 & -6 & 5 \\ 6 & 9 & -20 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]{/tex} , B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 4 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{|A|}{/tex} adj A |A| = 2(120 - 45) - 3(-80 - 30) + 10(36 + 36) = 150 + 330 + 720 = 1200 adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 75 & 110 & 72 \\ 150 & -100 & 0 \\ 75 & 30 & -24 \end{array}\right]^{T}{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 75 & 150 & 25 \\ 110 & -100 & 30 \\ 72 & 0 & -24 \end{array}\right]{/tex} X = A T B = {tex}\frac{1}{1200}{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 75 & 150 & 75 \\ 110 & -100 & 30 \\ 72 & 0 & -24 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 4 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{1200}\left[\begin{array}{l} 600 \\ 400 \\ 240 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 / 2 \\ 1 / 3 \\ 1 / 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex} , y = {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex} , z = {tex}\frac{1}{5}{/tex}
solution_html:
A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 3 & 10 \\ 4 & -6 & 5 \\ 6 & 9 & -20 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]{/tex}, B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 4 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} 
A-1 = {tex}\frac{1}{|A|}{/tex}adj A
|A| = 2(120 - 45) - 3(-80 - 30) + 10(36 + 36)
= 150 + 330 + 720 = 1200
adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 75 & 110 & 72 \\ 150 & -100 & 0 \\ 75 & 30 & -24 \end{array}\right]^{T}{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 75 & 150 & 25 \\ 110 & -100 & 30 \\ 72 & 0 & -24 \end{array}\right]{/tex} 
X = AT B = {tex}\frac{1}{1200}{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 75 & 150 & 75 \\ 110 & -100 & 30 \\ 72 & 0 & -24 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 4 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} 
= {tex}\frac{1}{1200}\left[\begin{array}{l} 600 \\ 400 \\ 240 \end{array}\right]{/tex} =  {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} 1 / 2 \\ 1 / 3 \\ 1 / 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{1}{2}{/tex}, y = {tex}\frac{1}{3}{/tex}, z = {tex}\frac{1}{5}{/tex}

question_text: If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ll} \mathrm{p} & 2 \\ 2 & \mathrm{p} \end{array}\right]{/tex} and |A 3 | = 125, then find the values of p.
explanation: |A| = p 2 - 4 |A 3 | = 125 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |A| 3 = 125 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |A| = 5 {tex}\therefore{/tex} p 2 - 4 = 5 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} p = {tex}\pm{/tex} 3

question_html: If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & -1 & 1 \\ 2 & -1 & 0 \\ 1 & 0 & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex}, find A2 and show that A2 = A-1.
question_text: If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & -1 & 1 \\ 2 & -1 & 0 \\ 1 & 0 & 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , find A 2 and show that A 2 = A -1 .
explanation: {tex}A^2=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & -1 & 1 \\ 2 & -1 & 0 \\ 1 & 0 & 0\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & -1 & 1 \\ 2 & -1 & 0 \\ 1 & 0 & 0\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}0 & 0 & 1 \\ 0 & -1 & 2 \\ 1 & -1 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}A^2 \cdot A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}0 & 0 & 1 \\ 0 & -1 & 2 \\ 1 & -1 & 1\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & -1 & 1 \\ 2 & -1 & 0 \\ 1 & 0 & 0\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{lll}1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1\end{array}\right]=I{/tex} or {tex}A \cdot A^2=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & -1 & 1 \\ 2 & -1 & 0 \\ 1 & 0 & 0\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ccc}0 & 0 & 1 \\ 0 & -1 & 2 \\ 1 & -1 & 1\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{lll}1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1\end{array}\right]=I{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A 2 = A -1
solution: {tex}A^2=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & -1 & 1 \\ 2 & -1 & 0 \\ 1 & 0 & 0\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & -1 & 1 \\ 2 & -1 & 0 \\ 1 & 0 & 0\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}0 & 0 & 1 \\ 0 & -1 & 2 \\ 1 & -1 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}A^2 \cdot A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}0 & 0 & 1 \\ 0 & -1 & 2 \\ 1 & -1 & 1\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & -1 & 1 \\ 2 & -1 & 0 \\ 1 & 0 & 0\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{lll}1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1\end{array}\right]=I{/tex} or {tex}A \cdot A^2=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & -1 & 1 \\ 2 & -1 & 0 \\ 1 & 0 & 0\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ccc}0 & 0 & 1 \\ 0 & -1 & 2 \\ 1 & -1 & 1\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{lll}1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1\end{array}\right]=I{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A 2 = A -1
solution_html:
{tex}A^2=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & -1 & 1 \\ 2 & -1 & 0 \\ 1 & 0 & 0\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & -1 & 1 \\ 2 & -1 & 0 \\ 1 & 0 & 0\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}0 & 0 & 1 \\ 0 & -1 & 2 \\ 1 & -1 & 1\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}A^2 \cdot A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}0 & 0 & 1 \\ 0 & -1 & 2 \\ 1 & -1 & 1\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & -1 & 1 \\ 2 & -1 & 0 \\ 1 & 0 & 0\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{lll}1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1\end{array}\right]=I{/tex}
or {tex}A \cdot A^2=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}1 & -1 & 1 \\ 2 & -1 & 0 \\ 1 & 0 & 0\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{ccc}0 & 0 & 1 \\ 0 & -1 & 2 \\ 1 & -1 & 1\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{lll}1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1\end{array}\right]=I{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A2 = A-1

question_html:
Using matrices, solve the following system of linear equations:
x + 2y - 3z = -4
2x + 3y + 2z = 2
3x - 3y - 4z = 11
question_text: Using matrices, solve the following system of linear equations: x + 2y - 3z = -4 2x + 3y + 2z = 2 3x - 3y - 4z = 11
explanation: |A| = 67 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 {tex}\therefore{/tex} X = A -1 B {tex}\operatorname{adj} \mathrm{A}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-6 & 17 & 13 \\ 14 & 5 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{A}^{-1}=\frac{1}{67}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-6 & 17 & 13 \\ 14 & 5 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex} So X = {tex}\frac{1}{67}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-6 & 17 & 13 \\ 14 & 5 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}-4 \\ 2 \\ 11\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{67}\left[\begin{array}{c}201 \\ -134 \\ 67\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c}3 \\ -2 \\ 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} x = 3, y = -2, z = 1
solution: |A| = 67 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 {tex}\therefore{/tex} X = A -1 B {tex}\operatorname{adj} \mathrm{A}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-6 & 17 & 13 \\ 14 & 5 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\mathrm{A}^{-1}=\frac{1}{67}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-6 & 17 & 13 \\ 14 & 5 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex} So X = {tex}\frac{1}{67}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-6 & 17 & 13 \\ 14 & 5 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}-4 \\ 2 \\ 11\end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\frac{1}{67}\left[\begin{array}{c}201 \\ -134 \\ 67\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c}3 \\ -2 \\ 1\end{array}\right]{/tex} x = 3, y = -2, z = 1
solution_html:
|A| = 67 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0
{tex}\therefore{/tex} X = A-1B
{tex}\operatorname{adj} \mathrm{A}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-6 & 17 & 13 \\ 14 & 5 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\mathrm{A}^{-1}=\frac{1}{67}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-6 & 17 & 13 \\ 14 & 5 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1\end{array}\right]{/tex}
So X = {tex}\frac{1}{67}\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-6 & 17 & 13 \\ 14 & 5 & -8 \\ -15 & 9 & -1\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}-4 \\ 2 \\ 11\end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\frac{1}{67}\left[\begin{array}{c}201 \\ -134 \\ 67\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c}3 \\ -2 \\ 1\end{array}\right]{/tex}
x = 3, y = -2, z = 1
