question_html:
Find the adjoint of the matrix
{tex} A = \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { - 1 } & { - 2 } & { - 2 } \\ { 2 } & { 1 } & { - 2 } \\ { 2 } & { - 2 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}and hence show that A(adj A) = |A| I3.
question_text: Find the adjoint of the matrix {tex} A = \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { - 1 } & { - 2 } & { - 2 } \\ { 2 } & { 1 } & { - 2 } \\ { 2 } & { - 2 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} and hence show that A(adj A) = |A| I 3 .
solution_html:
Given, A = {tex} \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { - 1 } & { - 2 } & { - 2 } \\ { 2 } & { 1 } & { - 2 } \\ { 2 } & { - 2 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Let Aij be the co-factor of an element aij of |A|. Then, co-factors of elements of |A| are
{tex} A _ { 11 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 1 + 1 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { - 2 } \\ { - 2 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right| = ( 1 - 4 ) = - 3{/tex}
{tex} A _ { 12 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 1 + 2 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 2 } & { - 2 } \\ { 2 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right| = - ( 2 + 4 ) = - 6{/tex}
{tex} A _ { 13 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 1 + 3 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 2 } & { 1 } \\ { 2 } & { - 2 } \end{array} \right| = ( - 4 - 2 ) = - 6{/tex}
{tex} A _ { 21 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 2 + 1 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { - 2 } & { - 2 } \\ { - 2 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right| = - ( - 2 - 4 ) = 6{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 22 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 2 + 2 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { - 1 } & { - 2 } \\ { 2 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right| = ( - 1 + 4 ) = 3{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 23 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 2 + 3 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { - 1 } & { - 2 } \\ { 2 } & { - 2 } \end{array} \right| = - ( 2 + 4 ) = - 6{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 31 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 3 + 1 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { - 2 } & { - 2 } \\ { 1 } & { - 2 } \end{array} \right| = ( 4 + 2 ) = 6{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 32 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 3 + 2 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { - 1 } & { - 2 } \\ { 2 } & { - 2 } \end{array} \right| = - ( 2 + 4 ) = - 6{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 33 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 3 + 3 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { - 1 } & { - 2 } \\ { 2 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right| = ( - 1 + 4 ) = 3{/tex}
Clearly, the adjoint of the matrix A is given by
adj A = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { l l l } { A _ { 11 } } & { A _ { 21 } } & { A _ { 31 } } \\ { A _ { 12 } } & { A _ { 22 } } & { A _ { 32 } } \\ { A _ { 13 } } & { A _ { 23 } } & { A _ { 33 } } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { - 3 } & { 6 } & { 6 } \\ { - 6 } & { 3 } & { - 6 } \\ { - 6 } & { - 6 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Now, |A| = {tex}\left| \begin{array} { c c c } { 1 } & { - 2 } & { - 2 } \\ { 2 } & { 1 } & { - 2 } \\ { 2 } & { - 2 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right|{/tex}
= -1 (1 - 4) + 2 (2 + 4) - 2 (-4 - 2)
= -1(-3) + 2(6) - 2(-6)
= 3+ 12+ 12= 27
and A.(adj A) = {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { - 1 } & { - 2 } & { - 2 } \\ { 2 } & { 1 } & { - 2 } \\ { 2 } & { - 2 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { - 3 } & { 6 } & { 6 } \\ { - 6 } & { 3 } & { - 6 } \\ { - 6 } & { - 6 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
= {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 3 + 12 + 12 } & { - 6 - 6 + 12 } & { - 6 + 12 - 6 } \\ { - 6 - 6 + 12 } & { 12 + 3 + 12 } & { 12 - 6 - 6 } \\ { - 6 + 12 - 6 } & { 12 - 6 - 6 } & { 12 + 12 + 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
= {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 27 } & { 0 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 27 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 0 } & { 27 } \end{array} \right] = 27 \left[ \begin{array} { l l l } { 1 } & { 0 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 1 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 0 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
= 27I3 = |A|I3

question_html: If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rr} \cos \theta & \sin \theta \\ -\sin \theta & \cos \theta \end{array}\right]{/tex} then for any natural number, find the value of Det (An).
question_text: If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rr} \cos \theta & \sin \theta \\ -\sin \theta & \cos \theta \end{array}\right]{/tex} then for any natural number, find the value of Det (A n ).
explanation: Let A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} \cos \theta & \sin \theta \\ -\sin \theta & \cos \theta \end{array}\right]{/tex} Then, A 2 = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} \cos \theta & \sin \theta \\ -\sin \theta & \cos \theta \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} \cos \theta & \sin \theta \\ -\sin \theta & \cos \theta \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} \cos ^{2} \theta-\sin ^{2} \theta & \cos \theta \sin \theta+\sin \theta \cos \theta \\ -\sin \theta \cos \theta-\cos \theta \sin \theta & -\sin ^{2} \theta+\cos ^{2} \theta \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} \cos 2 \theta & \sin 2 \theta \\ -\sin 2 \theta & \cos 2 \theta \end{array}\right]{/tex} Similarly, A n = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} \cos (n \theta) & \sin (n \theta) \\ -\sin (n \theta) & \cos (n \theta) \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} |A n | = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{cc} \cos (n \theta) & \sin (n \theta) \\ -\sin (n \theta) & \cos (n \theta) \end{array}\right|{/tex} = {tex}\cos ^{2}(n \theta)+\sin ^{2}(n \theta){/tex} = 1 Hence, Det (A n ) = 1
solution: Let A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} \cos \theta & \sin \theta \\ -\sin \theta & \cos \theta \end{array}\right]{/tex} Then, A 2 = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} \cos \theta & \sin \theta \\ -\sin \theta & \cos \theta \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} \cos \theta & \sin \theta \\ -\sin \theta & \cos \theta \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} \cos ^{2} \theta-\sin ^{2} \theta & \cos \theta \sin \theta+\sin \theta \cos \theta \\ -\sin \theta \cos \theta-\cos \theta \sin \theta & -\sin ^{2} \theta+\cos ^{2} \theta \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} \cos 2 \theta & \sin 2 \theta \\ -\sin 2 \theta & \cos 2 \theta \end{array}\right]{/tex} Similarly, A n = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} \cos (n \theta) & \sin (n \theta) \\ -\sin (n \theta) & \cos (n \theta) \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} |A n | = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{cc} \cos (n \theta) & \sin (n \theta) \\ -\sin (n \theta) & \cos (n \theta) \end{array}\right|{/tex} = {tex}\cos ^{2}(n \theta)+\sin ^{2}(n \theta){/tex} = 1 Hence, Det (A n ) = 1
solution_html:
Let A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} \cos \theta & \sin \theta \\ -\sin \theta & \cos \theta \end{array}\right]{/tex} 
Then, A2 = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} \cos \theta & \sin \theta \\ -\sin \theta & \cos \theta \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} \cos \theta & \sin \theta \\ -\sin \theta & \cos \theta \end{array}\right]{/tex} 
= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} \cos ^{2} \theta-\sin ^{2} \theta & \cos \theta \sin \theta+\sin \theta \cos \theta \\ -\sin \theta \cos \theta-\cos \theta \sin \theta & -\sin ^{2} \theta+\cos ^{2} \theta \end{array}\right]{/tex} 
= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} \cos 2 \theta & \sin 2 \theta \\ -\sin 2 \theta & \cos 2 \theta \end{array}\right]{/tex} 
Similarly, An = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{cc} \cos (n \theta) & \sin (n \theta) \\ -\sin (n \theta) & \cos (n \theta) \end{array}\right]{/tex} 
{tex}\therefore{/tex}
|An| = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{cc} \cos (n \theta) & \sin (n \theta) \\ -\sin (n \theta) & \cos (n \theta) \end{array}\right|{/tex} 
= {tex}\cos ^{2}(n \theta)+\sin ^{2}(n \theta){/tex} 
= 1
Hence, Det (An) = 1

question_text: If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 0 & -1 \\ 5 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} , then find A -1

{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 0 & -1 / 2 \\ 0 & 1 & 5 / 2 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 / 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 / 2 & 0 & 0 \\ -5 / 2 & 1 & 0 \\ 5 / 2 & -1 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}A      [By {tex}\mathrm{R}_{3} \rightarrow 2 \mathrm{R}_{3}{/tex}]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 0 & -1 / 2 \\ 0 & 1 & 5 / 2 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 / 2 & 0 & 0 \\ -5 / 2 & 1 & 0 \\ 5 & -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}A       [By {tex}\mathrm{R}_{2} \rightarrow \mathrm{R}_{2}-\frac{5}{2} \mathrm{R}_{3}{/tex}]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 0 & -1 / 2 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 / 2 & 0 & 0 \\ -15 & 6 & -5 \\ 5 & -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}A     [By {tex}\mathrm{R}_{1} \rightarrow \mathrm{R}_{1}+\frac{1}{2} \mathrm{R}_{3}{/tex}]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 1 & 0 & 0 \\ 0 & 1 & 0 \\ 0 & 0 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & -1 & 1 \\ -15 & 6 & -5 \\ 5 & -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} A-1 = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & -1 & 1 \\ -15 & 6 & -5 \\ 5 & -2 & 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}           {{tex}\because{/tex} I = A-1 A}

question_html: If A ={tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 5 & 6 & -3 \\ -4 & 3 & 2 \\ -4 & -7 & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex}then write the cofactor of the element a21 of its 2nd row.
question_text: If A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 5 & 6 & -3 \\ -4 & 3 & 2 \\ -4 & -7 & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} then write the cofactor of the element a 21 of its 2 nd row.
explanation: Given, {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 5 & 6 & -3 \\ -4 & 3 & 2 \\ -4 & -7 & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Here, a 21 = -4 The cofactor of the element a 21 = -4 is given by C 21 = (-1) (2+1) {tex}\left|\begin{array}{cc} 6 & -3 \\ -7 & 3 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = -1 (18 - 21) = 3
solution: Given, {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 5 & 6 & -3 \\ -4 & 3 & 2 \\ -4 & -7 & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Here, a 21 = -4 The cofactor of the element a 21 = -4 is given by C 21 = (-1) (2+1) {tex}\left|\begin{array}{cc} 6 & -3 \\ -7 & 3 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = -1 (18 - 21) = 3
solution_html:
Given,{tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 5 & 6 & -3 \\ -4 & 3 & 2 \\ -4 & -7 & 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Here, a21 = -4
The cofactor of the element a21 = -4 is given by
C21 = (-1)(2+1) {tex}\left|\begin{array}{cc} 6 & -3 \\ -7 & 3 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = -1 (18 - 21) = 3

question_html: If A is a square matrix of order 3 and |3A| = k |A|, then write the value of k.
question_text: If A is a square matrix of order 3 and |3A| = k |A|, then write the value of k.
explanation: We know that, if A is a square matrix of order n. Then, |mA|= m n |A|. Here, the matrix A is of order {tex}3 \times 3{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} |3A|= (3) 3 |A| = 27 |A| On comparing with given equation, we get k = 27
solution: We know that, if A is a square matrix of order n. Then, |mA|= m n |A|. Here, the matrix A is of order {tex}3 \times 3{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} |3A|= (3) 3 |A| = 27 |A| On comparing with given equation, we get k = 27
solution_html:
We know that, if A is a square matrix of order n.
Then, |mA|= mn|A|.
Here, the matrix A is of order {tex}3 \times 3{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} |3A|= (3)3 |A| = 27 |A|
On comparing with given equation, we get
k = 27

question_html: If {tex}\left| \begin{array} { c c } { 2 x } & { 5 } \\ { 8 } & { x } \end{array} \right| = \left| \begin{array} { r r } { 6 } & { - 2 } \\ { 7 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right|{/tex}, then write the value of x.
question_text: If {tex}\left| \begin{array} { c c } { 2 x } & { 5 } \\ { 8 } & { x } \end{array} \right| = \left| \begin{array} { r r } { 6 } & { - 2 } \\ { 7 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right|{/tex} , then write the value of x.
explanation: Given, {tex}\left| \begin{array} { c c } { 2 x } & { 5 } \\ { 8 } & { x } \end{array} \right| = \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 6 } & { - 2 } \\ { 7 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right|{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x 2 - 40 = 18- (-14) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}2 x ^ { 2 } - 40 = 32 \Rightarrow 2 x ^ { 2 } = 72 \Rightarrow x ^ { 2 } = 36{/tex} {tex}\therefore \quad x = \pm 6{/tex}
solution: Given, {tex}\left| \begin{array} { c c } { 2 x } & { 5 } \\ { 8 } & { x } \end{array} \right| = \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 6 } & { - 2 } \\ { 7 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right|{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x 2 - 40 = 18- (-14) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}2 x ^ { 2 } - 40 = 32 \Rightarrow 2 x ^ { 2 } = 72 \Rightarrow x ^ { 2 } = 36{/tex} {tex}\therefore \quad x = \pm 6{/tex}
solution_html:
Given, {tex}\left| \begin{array} { c c } { 2 x } & { 5 } \\ { 8 } & { x } \end{array} \right| = \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 6 } & { - 2 } \\ { 7 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right|{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 2x2 - 40 = 18- (-14)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}2 x ^ { 2 } - 40 = 32 \Rightarrow 2 x ^ { 2 } = 72 \Rightarrow x ^ { 2 } = 36{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad x = \pm 6{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
Write the value of the determinant
{tex}\left| \begin{array} { c c } { p } & { p + 1 } \\ { p - 1 } & { p } \end{array} \right|{/tex}.
question_text: Write the value of the determinant {tex}\left| \begin{array} { c c } { p } & { p + 1 } \\ { p - 1 } & { p } \end{array} \right|{/tex} .
explanation: Let {tex}\Delta = \left| \begin{array} { c c } { p } & { p + 1 } \\ { p - 1 } & { p } \end{array} \right|{/tex} On expanding, we get {tex}\Delta = p ^ { 2 } - ( p - 1 ) ( p + 1 ) \Rightarrow \Delta = p ^ { 2 } - \left( p ^ { 2 } - 1 ^ { 2 } \right){/tex} {tex}\left[ \because a ^ { 2 } - b ^ { 2 } = ( a + b ) ( a - b ) \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \Delta = p ^ { 2 } - p ^ { 2 } + 1{/tex} {tex}\therefore \quad \Delta = 1{/tex}
solution: Let {tex}\Delta = \left| \begin{array} { c c } { p } & { p + 1 } \\ { p - 1 } & { p } \end{array} \right|{/tex} On expanding, we get {tex}\Delta = p ^ { 2 } - ( p - 1 ) ( p + 1 ) \Rightarrow \Delta = p ^ { 2 } - \left( p ^ { 2 } - 1 ^ { 2 } \right){/tex} {tex}\left[ \because a ^ { 2 } - b ^ { 2 } = ( a + b ) ( a - b ) \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad \Delta = p ^ { 2 } - p ^ { 2 } + 1{/tex} {tex}\therefore \quad \Delta = 1{/tex}
solution_html:
Let {tex}\Delta = \left| \begin{array} { c c } { p } & { p + 1 } \\ { p - 1 } & { p } \end{array} \right|{/tex}
On expanding, we get
{tex}\Delta = p ^ { 2 } - ( p - 1 ) ( p + 1 ) \Rightarrow \Delta = p ^ { 2 } - \left( p ^ { 2 } - 1 ^ { 2 } \right){/tex}
{tex}\left[ \because a ^ { 2 } - b ^ { 2 } = ( a + b ) ( a - b ) \right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad \Delta = p ^ { 2 } - p ^ { 2 } + 1{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad \Delta = 1{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Find |adj A|, if A= {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 5 } & { 2 } \\ { 7 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}.
question_text: Find |adj A|, if A= {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 5 } & { 2 } \\ { 7 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} .
explanation: Given A = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 5 } & { 2 } \\ { 7 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Clearly, |A| = {tex}\left| \begin{array} { l l } { 5 } & { 2 } \\ { 7 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right|{/tex} = 15 - 14 =1 We know that, if A is a non-singular matrix of order n |adj (A) | = |A| n-1 . {tex}\therefore{/tex} |adj(A) |= |A| 2-1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |adj(A) |= (1) 2-1 = 1
solution: Given A = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 5 } & { 2 } \\ { 7 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Clearly, |A| = {tex}\left| \begin{array} { l l } { 5 } & { 2 } \\ { 7 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right|{/tex} = 15 - 14 =1 We know that, if A is a non-singular matrix of order n |adj (A) | = |A| n-1 . {tex}\therefore{/tex} |adj(A) |= |A| 2-1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |adj(A) |= (1) 2-1 = 1
solution_html:
Given A = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 5 } & { 2 } \\ { 7 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Clearly, |A| = {tex}\left| \begin{array} { l l } { 5 } & { 2 } \\ { 7 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right|{/tex}= 15 - 14 =1
We know that, if A is a non-singular matrix of order n |adj (A) | = |A|n-1.
{tex}\therefore{/tex}|adj(A) |= |A|2-1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |adj(A) |= (1)2-1 = 1

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}\left|\begin{array}{cc} 3 x & 7 \\ -2 & 4 \end{array}\right|=\left|\begin{array}{ll} 8 & 7 \\ 6 & 4 \end{array}\right|{/tex}. find the value of x.
question_text: If {tex}\left|\begin{array}{cc} 3 x & 7 \\ -2 & 4 \end{array}\right|=\left|\begin{array}{ll} 8 & 7 \\ 6 & 4 \end{array}\right|{/tex} . find the value of x.
explanation: We are given that, {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 3 x & 7 \\ -2 & 4 \end{array}\right|=\left|\begin{array}{ll} 8 & 7 \\ 6 & 4 \end{array}\right|{/tex} We need to find the value of x. Determinant of 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 matrix is found as, LHS = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{cc} 3 x & 7 \\ -2 & 4 \end{array}\right|{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} LHS = 3x {tex}\times{/tex} 4 - 7 {tex}\times{/tex} (-2) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} LHS = 12x - (-14) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} LHS = 12x + 14 Let us take right hand side (RHS) of the given matrix equation. RHS = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 8 & 7 \\ 6 & 4 \end{array}\right|{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} RHS = 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 - 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} RHS = 32 - 42 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} RHS = -10 Now, LHS = RHS {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 12x + 14 = -10 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 12x = -10 - 14 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 12x = -24 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{-24}{12}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = -2 Thus, the value of x is -2
solution: We are given that, {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 3 x & 7 \\ -2 & 4 \end{array}\right|=\left|\begin{array}{ll} 8 & 7 \\ 6 & 4 \end{array}\right|{/tex} We need to find the value of x. Determinant of 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 matrix is found as, LHS = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{cc} 3 x & 7 \\ -2 & 4 \end{array}\right|{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} LHS = 3x {tex}\times{/tex} 4 - 7 {tex}\times{/tex} (-2) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} LHS = 12x - (-14) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} LHS = 12x + 14 Let us take right hand side (RHS) of the given matrix equation. RHS = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 8 & 7 \\ 6 & 4 \end{array}\right|{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} RHS = 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 - 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 6 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} RHS = 32 - 42 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} RHS = -10 Now, LHS = RHS {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 12x + 14 = -10 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 12x = -10 - 14 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 12x = -24 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{-24}{12}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = -2 Thus, the value of x is -2
solution_html:
We are given that,
{tex}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 3 x & 7 \\ -2 & 4 \end{array}\right|=\left|\begin{array}{ll} 8 & 7 \\ 6 & 4 \end{array}\right|{/tex} 
We need to find the value of x.
Determinant of 2 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 matrix is found as,
LHS = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{cc} 3 x & 7 \\ -2 & 4 \end{array}\right|{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} LHS = 3x {tex}\times{/tex} 4 - 7 {tex}\times{/tex} (-2)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} LHS = 12x - (-14)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} LHS = 12x + 14
Let us take right hand side (RHS) of the given matrix equation.
RHS = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ll} 8 & 7 \\ 6 & 4 \end{array}\right|{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} RHS = 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 4 - 7 {tex}\times{/tex} 6
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} RHS = 32 - 42
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} RHS = -10
Now,
LHS = RHS
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 12x + 14 = -10
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 12x = -10 - 14
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 12x = -24
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = {tex}\frac{-24}{12}{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = -2
Thus, the value of x is -2

question_html: A total amount of Rs 7000 is deposited in three different savings bank accounts with annual interest rates of 5%, 8% and {tex}8 \frac { 1 } { 2 } \%{/tex} ,respectively. The total annual 2% interest from these three accounts is Rs 550. Equal amounts have been deposited in the 5% and 8% savings accounts. Find the amount deposited in each of the three accounts, with the help of matrices.
question_text: A total amount of Rs 7000 is deposited in three different savings bank accounts with annual interest rates of 5%, 8% and {tex}8 \frac { 1 } { 2 } \%{/tex} ,respectively. The total annual 2% interest from these three accounts is Rs 550. Equal amounts have been deposited in the 5% and 8% savings accounts. Find the amount deposited in each of the three accounts, with the help of matrices.
explanation: Let Rs x, Rs y and Rs z be invested in saving accounts at the rate of 5%, 8% and {tex}8 \frac { 1 } { 2 } \%{/tex} , respectively. Then, according to given condition ,we have the following system of equations x + y + z = 7000, ...(i) and {tex}\frac { 5 x } { 100 } + \frac { 8 y } { 100 } + \frac { 17 z } { 200 } = 550{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 10x + 16x + 17z = 110000 ...(ii) Also, x - y = 0 ...(iii) This system of equations can be written in matrix from as AX = B where, A = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 1 } & { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 10 } & { 16 } & { 17 } \\ { 1 } & { - 1 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right] , X = \left[ \begin{array} { c } { x } \\ { y } \\ { z } \end{array} \right] \text { and } B = \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 7000 } \\ { 110000 } \\ { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Here, |A| = {tex}\left| \begin{array} { c c c } { 1 } & { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 10 } & { 16 } & { 17 } \\ { 1 } & { - 1 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right|{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}| A | = 1 ( 0 + 17 ) - 1 ( 0 - 17 ) + 1 ( - 10 - 16 ){/tex} = 17 +17 - 26 = 8 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0 So, A is non- singular matrix and its inverse exists. Now, cofactors of elements of |A| are, {tex}A _ { 11 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 2 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 16 } & { 17 } \\ { - 1 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( 0 + 17 ) = 17{/tex} {tex}A _ { 12 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 3 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 10 } & { 17 } \\ { 1 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right] = - 1 ( 0 - 17 ) = 17{/tex} {tex}A _ { 13 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 4 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 10 } & { 16 } \\ { 1 } & { - 1 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( - 10 - 16 ) = - 26{/tex} {tex}A _ { 21 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 3 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { - 1 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( 0 + 1 ) = - 1{/tex} {tex}A _ { 22 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 4 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 1 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( 0 - 1 ) = - 1{/tex} {tex}A _ { 23 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 5 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 1 } & { - 1 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( - 1 - 1 ) = 2{/tex} {tex}A _ { 31 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 4 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 16 } & { 17 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( 17 - 16 ) = 1{/tex} {tex}A _ { 32 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 5 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 10 } & { 17 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( 17 - 10 ) = - 7{/tex} {tex}A _ { 33 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 6 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 10 } & { 16 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( 16 - 10 ) = 6{/tex} {tex}\therefore \operatorname { adj } ( A ) = \left[ \begin{array} { l l l } { A _ { 11 } } & { A _ { 12 } } & { A _ { 13 } } \\ { A _ { 21 } } & { A _ { 22 } } & { A _ { 23 } } \\ { A _ { 31 } } & { A _ { 32 } } & { A _ { 33 } } \end{array} \right] ^ { T }{/tex} {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 17 } & { 17 } & { - 26 } \\ { - 1 } & { - 1 } & { 2 } \\ { 1 } & { - 7 } & { 6 } \end{array} \right] ^ { T } = \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 17 } & { - 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 17 } & { - 1 } & { - 7 } \\ { - 26 } & { 2 } & { 6 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Now, A -1 = {tex} \frac { \operatorname { adj } ( A ) } { | A | } = \frac { 1 } { 8 } \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 17 } & { - 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 17 } & { - 1 } & { - 7 } \\ { - 26 } & { 2 } & { 6 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} and the solution of given system is given by X = A -1 B. {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { l } { x } \\ { y } \\ { z } \end{array} \right] = \frac { 1 } { 8 } \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 17 } & { - 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 17 } & { - 1 } & { - 7 } \\ { - 26 } & { 2 } & { 6 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 7000 } \\ { 110000 } \\ { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 8 } \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 119000 - 110000 + 0 } \\ { 119000 - 110000 + 0 } \\ { - 182000 + 220000 + 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 8 } \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 9000 } \\ { 9000 } \\ { 38000 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 1125 } \\ { 1125 } \\ { 4750 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} On comparing the corresponding elements, we get x = 1125, y = 1125, z = 4750. Hence, the amount deposited in each type of account is Rs 1125, Rs 1125 and Rs 4750, respectively.

10 ) = 6{/tex} {tex}\therefore \operatorname { adj } ( A ) = \left[ \begin{array} { l l l } { A _ { 11 } } & { A _ { 12 } } & { A _ { 13 } } \\ { A _ { 21 } } & { A _ { 22 } } & { A _ { 23 } } \\ { A _ { 31 } } & { A _ { 32 } } & { A _ { 33 } } \end{array} \right] ^ { T }{/tex} {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 17 } & { 17 } & { - 26 } \\ { - 1 } & { - 1 } & { 2 } \\ { 1 } & { - 7 } & { 6 } \end{array} \right] ^ { T } = \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 17 } & { - 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 17 } & { - 1 } & { - 7 } \\ { - 26 } & { 2 } & { 6 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Now, A -1 = {tex} \frac { \operatorname { adj } ( A ) } { | A | } = \frac { 1 } { 8 } \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 17 } & { - 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 17 } & { - 1 } & { - 7 } \\ { - 26 } & { 2 } & { 6 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} and the solution of given system is given by X = A -1 B. {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { l } { x } \\ { y } \\ { z } \end{array} \right] = \frac { 1 } { 8 } \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 17 } & { - 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 17 } & { - 1 } & { - 7 } \\ { - 26 } & { 2 } & { 6 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 7000 } \\ { 110000 } \\ { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 8 } \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 119000 - 110000 + 0 } \\ { 119000 - 110000 + 0 } \\ { - 182000 + 220000 + 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 8 } \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 9000 } \\ { 9000 } \\ { 38000 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 1125 } \\ { 1125 } \\ { 4750 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} On comparing the corresponding elements, we get x = 1125, y = 1125, z = 4750. Hence, the amount deposited in each type of account is Rs 1125, Rs 1125 and Rs 4750, respectively.
solution_html:
Let Rs x, Rs y and Rs z be invested in saving accounts at the rate of 5%, 8% and {tex}8 \frac { 1 } { 2 } \%{/tex}, respectively.
Then, according to given condition ,we have the following system of equations
x + y + z = 7000, ...(i)
and {tex}\frac { 5 x } { 100 } + \frac { 8 y } { 100 } + \frac { 17 z } { 200 } = 550{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 10x + 16x + 17z = 110000 ...(ii)
Also, x - y = 0 ...(iii)
This system of equations can be written in matrix from as AX = B
where, A ={tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 1 } & { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 10 } & { 16 } & { 17 } \\ { 1 } & { - 1 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right] , X = \left[ \begin{array} { c } { x } \\ { y } \\ { z } \end{array} \right] \text { and } B = \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 7000 } \\ { 110000 } \\ { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Here, |A| = {tex}\left| \begin{array} { c c c } { 1 } & { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 10 } & { 16 } & { 17 } \\ { 1 } & { - 1 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right|{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}| A | = 1 ( 0 + 17 ) - 1 ( 0 - 17 ) + 1 ( - 10 - 16 ){/tex}
= 17 +17 - 26 = 8 {tex}\neq{/tex} 0
So, A is non- singular matrix and its inverse exists.
Now, cofactors of elements of |A| are,
{tex}A _ { 11 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 2 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 16 } & { 17 } \\ { - 1 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( 0 + 17 ) = 17{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 12 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 3 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 10 } & { 17 } \\ { 1 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right] = - 1 ( 0 - 17 ) = 17{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 13 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 4 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 10 } & { 16 } \\ { 1 } & { - 1 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( - 10 - 16 ) = - 26{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 21 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 3 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { - 1 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( 0 + 1 ) = - 1{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 22 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 4 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 1 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( 0 - 1 ) = - 1{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 23 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 5 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 1 } & { - 1 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( - 1 - 1 ) = 2{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 31 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 4 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 16 } & { 17 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( 17 - 16 ) = 1{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 32 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 5 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 10 } & { 17 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( 17 - 10 ) = - 7{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 33 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 6 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 10 } & { 16 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( 16 - 10 ) = 6{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \operatorname { adj } ( A ) = \left[ \begin{array} { l l l } { A _ { 11 } } & { A _ { 12 } } & { A _ { 13 } } \\ { A _ { 21 } } & { A _ { 22 } } & { A _ { 23 } } \\ { A _ { 31 } } & { A _ { 32 } } & { A _ { 33 } } \end{array} \right] ^ { T }{/tex}
{tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 17 } & { 17 } & { - 26 } \\ { - 1 } & { - 1 } & { 2 } \\ { 1 } & { - 7 } & { 6 } \end{array} \right] ^ { T } = \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 17 } & { - 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 17 } & { - 1 } & { - 7 } \\ { - 26 } & { 2 } & { 6 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Now, A-1 = {tex} \frac { \operatorname { adj } ( A ) } { | A | } = \frac { 1 } { 8 } \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 17 } & { - 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 17 } & { - 1 } & { - 7 } \\ { - 26 } & { 2 } & { 6 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
and the solution of given system is given by
X = A-1 B.
{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { l } { x } \\ { y } \\ { z } \end{array} \right] = \frac { 1 } { 8 } \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 17 } & { - 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 17 } & { - 1 } & { - 7 } \\ { - 26 } & { 2 } & { 6 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 7000 } \\ { 110000 } \\ { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 1 } { 8 } \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 119000 - 110000 + 0 } \\ { 119000 - 110000 + 0 } \\ { - 182000 + 220000 + 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \frac { 1 } { 8 } \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 9000 } \\ { 9000 } \\ { 38000 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 1125 } \\ { 1125 } \\ { 4750 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
On comparing the corresponding elements, we get x = 1125, y = 1125, z = 4750.
Hence, the amount deposited in each type of account is Rs 1125, Rs 1125 and Rs 4750, respectively.

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
Two schools P and Q want to award their selected students on the values of Tolerance, Kindness, and Leadership. The school P wants to award Rs x each, Rs y each and Rs z each for the three respective values to 3, 2 and 1 students respectively with total award money of Rs2200.
School Q wants to spend Rs 3100 to award its 4, 1 and 3 students on the respective values (by giving the same award money to the three values as school P). If the total amount of award for one prize on each value is Rs1200, using matrices, find the award money for each value.
question_text: Two schools P and Q want to award their selected students on the values of Tolerance, Kindness, and Leadership. The school P wants to award Rs x each, Rs y each and Rs z each for the three respective values to 3, 2 and 1 students respectively with total award money of Rs2200. School Q wants to spend Rs 3100 to award its 4, 1 and 3 students on the respective values (by giving the same award money to the three values as school P). If the total amount of award for one prize on each value is Rs1200, using matrices, find the award money for each value.
explanation: Three equations are formed from the given statements: 3x + 2y + z = 2200 4x + y + 3z = 3100 and x + y + z = 1200 Converting the system of equations in matrix form we get, {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{lll}{3} & {2} & {1} \\ {4} & {1} & {3} \\ {1} & {1} & {1}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{l}{x} \\ {y} \\ {z}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{l}{2200} \\ {3100} \\ {1200}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} i.e. AX = B where A = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{lll}{3} & {2} & {1} \\ {4} & {1} & {3} \\ {1} & {1} & {1}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} , X = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{l}{x} \\ {y} \\ {z}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} and B = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{c}{2200} \\ {3100} \\ {1200}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} X = A -1 B A -1 = {tex}\begin{equation} \frac{1}{|A|} \end{equation}{/tex} (Adj A) |A| = 3(1 − 3) −2(4 − 3) + 1(4 − 1) = −6 −2 + 3 = −5 Adj A = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{ccc}{-2} & {-1} & {5} \\ {-1} & {2} & {-5} \\ {3} & {-1} & {-5}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} A -1 = {tex}\begin{equation} \frac{1}{-5} \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{ccc}{-2} & {-1} & {5} \\ {-1} & {2} & {-5} \\ {3} & {-1} & {-5}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} = {tex}\begin{equation} \frac{1}{5} \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{ccc}{2} & {1} & {-5} \\ {1} & {-2} & {5} \\ {-3} & {1} & {5}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} X = {tex}\begin{equation} \frac{1}{5} \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{ccc}{2} & {1} & {-5} \\ {1} & {-2} & {5} \\ {-3} & {1} & {5}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{c}{2200} \\ {3100} \\ {1200}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} = {tex}\begin{equation} \frac{1}{5} \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{c}{4400+3100-6000} \\ {2200-6200+6000} \\ {-6600+3100+6000}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{c}{300} \\ {400} \\ {500}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 300, y = 400 and z = 500 i.e. The award money for each value are Rs.300 for Tolerance, Rs.400 for Kindness and Rs.500 for Leadership.
solution: Three equations are formed from the given statements: 3x + 2y + z = 2200 4x + y + 3z = 3100 and x + y + z = 1200 Converting the system of equations in matrix form we get, {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{lll}{3} & {2} & {1} \\ {4} & {1} & {3} \\ {1} & {1} & {1}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{l}{x} \\ {y} \\ {z}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{l}{2200} \\ {3100} \\ {1200}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} i.e. AX = B where A = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{lll}{3} & {2} & {1} \\ {4} & {1} & {3} \\ {1} & {1} & {1}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} , X = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{l}{x} \\ {y} \\ {z}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} and B = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{c}{2200} \\ {3100} \\ {1200}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} X = A -1 B A -1 = {tex}\begin{equation} \frac{1}{|A|} \end{equation}{/tex} (Adj A) |A| = 3(1 − 3) −2(4 − 3) + 1(4 − 1) = −6 −2 + 3 = −5 Adj A = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{ccc}{-2} & {-1} & {5} \\ {-1} & {2} & {-5} \\ {3} & {-1} & {-5}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} A -1 = {tex}\begin{equation} \frac{1}{-5} \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{ccc}{-2} & {-1} & {5} \\ {-1} & {2} & {-5} \\ {3} & {-1} & {-5}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} = {tex}\begin{equation} \frac{1}{5} \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{ccc}{2} & {1} & {-5} \\ {1} & {-2} & {5} \\ {-3} & {1} & {5}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} X = {tex}\begin{equation} \frac{1}{5} \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{ccc}{2} & {1} & {-5} \\ {1} & {-2} & {5} \\ {-3} & {1} & {5}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{c}{2200} \\ {3100} \\ {1200}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} = {tex}\begin{equation} \frac{1}{5} \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{c}{4400+3100-6000} \\ {2200-6200+6000} \\ {-6600+3100+6000}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{c}{300} \\ {400} \\ {500}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 300, y = 400 and z = 500 i.e. The award money for each value are Rs.300 for Tolerance, Rs.400 for Kindness and Rs.500 for Leadership.
solution_html:
Three equations are formed from the given statements: 
3x + 2y + z = 2200
4x + y + 3z = 3100 and
x + y + z = 1200
Converting the system of equations in matrix form we get,
{tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{lll}{3} & {2} & {1} \\ {4} & {1} & {3} \\ {1} & {1} & {1}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{l}{x} \\ {y} \\ {z}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex}={tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{l}{2200} \\ {3100} \\ {1200}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex}
i.e. AX = B
where A = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{lll}{3} & {2} & {1} \\ {4} & {1} & {3} \\ {1} & {1} & {1}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex}, X = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{l}{x} \\ {y} \\ {z}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} and B = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{c}{2200} \\ {3100} \\ {1200}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} X = A-1 B
A-1 = {tex}\begin{equation} \frac{1}{|A|} \end{equation}{/tex} (Adj A)
|A| = 3(1 − 3) −2(4 − 3) + 1(4 − 1) = −6 −2 + 3 = −5
Adj A = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{ccc}{-2} & {-1} & {5} \\ {-1} & {2} & {-5} \\ {3} & {-1} & {-5}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} A-1 = {tex}\begin{equation} \frac{1}{-5} \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{ccc}{-2} & {-1} & {5} \\ {-1} & {2} & {-5} \\ {3} & {-1} & {-5}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex}
= {tex}\begin{equation} \frac{1}{5} \end{equation}{/tex}{tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{ccc}{2} & {1} & {-5} \\ {1} & {-2} & {5} \\ {-3} & {1} & {5}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} X = {tex}\begin{equation} \frac{1}{5} \end{equation}{/tex}{tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{ccc}{2} & {1} & {-5} \\ {1} & {-2} & {5} \\ {-3} & {1} & {5}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex}{tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{c}{2200} \\ {3100} \\ {1200}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex}
= {tex}\begin{equation} \frac{1}{5} \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{c}{4400+3100-6000} \\ {2200-6200+6000} \\ {-6600+3100+6000}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex}
= {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{c}{300} \\ {400} \\ {500}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 300, y = 400 and z = 500
i.e. The award money for each value are Rs.300 for Tolerance, Rs.400 for Kindness and Rs.500 for Leadership.

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Two schools A and B want to award their selected students on the values of sincerity, truthfulness and helpfulness. The school A wants to award ₹ x each ₹ y each and ₹ z each for the three respective values to 3, 2 and 1 students respectively with total award money of ₹ 16,00. School B wants to spend ₹ 2,300 to award its 4 ,1 and 3 students on the respective values (by giving the same award money to the three values as before). If the total amount of award for one prize on each value is ₹ 900, using matrices, find the award money for each value. Apart from these three values, suggest one more value which should be considered for awards.
question_text: Two schools A and B want to award their selected students on the values of sincerity, truthfulness and helpfulness. The school A wants to award ₹ x each ₹ y each and ₹ z each for the three respective values to 3, 2 and 1 students respectively with total award money of ₹ 16,00. School B wants to spend ₹ 2,300 to award its 4 ,1 and 3 students on the respective values (by giving the same award money to the three values as before). If the total amount of award for one prize on each value is ₹ 900, using matrices, find the award money for each value. Apart from these three values, suggest one more value which should be considered for awards.
explanation: Let x, y, z be the prize amount per person for sincerity, truthfulness and helpfulness respectively According to question, 3x + 2y + z = 1600 4x + y + 3z = 2300 x + y + z = 900 These three equations can be written as {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 3 & 2 & 1 \\ 4 & 1 & 3 \\ 1 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 1600 \\ 2300 \\ 900 \end{array}\right]{/tex} A X = B |A| = 3(1 – 3) – 2(4 – 3) + 1(4 – 1) = 3(– 2) – 2(1) + 1(3) = – 6 – 2 + 3 = – 5 Hence, the unique solution given by x = A –1 B C 11 = (– 1) 1 + 1 (1 – 3) = – 2 C 12 = (– 1) 1 + 2 (4 – 3) = – 1 C 13 = (– 1) 1 + 3 (4 – 1) = 3 C 21 = (– 1) 2 + 1 (2 – 1) = – 1 C 22 = (– 1) 2 + 2 (3 – 1) = 2 C 23 = (– 1) 2 + 3 (3 – 2 ) = – 1 C 31 = (– 1) 3 + 1 (6 – 1) = 5 C 32 = (– 1) 3 + 2 (9 – 4) = – 5 C 33 = (– 1) 3 + 3 (3 – 8) = – 5 {tex}\text { Adj } \mathrm{A}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -1 & 3 \\ -1 & 2 & -1 \\ 5 & -5 & -5 \end{array}\right]^{\mathrm{T}}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -1 & 5 \\ -1 & 2 & -5 \\ 3 & -1 & -5 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}X=A^{-1} B=\frac{1}{|A|}(\text { adj } A) B{/tex} {tex}X=\frac{1}{-5}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -1 & 5 \\ -1 & 2 & -5 \\ 3 & -1 & -5 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 1600 \\ 2300 \\ 900 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -1 & 5 \\ -1 & 2 & -5 \\ 3 & -1 & -5 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 1600 \\ 2300 \\ 900 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{c} 640+460-900 \\ -320-920+900 \\ -960+460+900 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} \mathrm{x} \\ \mathrm{y} \\ \mathrm{z} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 200 \\ 300 \\ 400 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Hence, x = 200, y = 300 and z = 400 Excellence in extracurricular activities should be another value considered for an award
solution: Let x, y, z be the prize amount per person for sincerity, truthfulness and helpfulness respectively According to question, 3x + 2y + z = 1600 4x + y + 3z = 2300 x + y + z = 900 These three equations can be written as {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 3 & 2 & 1 \\ 4 & 1 & 3 \\ 1 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 1600 \\ 2300 \\ 900 \end{array}\right]{/tex} A X = B |A| = 3(1 – 3) – 2(4 – 3) + 1(4 – 1) = 3(– 2) – 2(1) + 1(3) = – 6 – 2 + 3 = – 5 Hence, the unique solution given by x = A –1 B C 11 = (– 1) 1 + 1 (1 – 3) = – 2 C 12 = (– 1) 1 + 2 (4 – 3) = – 1 C 13 = (– 1) 1 + 3 (4 – 1) = 3 C 21 = (– 1) 2 + 1 (2 – 1) = – 1 C 22 = (– 1) 2 + 2 (3 – 1) = 2 C 23 = (– 1) 2 + 3 (3 – 2 ) = – 1 C 31 = (– 1) 3 + 1 (6 – 1) = 5 C 32 = (– 1) 3 + 2 (9 – 4) = – 5 C 33 = (– 1) 3 + 3 (3 – 8) = – 5 {tex}\text { Adj } \mathrm{A}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -1 & 3 \\ -1 & 2 & -1 \\ 5 & -5 & -5 \end{array}\right]^{\mathrm{T}}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -1 & 5 \\ -1 & 2 & -5 \\ 3 & -1 & -5 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}X=A^{-1} B=\frac{1}{|A|}(\text { adj } A) B{/tex} {tex}X=\frac{1}{-5}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -1 & 5 \\ -1 & 2 & -5 \\ 3 & -1 & -5 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 1600 \\ 2300 \\ 900 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -1 & 5 \\ -1 & 2 & -5 \\ 3 & -1 & -5 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 1600 \\ 2300 \\ 900 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{c} 640+460-900 \\ -320-920+900 \\ -960+460+900 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} \mathrm{x} \\ \mathrm{y} \\ \mathrm{z} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 200 \\ 300 \\ 400 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Hence, x = 200, y = 300 and z = 400 Excellence in extracurricular activities should be another value considered for an award
solution_html:
Let x, y, z be the prize amount per person for sincerity, truthfulness and helpfulness respectively
According to question,
3x + 2y + z = 1600
4x + y + 3z = 2300
x + y + z = 900
These three equations can be written as
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 3 & 2 & 1 \\ 4 & 1 & 3 \\ 1 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 1600 \\ 2300 \\ 900 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
A X = B
|A| = 3(1 – 3) – 2(4 – 3) + 1(4 – 1)
= 3(– 2) – 2(1) + 1(3)
= – 6 – 2 + 3
= – 5
Hence, the unique solution given by x = A–1B
C11 = (– 1)1 + 1 (1 – 3) = – 2
C12 = (– 1)1 + 2 (4 – 3) = – 1
C13 = (– 1)1 + 3 (4 – 1) = 3
C21 = (– 1)2 + 1 (2 – 1) = – 1
C22 = (– 1)2 + 2 (3 – 1) = 2
C23 = (– 1)2 + 3 (3 – 2 ) = – 1
C31 = (– 1)3 + 1 (6 – 1) = 5
C32 = (– 1)3 + 2 (9 – 4) = – 5
C33 = (– 1)3 + 3 (3 – 8) = – 5
{tex}\text { Adj } \mathrm{A}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -1 & 3 \\ -1 & 2 & -1 \\ 5 & -5 & -5 \end{array}\right]^{\mathrm{T}}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -1 & 5 \\ -1 & 2 & -5 \\ 3 & -1 & -5 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}X=A^{-1} B=\frac{1}{|A|}(\text { adj } A) B{/tex}
{tex}X=\frac{1}{-5}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -1 & 5 \\ -1 & 2 & -5 \\ 3 & -1 & -5 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 1600 \\ 2300 \\ 900 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -1 & 5 \\ -1 & 2 & -5 \\ 3 & -1 & -5 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 1600 \\ 2300 \\ 900 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{c} 640+460-900 \\ -320-920+900 \\ -960+460+900 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} \mathrm{x} \\ \mathrm{y} \\ \mathrm{z} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 200 \\ 300 \\ 400 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Hence, x = 200, y = 300 and z = 400

question_html: Two schools P and Q want to award their selected students on the values of Discipline, Politeness and Punctuality. The school P wants to award ₹ x each, ₹ y each and ₹ z each for the three respectively values to its 3, 2 and 1 students with total award money of ₹ 1,000. School Q wants to spend ₹ 1,500 to award its 4, 1 and 3 students on the respective values (by giving the same award money for three values as before). If the total amount of awards for one prize on each value is ₹ 600, using matrices, find the award money for each value. Apart from the above three values, suggest one more value for awards.
question_text: Two schools P and Q want to award their selected students on the values of Discipline, Politeness and Punctuality. The school P wants to award ₹ x each, ₹ y each and ₹ z each for the three respectively values to its 3, 2 and 1 students with total award money of ₹ 1,000. School Q wants to spend ₹ 1,500 to award its 4, 1 and 3 students on the respective values (by giving the same award money for three values as before). If the total amount of awards for one prize on each value is ₹ 600, using matrices, find the award money for each value. Apart from the above three values, suggest one more value for awards.
explanation: Let x,y and z be the prize amount per student for Discipline, Politeness and Punctuality respectively. 3x + 2y + z = 1000 4x + y + 3z = 1500 x + y + z = 600 These three equations can be written as {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 3 & 2 & 1 \\ 4 & 1 & 3 \\ 1 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 1000 \\ 1500 \\ 600 \end{array}\right]{/tex} A X = B |A| = 3(1 – 3) – 2(4 – 3) + 1(4 – 1) = 3(– 2) – 2(1) + 1(3) = – 6 – 2 + 3 = – 5 Hence, the unique solution given by x = A – 1 B C 11 = (– 1) 1 + 1 (1 – 3) = – 2 C 12 = (– 1) 1 + 2 (4 – 3) = – 1 C 13 = (– 1) 1 + 3 (4 – 1) = 3 C 21 = (– 1) 2 + 1 (2 – 1) = – 1 C 22 = (– 1) 2 + 2 (3 – 1) = 2 C 23 = (– 1) 2 + 3 (3 – 2 ) = – 1 C 31 = (– 1) 3 + 1 (6 – 1) = 5 C 32 = (– 1) 3 + 2 (9 – 4) = – 5 C 33 = (– 1) 3 + 3 (3 – 8) = – 5 {tex}\text { Adj } A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -1 & 3 \\ -1 & 2 & -1 \\ 5 & -5 & -5 \end{array}\right]^{T}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -1 & 5 \\ -1 & 2 & -5 \\ 3 & -1 & -5 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}x=A^{-1} B=\frac{1}{|A|}(\operatorname{adj} A) B{/tex} {tex}X=\frac{1}{-5}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -1 & 5 \\ -1 & 2 & -5 \\ 3 & -1 & -5 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 1000 \\ 1500 \\ 600 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -1 & 5 \\ -1 & 2 & -5 \\ 3 & -1 & -5 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 1600 \\ 2300 \\ 900 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{c} 640+460-900 \\ -320-920+900 \\ -960+460+900 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} \mathrm{x} \\ \mathrm{y} \\ \mathrm{z} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 100 \\ 200 \\ 300 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Hence, x = 100, y = 200 and z = 300
solution: Let x,y and z be the prize amount per student for Discipline, Politeness and Punctuality respectively. 3x + 2y + z = 1000 4x + y + 3z = 1500 x + y + z = 600 These three equations can be written as {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 3 & 2 & 1 \\ 4 & 1 & 3 \\ 1 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 1000 \\ 1500 \\ 600 \end{array}\right]{/tex} A X = B |A| = 3(1 – 3) – 2(4 – 3) + 1(4 – 1) = 3(– 2) – 2(1) + 1(3) = – 6 – 2 + 3 = – 5 Hence, the unique solution given by x = A – 1 B C 11 = (– 1) 1 + 1 (1 – 3) = – 2 C 12 = (– 1) 1 + 2 (4 – 3) = – 1 C 13 = (– 1) 1 + 3 (4 – 1) = 3 C 21 = (– 1) 2 + 1 (2 – 1) = – 1 C 22 = (– 1) 2 + 2 (3 – 1) = 2 C 23 = (– 1) 2 + 3 (3 – 2 ) = – 1 C 31 = (– 1) 3 + 1 (6 – 1) = 5 C 32 = (– 1) 3 + 2 (9 – 4) = – 5 C 33 = (– 1) 3 + 3 (3 – 8) = – 5 {tex}\text { Adj } A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -1 & 3 \\ -1 & 2 & -1 \\ 5 & -5 & -5 \end{array}\right]^{T}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -1 & 5 \\ -1 & 2 & -5 \\ 3 & -1 & -5 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}x=A^{-1} B=\frac{1}{|A|}(\operatorname{adj} A) B{/tex} {tex}X=\frac{1}{-5}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -1 & 5 \\ -1 & 2 & -5 \\ 3 & -1 & -5 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 1000 \\ 1500 \\ 600 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -1 & 5 \\ -1 & 2 & -5 \\ 3 & -1 & -5 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 1600 \\ 2300 \\ 900 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{c} 640+460-900 \\ -320-920+900 \\ -960+460+900 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} \mathrm{x} \\ \mathrm{y} \\ \mathrm{z} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 100 \\ 200 \\ 300 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Hence, x = 100, y = 200 and z = 300
solution_html:
Let x,y and z be the prize amount per student for Discipline, Politeness and Punctuality respectively.
3x + 2y + z = 1000
4x + y + 3z = 1500
x + y + z = 600
These three equations can be written as
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 3 & 2 & 1 \\ 4 & 1 & 3 \\ 1 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 1000 \\ 1500 \\ 600 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
A X = B
|A| = 3(1 – 3) – 2(4 – 3) + 1(4 – 1)
= 3(– 2) – 2(1) + 1(3)
= – 6 – 2 + 3
= – 5
Hence, the unique solution given by x = A – 1B
C11 = (– 1)1 + 1 (1 – 3) = – 2
C12 = (– 1)1 + 2 (4 – 3) = – 1
C13 = (– 1)1 + 3 (4 – 1) = 3
C21 = (– 1)2 + 1 (2 – 1) = – 1
C22 = (– 1)2 + 2 (3 – 1) = 2
C23 = (– 1)2 + 3 (3 – 2 ) = – 1
C31 = (– 1)3 + 1 (6 – 1) = 5
C32 = (– 1)3 + 2 (9 – 4) = – 5
C33 = (– 1)3 + 3 (3 – 8) = – 5
{tex}\text { Adj } A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -1 & 3 \\ -1 & 2 & -1 \\ 5 & -5 & -5 \end{array}\right]^{T}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -1 & 5 \\ -1 & 2 & -5 \\ 3 & -1 & -5 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}x=A^{-1} B=\frac{1}{|A|}(\operatorname{adj} A) B{/tex}
{tex}X=\frac{1}{-5}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -1 & 5 \\ -1 & 2 & -5 \\ 3 & -1 & -5 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 1000 \\ 1500 \\ 600 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & -1 & 5 \\ -1 & 2 & -5 \\ 3 & -1 & -5 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 1600 \\ 2300 \\ 900 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{c} 640+460-900 \\ -320-920+900 \\ -960+460+900 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} \mathrm{x} \\ \mathrm{y} \\ \mathrm{z} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 100 \\ 200 \\ 300 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Hence, x = 100, y = 200 and z = 300

question_html: If {tex}\left| \begin{array} { c c } { 2 x } & { x + 3 } \\ { 2 ( x + 1 ) } & { x + 1 } \end{array} \right| = \left| \begin{array} { l l } { 1 } & { 5 } \\ { 3 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right|{/tex}, then find the value of x.
question_text: If {tex}\left| \begin{array} { c c } { 2 x } & { x + 3 } \\ { 2 ( x + 1 ) } & { x + 1 } \end{array} \right| = \left| \begin{array} { l l } { 1 } & { 5 } \\ { 3 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right|{/tex} , then find the value of x.
explanation: Given, {tex}\left| \begin{array} { c c } { 2 x } & { x + 3 } \\ { 2 ( x + 1 ) } & { x + 1 } \end{array} \right| = \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 5 } \\ { 3 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right|{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2 x ( x + 1 ) - ( x + 3 ) ( 2 x + 2 ) = 3 - 15{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad 2 x ^2 { } + 2 x - \left( 2 x ^2{ } + 8 x + 6 \right) = - 12{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}- 6 x - 6 = - 12 \Rightarrow 6 x = 6{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}x{/tex} = 1
solution: Given, {tex}\left| \begin{array} { c c } { 2 x } & { x + 3 } \\ { 2 ( x + 1 ) } & { x + 1 } \end{array} \right| = \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 5 } \\ { 3 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right|{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow 2 x ( x + 1 ) - ( x + 3 ) ( 2 x + 2 ) = 3 - 15{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \quad 2 x ^2 { } + 2 x - \left( 2 x ^2{ } + 8 x + 6 \right) = - 12{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}- 6 x - 6 = - 12 \Rightarrow 6 x = 6{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}x{/tex} = 1
solution_html:
Given, {tex}\left| \begin{array} { c c } { 2 x } & { x + 3 } \\ { 2 ( x + 1 ) } & { x + 1 } \end{array} \right| = \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 5 } \\ { 3 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right|{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow 2 x ( x + 1 ) - ( x + 3 ) ( 2 x + 2 ) = 3 - 15{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad 2 x ^2 { } + 2 x - \left( 2 x ^2{ } + 8 x + 6 \right) = - 12{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}- 6 x - 6 = - 12 \Rightarrow 6 x = 6{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} {tex}x{/tex} = 1

question_text: If A {tex}_{ij}{/tex} is the cofactor of the element a {tex}_{ij}{/tex} of the determinant {tex}\left| \begin{array} { c c c } { 2 } & { - 3 } & { 5 } \\ { 6 } & { 0 } & { 4 } \\ { 1 } & { 5 } & { - 7 } \end{array} \right|{/tex} , then write the value of a 32 .A 32 .
explanation: Let {tex}\Delta = \left| \begin{array} { c c c } { 2 } & { - 3 } & { 5 } \\ { 6 } & { 0 } & { 4 } \\ { 1 } & { 5 } & { - 7 } \end{array} \right|{/tex} Here, a 32 = 5 Given, A ij is the cofactor of the element a ij of A. {tex} \therefore \quad A _ { 32 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 3+ 2 } \left| \begin{array} { l l } { 2 } & { 5 } \\ { 6 } & { 4 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( 8 - 30 ) = 22{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow a _ { 32 } A _ { 32 } = 5 \times 22 = 110{/tex}

question_html: If A is a square matrix of order 3 such that | adj A|= 64, then find |A|.
question_text: If A is a square matrix of order 3 such that | adj A|= 64, then find |A|.
explanation: We know that, for a square matrix of order n, |adj (A) |= |A| n-1 Here, the order of A is 3 {tex}\times {/tex} 3 ,therefore n = 3 Now, |adj (A) | =|A| {tex}^{3-1}{/tex} = |A| 2 Given, |adj (A)|= 64, Therefore,64 =|A| 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (8) 2 =|A| 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |A|= ± 8 [taking square root]
solution: We know that, for a square matrix of order n, |adj (A) |= |A| n-1 Here, the order of A is 3 {tex}\times {/tex} 3 ,therefore n = 3 Now, |adj (A) | =|A| {tex}^{3-1}{/tex} = |A| 2 Given, |adj (A)|= 64, Therefore,64 =|A| 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (8) 2 =|A| 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |A|= ± 8 [taking square root]
solution_html:
We know that, for a square matrix of order n, 
|adj (A) |= |A|n-1 
Here, the order of A is 3{tex}\times {/tex}3 ,therefore n = 3
Now, |adj (A) | =|A|{tex}^{3-1}{/tex} = |A|2
Given, |adj (A)|= 64,

Therefore,64 =|A|2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (8)2 =|A|2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |A|= ± 8  [taking square root]

question_html: Two institutions decided to award their employees for the three values of resourcefulness, competence and determination in the form of prizes at the rate of Rs.x, Rs.y, and Rs.z, respectively per person. The first institution decided to award respectively 4, 3 and 2 employees with total prize money of Rs.37000 and the second institution decided to award respectively 5, 3 and 4 employees with total prize money of, Rs.47000. If all the three prizes per person together amount to Rs.12000, then using a matrix method, find the values of x, y, and z. What values are described in this question?
question_text: Two institutions decided to award their employees for the three values of resourcefulness, competence and determination in the form of prizes at the rate of Rs.x, Rs.y, and Rs.z, respectively per person. The first institution decided to award respectively 4, 3 and 2 employees with total prize money of Rs.37000 and the second institution decided to award respectively 5, 3 and 4 employees with total prize money of, Rs.47000. If all the three prizes per person together amount to Rs.12000, then using a matrix method, find the values of x, y, and z. What values are described in this question?
explanation: Let the numbers are x, y, z be the cash awards for Resourcefulness, Competence, and Determination respectively 4x + 3y + 2z = 37000 …… (i) Also, 5x + 3y + 4z = 47000 …… (ii) Again, x + y + z = 12000 …… (iii) {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{lll}{4} & {3} & {2} \\ {5} & {3} & {4} \\ {1} & {1} & {1}\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l}{x} \\ {y} \\ {z}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{c}{37000} \\ {47000} \\ {12000}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} A X = B |A| = 4(3 - 4) - 3(5 - 4) + 2(5 - 3) = 4(-1) - 3(1) + 2(2) = -4 - 3 + 4 = -3 Hence, the unique solution given by X = A -1 B C 11 = (-1) 1+1 (3 - 4) = -1 C 12 = (-1) 1+2 (5 - 4) = -1 C 13 = (-1) 1+3 (5 - 3) = 2 C 21 = (-1) 2+1 (3 - 2) = -1 C 22 = (-1) 2+2 (4 - 2) = 2 C 23 = (-1) 2+3 (4 - 3 ) = -1 C 31 = (-1) 3+1 (12 - 6) = 6 C 32 = (-1) 3+2 (16 - 10) = -6 C 33 = (-1) 3+3 (12 - 15) = -3 Adj A = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{ccc}{-1} & {-1} & {2} \\ {-1} & {2} & {-1} \\ {6} & {-6} & {-3}\end{array}\right]^{\mathrm{T}} \end{equation}{/tex} = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{ccc}{-1} & {-1} & {6} \\ {-1} & {2} & {-6} \\ {2} & {-1} & {-3}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} X = A –1 B = {tex}\begin{equation} \frac{1}{|A|} \end{equation}{/tex} (Adj A) B X = {tex}\begin{equation} \frac{1}{-3} \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{ccc}{-1} & {-1} & {6} \\ {-1} & {2} & {-6} \\ {2} & {-1} & {-3}\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}{37000} \\ {47000} \\ {12000}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} X = {tex}\begin{equation} \frac{1}{-3} \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{c}{-37000-47000+72000} \\ {-37000+94000-72000} \\ {74000-47000-36000}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{l}{\mathrm{x}} \\ {\mathrm{y}} \\ {\mathrm{z}}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{l}{4000} \\ {5000} \\ {3000}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} Hence, x = 4000, y = 5000 and z = 3000 Thus, the value x, y, z describes the number of prizes per person for Resourcefulness, Competence, and Determination.
solution: Let the numbers are x, y, z be the cash awards for Resourcefulness, Competence, and Determination respectively 4x + 3y + 2z = 37000 …… (i) Also, 5x + 3y + 4z = 47000 …… (ii) Again, x + y + z = 12000 …… (iii) {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{lll}{4} & {3} & {2} \\ {5} & {3} & {4} \\ {1} & {1} & {1}\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l}{x} \\ {y} \\ {z}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{c}{37000} \\ {47000} \\ {12000}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} A X = B |A| = 4(3 - 4) - 3(5 - 4) + 2(5 - 3) = 4(-1) - 3(1) + 2(2) = -4 - 3 + 4 = -3 Hence, the unique solution given by X = A -1 B C 11 = (-1) 1+1 (3 - 4) = -1 C 12 = (-1) 1+2 (5 - 4) = -1 C 13 = (-1) 1+3 (5 - 3) = 2 C 21 = (-1) 2+1 (3 - 2) = -1 C 22 = (-1) 2+2 (4 - 2) = 2 C 23 = (-1) 2+3 (4 - 3 ) = -1 C 31 = (-1) 3+1 (12 - 6) = 6 C 32 = (-1) 3+2 (16 - 10) = -6 C 33 = (-1) 3+3 (12 - 15) = -3 Adj A = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{ccc}{-1} & {-1} & {2} \\ {-1} & {2} & {-1} \\ {6} & {-6} & {-3}\end{array}\right]^{\mathrm{T}} \end{equation}{/tex} = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{ccc}{-1} & {-1} & {6} \\ {-1} & {2} & {-6} \\ {2} & {-1} & {-3}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} X = A –1 B = {tex}\begin{equation} \frac{1}{|A|} \end{equation}{/tex} (Adj A) B X = {tex}\begin{equation} \frac{1}{-3} \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{ccc}{-1} & {-1} & {6} \\ {-1} & {2} & {-6} \\ {2} & {-1} & {-3}\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}{37000} \\ {47000} \\ {12000}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} X = {tex}\begin{equation} \frac{1}{-3} \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{c}{-37000-47000+72000} \\ {-37000+94000-72000} \\ {74000-47000-36000}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{l}{\mathrm{x}} \\ {\mathrm{y}} \\ {\mathrm{z}}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{l}{4000} \\ {5000} \\ {3000}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex} Hence, x = 4000, y = 5000 and z = 3000 Thus, the value x, y, z describes the number of prizes per person for Resourcefulness, Competence, and Determination.

Hence, the unique solution given by X = A-1B

C32 = (-1)3+2 (16 - 10) = -6
C33 = (-1)3+3 (12 - 15) = -3
Adj A = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{ccc}{-1} & {-1} & {2} \\ {-1} & {2} & {-1} \\ {6} & {-6} & {-3}\end{array}\right]^{\mathrm{T}} \end{equation}{/tex} = {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{ccc}{-1} & {-1} & {6} \\ {-1} & {2} & {-6} \\ {2} & {-1} & {-3}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex}
X = A–1 B = {tex}\begin{equation} \frac{1}{|A|} \end{equation}{/tex} (Adj A) B
X = {tex}\begin{equation} \frac{1}{-3} \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{ccc}{-1} & {-1} & {6} \\ {-1} & {2} & {-6} \\ {2} & {-1} & {-3}\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}{37000} \\ {47000} \\ {12000}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex}
X = {tex}\begin{equation} \frac{1}{-3} \end{equation}{/tex} {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{c}{-37000-47000+72000} \\ {-37000+94000-72000} \\ {74000-47000-36000}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex}
{tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{l}{\mathrm{x}} \\ {\mathrm{y}} \\ {\mathrm{z}}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex}= {tex}\begin{equation} \left[\begin{array}{l}{4000} \\ {5000} \\ {3000}\end{array}\right] \end{equation}{/tex}
Hence, x = 4000, y = 5000 and z = 3000
Thus, the value x, y, z describes the number of prizes per person for Resourcefulness, Competence, and Determination.

question_text: Find the equation of the line joining A (1, 3) and B (0, 0) using determinants and find k if D (k, 0) is a point such that area of {tex}\Delta ABD{/tex} is 3 square units.

question_html: Find the values of x, if {tex}\left|\begin{array}{cc} {x+1} & {x-1} \\ {x-3} & {x+2} \end{array}\right|=\left|\begin{array}{cc} {4} & {-1} \\ {1} & {3} \end{array}\right|{/tex}
question_text: Find the values of x, if {tex}\left|\begin{array}{cc} {x+1} & {x-1} \\ {x-3} & {x+2} \end{array}\right|=\left|\begin{array}{cc} {4} & {-1} \\ {1} & {3} \end{array}\right|{/tex}
explanation: We have {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ll} {x+1} & {x-1} \\ {x-3} & {x+2} \end{array}\right|=\left|\begin{array}{cc} {4} & {-1} \\ {1} & {3} \end{array}\right|{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x + 1)(x + 2) - (x - 1)(x - 3) = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 - 1 {tex}\times{/tex} (-1) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x 2 + 2x + x + 2) - (x 2 - 3x - x + 3) = 12 + 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -2x -1 = 13 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -2x = 14 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = -7
solution: We have {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ll} {x+1} & {x-1} \\ {x-3} & {x+2} \end{array}\right|=\left|\begin{array}{cc} {4} & {-1} \\ {1} & {3} \end{array}\right|{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x + 1)(x + 2) - (x - 1)(x - 3) = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 - 1 {tex}\times{/tex} (-1) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x 2 + 2x + x + 2) - (x 2 - 3x - x + 3) = 12 + 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -2x -1 = 13 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -2x = 14 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = -7
solution_html:
We have {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ll} {x+1} & {x-1} \\ {x-3} & {x+2} \end{array}\right|=\left|\begin{array}{cc} {4} & {-1} \\ {1} & {3} \end{array}\right|{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x + 1)(x + 2) - (x - 1)(x - 3) = 4 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 - 1 {tex}\times{/tex} (-1)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x2 + 2x + x + 2) - (x2 - 3x - x + 3) = 12 + 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -2x -1 = 13
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} -2x = 14
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = -7
