question_html: The management committee of a residential colony decided to award some of its members (say x) for honesty, some (say y) for helping others (say z) for supervising the workers to keep die colony neat and clean. The sum of all the awardees is 12. Three times the sum of awardees for cooperation and supervision added to two times the number of awardees for honesty is 33. If the sum of the number of awardees for honesty and supervision is twice the number of awardees for helping others, using the matrix method, find the number of awardees of each category. Apart from these values, namely, honesty, cooperation and supervision, suggest one more value which the management must include for awards.
question_text: The management committee of a residential colony decided to award some of its members (say x) for honesty, some (say y) for helping others (say z) for supervising the workers to keep die colony neat and clean. The sum of all the awardees is 12. Three times the sum of awardees for cooperation and supervision added to two times the number of awardees for honesty is 33. If the sum of the number of awardees for honesty and supervision is twice the number of awardees for helping others, using the matrix method, find the number of awardees of each category. Apart from these values, namely, honesty, cooperation and supervision, suggest one more value which the management must include for awards.
explanation: Let the numbers are x, y, z, then according to question, we have 3x + 5y – 4 z = 6000 …(i) Also, 2x – 3y + z = 5000 …(ii) Moreover, – x + 4y + 6z = 13000 …(iii) Writing equations (i) ,(ii) and (iii) in the matrix form we have. {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 2 & 3 & 3 \\ 1 & -2 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 12 \\ 33 \\ 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} AX = B |A| = 1(3 + 6) – 1(2 – 3) + 1(– 4 – 3) = 1(9) – 1(– 1) – 7 = 9 + 1 – 7 = 3 Hence, the unique solution given by x = A – 1 B C 11 = (– 1) 1 + 1 (3 + 6) = 9 C 12 = (– 1) 1 + 2 (2 – 3) = 1 C 13 = (– 1) 1 + 3 (– 4 – 3) = – 7 C 21 = (– 1) 2 + 1 (1 + 2) = – 3 C 22 = (– 1) 2 + 2 (1 – 1) = 0 C 23 = (– 1) 2 + 3 (– 2 – 1 ) = 3 C 31 = (– 1) 3 + 1 (3 – 3) = 0 C 32 = (– 1) 3 + 2 (3 – 2) = – 1 C 33 = (– 1) 3 + 3 (3 – 2) = 1 {tex}\text { Adj } \mathrm{A}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 9 & 1 & -7 \\ -3 & 0 & 3 \\ 0 & -1 & 1 \end{array}\right]^{\mathrm{T}}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 9 & -3 & 0 \\ 1 & 0 & -1 \\ -7 & 3 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}X=A^{-1} B=\frac{1}{|A|}(\text { adj } A) B{/tex} {tex}X=\frac{1}{3}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 9 & -3 & 0 \\ 1 & 0 & -1 \\ -7 & 3 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 12 \\ 33 \\ 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 9 & -3 & 0 \\ 1 & 0 & -1 \\ -7 & 3 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 4 \\ 11 \\ 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}X=\frac{1}{-151}\left[\begin{array}{c} 36-33+0 \\ 1+0-1 \\ -7+3+1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 3 \\ 4 \\ 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Hence, x = 3, y = 4 and z = 5
solution: Let the numbers are x, y, z, then according to question, we have 3x + 5y – 4 z = 6000 …(i) Also, 2x – 3y + z = 5000 …(ii) Moreover, – x + 4y + 6z = 13000 …(iii) Writing equations (i) ,(ii) and (iii) in the matrix form we have. {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 2 & 3 & 3 \\ 1 & -2 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 12 \\ 33 \\ 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} AX = B |A| = 1(3 + 6) – 1(2 – 3) + 1(– 4 – 3) = 1(9) – 1(– 1) – 7 = 9 + 1 – 7 = 3 Hence, the unique solution given by x = A – 1 B C 11 = (– 1) 1 + 1 (3 + 6) = 9 C 12 = (– 1) 1 + 2 (2 – 3) = 1 C 13 = (– 1) 1 + 3 (– 4 – 3) = – 7 C 21 = (– 1) 2 + 1 (1 + 2) = – 3 C 22 = (– 1) 2 + 2 (1 – 1) = 0 C 23 = (– 1) 2 + 3 (– 2 – 1 ) = 3 C 31 = (– 1) 3 + 1 (3 – 3) = 0 C 32 = (– 1) 3 + 2 (3 – 2) = – 1 C 33 = (– 1) 3 + 3 (3 – 2) = 1 {tex}\text { Adj } \mathrm{A}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 9 & 1 & -7 \\ -3 & 0 & 3 \\ 0 & -1 & 1 \end{array}\right]^{\mathrm{T}}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 9 & -3 & 0 \\ 1 & 0 & -1 \\ -7 & 3 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}X=A^{-1} B=\frac{1}{|A|}(\text { adj } A) B{/tex} {tex}X=\frac{1}{3}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 9 & -3 & 0 \\ 1 & 0 & -1 \\ -7 & 3 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 12 \\ 33 \\ 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 9 & -3 & 0 \\ 1 & 0 & -1 \\ -7 & 3 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 4 \\ 11 \\ 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}X=\frac{1}{-151}\left[\begin{array}{c} 36-33+0 \\ 1+0-1 \\ -7+3+1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 3 \\ 4 \\ 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Hence, x = 3, y = 4 and z = 5
solution_html:
Let the numbers are x, y, z, then according to question, we have
3x + 5y – 4 z = 6000 …(i)
Also,
2x – 3y + z = 5000 …(ii)
Moreover,
– x + 4y + 6z = 13000 …(iii)
Writing equations (i) ,(ii) and (iii)  in the matrix form we have.
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 2 & 3 & 3 \\ 1 & -2 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 12 \\ 33 \\ 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
AX = B
|A| = 1(3 + 6) – 1(2 – 3) + 1(– 4 – 3)
= 1(9) – 1(– 1) – 7
= 9 + 1 – 7
= 3
Hence, the unique solution given by x = A – 1B
C11 = (– 1)1 + 1 (3 + 6) = 9
C12 = (– 1)1 + 2 (2 – 3) = 1
C13 = (– 1)1 + 3 (– 4 – 3) = – 7
C21 = (– 1)2 + 1 (1 + 2) = – 3
C22 = (– 1)2 + 2 (1 – 1) = 0
C23 = (– 1)2 + 3 (– 2 – 1 ) = 3
C31 = (– 1)3 + 1 (3 – 3) = 0
C32 = (– 1)3 + 2 (3 – 2) = – 1
C33 = (– 1)3 + 3 (3 – 2) = 1
{tex}\text { Adj } \mathrm{A}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 9 & 1 & -7 \\ -3 & 0 & 3 \\ 0 & -1 & 1 \end{array}\right]^{\mathrm{T}}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 9 & -3 & 0 \\ 1 & 0 & -1 \\ -7 & 3 & 1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}X=A^{-1} B=\frac{1}{|A|}(\text { adj } A) B{/tex}
{tex}X=\frac{1}{3}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 9 & -3 & 0 \\ 1 & 0 & -1 \\ -7 & 3 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 12 \\ 33 \\ 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 9 & -3 & 0 \\ 1 & 0 & -1 \\ -7 & 3 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 4 \\ 11 \\ 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}X=\frac{1}{-151}\left[\begin{array}{c} 36-33+0 \\ 1+0-1 \\ -7+3+1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 3 \\ 4 \\ 5 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Hence, x = 3, y = 4 and z = 5

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
Two factories decided to award their employees for three values of (a) adaptable to new techniques, (b) careful and alert in difficult situations and (c) keeping calm in tense situations, at the rate of ₹ x, ₹ y and ₹ z per person respectively. The first factory decided to honour respectively 2 ,4 and 3 employees with a total prize money of ₹ 29000. The second factory decided to honour respectively 5, 2 and 3 employees with the prize money of ₹ 30500. If the three prizes per person together cost ₹ 9500, then

  1. represent the above situation by a matrix equation and form linear equations using matrix multiplication.
  2. Solve these equations using matrices.
  3. Which values are reflected in the questions?
question_text: Two factories decided to award their employees for three values of (a) adaptable to new techniques, (b) careful and alert in difficult situations and (c) keeping calm in tense situations, at the rate of ₹ x, ₹ y and ₹ z per person respectively. The first factory decided to honour respectively 2 ,4 and 3 employees with a total prize money of ₹ 29000. The second factory decided to honour respectively 5, 2 and 3 employees with the prize money of ₹ 30500. If the three prizes per person together cost ₹ 9500, then represent the above situation by a matrix equation and form linear equations using matrix multiplication. Solve these equations using matrices. Which values are reflected in the questions?
explanation: Let x, y, z be the prize amount per person for adaptability, carefulness and calmness respectively Accoring to question, 2x + 4y + 3z = 29000 5x + 2y + 3z = 30500 X + y + z = 9500 These three equations can be written as {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 2 & 4 & 3 \\ 5 & 2 & 3 \\ 1 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 29000 \\ 30500 \\ 9500 \end{array}\right]{/tex} A X = B |A| = 2(2 – 3) – 4(5 – 3) + 3(5 – 2) = 2(– 1) – 4(2) + 3(3) = – 2 – 8 + 9 = – 1 Hence, the unique solution given by x = A – 1 B C 11 = (– 1) 1 + 1 (2 – 3) = – 1 C 12 = (– 1) 1 + 2 (5 – 3) = – 2 C 13 = (– 1) 1 + 3 (5 – 2) = 3 C 21 = (– 1) 2 + 1 (4 – 3) = – 1 C 22 = (– 1) 2 + 2 (2 – 3) = – 1 C 23 = (– 1) 2 + 3 (2 – 4 ) = – 2 C 31 = (– 1) 3 + 1 (12 – 6) = 6 C 32 = (– 1) 3 + 2 (6 – 15) = – 9 C 33 = (– 1) 3 + 3 (4 – 20) = – 16 {tex}\text { Adj } A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -1 & -2 & 3 \\ -1 & -1 & 2 \\ 6 & 9 & -16 \end{array}\right]^{T}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -1 & -1 & 6 \\ -2 & -1 & 9 \\ 3 & 2 & -16 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}X=A^{-1} B=\frac{1}{|A|}(\text { adj } A) B{/tex} {tex}X=\frac{1}{-1}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -1 & -1 & 6 \\ -2 & -1 & 9 \\ 3 & 2 & -16 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 29000 \\ 30500 \\ 9500 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 1 & -6 \\ - & 1 & -9 \\ -3 & -2 & 16 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 29000 \\ 30500 \\ 9500 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{c} 29000+30500-57000 \\ 58000+30500-85500 \\ -87000-61000+152000 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} \mathrm{x} \\ \mathrm{y} \\ \mathrm{z} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 2500 \\ 3000 \\ 4000 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Hence, x = 2500, y = 3000 and z = 4000
solution: Let x, y, z be the prize amount per person for adaptability, carefulness and calmness respectively Accoring to question, 2x + 4y + 3z = 29000 5x + 2y + 3z = 30500 X + y + z = 9500 These three equations can be written as {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 2 & 4 & 3 \\ 5 & 2 & 3 \\ 1 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 29000 \\ 30500 \\ 9500 \end{array}\right]{/tex} A X = B |A| = 2(2 – 3) – 4(5 – 3) + 3(5 – 2) = 2(– 1) – 4(2) + 3(3) = – 2 – 8 + 9 = – 1 Hence, the unique solution given by x = A – 1 B C 11 = (– 1) 1 + 1 (2 – 3) = – 1 C 12 = (– 1) 1 + 2 (5 – 3) = – 2 C 13 = (– 1) 1 + 3 (5 – 2) = 3 C 21 = (– 1) 2 + 1 (4 – 3) = – 1 C 22 = (– 1) 2 + 2 (2 – 3) = – 1 C 23 = (– 1) 2 + 3 (2 – 4 ) = – 2 C 31 = (– 1) 3 + 1 (12 – 6) = 6 C 32 = (– 1) 3 + 2 (6 – 15) = – 9 C 33 = (– 1) 3 + 3 (4 – 20) = – 16 {tex}\text { Adj } A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -1 & -2 & 3 \\ -1 & -1 & 2 \\ 6 & 9 & -16 \end{array}\right]^{T}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -1 & -1 & 6 \\ -2 & -1 & 9 \\ 3 & 2 & -16 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}X=A^{-1} B=\frac{1}{|A|}(\text { adj } A) B{/tex} {tex}X=\frac{1}{-1}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -1 & -1 & 6 \\ -2 & -1 & 9 \\ 3 & 2 & -16 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 29000 \\ 30500 \\ 9500 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 1 & -6 \\ - & 1 & -9 \\ -3 & -2 & 16 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 29000 \\ 30500 \\ 9500 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{c} 29000+30500-57000 \\ 58000+30500-85500 \\ -87000-61000+152000 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} \mathrm{x} \\ \mathrm{y} \\ \mathrm{z} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 2500 \\ 3000 \\ 4000 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Hence, x = 2500, y = 3000 and z = 4000
solution_html:
Let x, y, z be the prize amount per person for adaptability, carefulness and calmness respectively
Accoring to question, 
2x + 4y + 3z = 29000
5x + 2y + 3z = 30500
X + y + z = 9500
These three equations can be written as
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 2 & 4 & 3 \\ 5 & 2 & 3 \\ 1 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 29000 \\ 30500 \\ 9500 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
A X = B
|A| = 2(2 – 3) – 4(5 – 3) + 3(5 – 2)
= 2(– 1) – 4(2) + 3(3)
= – 2 – 8 + 9
= – 1
Hence, the unique solution given by x = A – 1B
C11 = (– 1)1 + 1 (2 – 3) = – 1
C12 = (– 1)1 + 2 (5 – 3) = – 2
C13 = (– 1)1 + 3 (5 – 2) = 3
C21 = (– 1)2 + 1 (4 – 3) = – 1
C22 = (– 1)2 + 2 (2 – 3) = – 1
C23 = (– 1)2 + 3 (2 – 4 ) = – 2
C31 = (– 1)3 + 1 (12 – 6) = 6
C32 = (– 1)3 + 2 (6 – 15) = – 9
C33 = (– 1)3 + 3 (4 – 20) = – 16
{tex}\text { Adj } A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -1 & -2 & 3 \\ -1 & -1 & 2 \\ 6 & 9 & -16 \end{array}\right]^{T}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -1 & -1 & 6 \\ -2 & -1 & 9 \\ 3 & 2 & -16 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}X=A^{-1} B=\frac{1}{|A|}(\text { adj } A) B{/tex}
{tex}X=\frac{1}{-1}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -1 & -1 & 6 \\ -2 & -1 & 9 \\ 3 & 2 & -16 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 29000 \\ 30500 \\ 9500 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 1 & -6 \\ - & 1 & -9 \\ -3 & -2 & 16 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 29000 \\ 30500 \\ 9500 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}X=\left[\begin{array}{c} 29000+30500-57000 \\ 58000+30500-85500 \\ -87000-61000+152000 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} \mathrm{x} \\ \mathrm{y} \\ \mathrm{z} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 2500 \\ 3000 \\ 4000 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Hence, x = 2500, y = 3000 and z = 4000

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
Given {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&1 \\ 1&{ - 2}&{ - 2} \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 4}&4&4 \\ 7&1&3 \\ 5&{ - 3}&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}.
Find AB and use this result in solving the following system of equations :
x - y + z = 4
x - 2y - 2z = 9
2x + y + 3z = 1
question_text: Given {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&1 \\ 1&{ - 2}&{ - 2} \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 4}&4&4 \\ 7&1&3 \\ 5&{ - 3}&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} . Find AB and use this result in solving the following system of equations : x - y + z = 4 x - 2y - 2z = 9 2x + y + 3z = 1
explanation: x - y + z = 4 x - 2y - 2z = 9 2x + y + 3z = 1 Let {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&1 \\ 1&{ - 2}&{ - 2} \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]C = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 9 \\ 1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} AX = C {tex}AB = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&1 \\ 1&{ - 2}&{ - 2} \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 4}&4&4 \\ { - 7}&1&3 \\ 5&{ - 3}&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 8&0&0 \\ 0&8&0 \\ 0&0&8 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} AB = 8I {tex}{A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{8}B\left[ \begin{gathered} \because {A^{ - 1}}AB = 8{A^{ - 1}}I \hfill \\ B = 8{A^{ - 1}} \hfill \\ \end{gathered} \right]{/tex} AX = C {tex}X = {A^{ - 1}}C{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{8}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 4}&4&4 \\ { - 7}&1&3 \\ 5&{ - 3}&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 9 \\ 1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3 \\ { - 2} \\ 1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}x = 3,y = - 2,z = - 1{/tex}
solution: x - y + z = 4 x - 2y - 2z = 9 2x + y + 3z = 1 Let {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&1 \\ 1&{ - 2}&{ - 2} \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]C = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 9 \\ 1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} AX = C {tex}AB = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&1 \\ 1&{ - 2}&{ - 2} \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 4}&4&4 \\ { - 7}&1&3 \\ 5&{ - 3}&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 8&0&0 \\ 0&8&0 \\ 0&0&8 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} AB = 8I {tex}{A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{8}B\left[ \begin{gathered} \because {A^{ - 1}}AB = 8{A^{ - 1}}I \hfill \\ B = 8{A^{ - 1}} \hfill \\ \end{gathered} \right]{/tex} AX = C {tex}X = {A^{ - 1}}C{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{8}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 4}&4&4 \\ { - 7}&1&3 \\ 5&{ - 3}&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 9 \\ 1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3 \\ { - 2} \\ 1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}x = 3,y = - 2,z = - 1{/tex}
solution_html:
x - y + z = 4
x - 2y - 2z = 9
2x + y + 3z = 1
Let {tex}A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&1 \\ 1&{ - 2}&{ - 2} \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]X = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]C = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 9 \\ 1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
AX = C
{tex}AB = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&{ - 1}&1 \\ 1&{ - 2}&{ - 2} \\ 2&1&3 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 4}&4&4 \\ { - 7}&1&3 \\ 5&{ - 3}&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 8&0&0 \\ 0&8&0 \\ 0&0&8 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
AB = 8I
{tex}{A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{8}B\left[ \begin{gathered} \because {A^{ - 1}}AB = 8{A^{ - 1}}I \hfill \\ B = 8{A^{ - 1}} \hfill \\ \end{gathered} \right]{/tex}
AX = C
{tex}X = {A^{ - 1}}C{/tex}
{tex}= \frac{1}{8}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} { - 4}&4&4 \\ { - 7}&1&3 \\ 5&{ - 3}&{ - 1} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 9 \\ 1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3 \\ { - 2} \\ 1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex}x = 3,y = - 2,z = - 1{/tex}

question_html: Solve the system of linear equation, using matrix method 2x + 3y + 3z = 5; x - 2y + z = - 4; 3x - y - 2z = 3
question_text: Solve the system of linear equation, using matrix method 2x + 3y + 3z = 5; x - 2y + z = - 4; 3x - y - 2z = 3
explanation: Matrix form of given equations is AX = B {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3&3 \\ 1&{ - 2}&1 \\ 3&{ - 1}&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5 \\ { - 4} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Here {tex}\left| A \right| = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3&3 \\ 1&{ - 2}&1 \\ 3&{ - 1}&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right],X\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5 \\ { - 4} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3&3 \\ 1&{ - 2}&1 \\ 3&{ - 1}&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right|{/tex} = 2(4 + 1) - 3( - 2 - 3) + 3( - 1 + 6) {tex} = 10 + 15 + 15 = 40 \ne 0{/tex} Therefore, solution is unique and {tex}X = {A^{ - 1}}B = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}\left( {adj.A} \right)B{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{{40}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5&3&9 \\ 5&{ - 13}&1 \\ 5&{11}&{ - 7} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5 \\ { - 4} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{{40}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {25 - 12 + 27} \\ {25 + 52 + 3} \\ {25 - 44 - 21} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{{40}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {40} \\ {80} \\ { - 40} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ { - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Therefore, x = 1, y = 2 and z = - 1 .
solution: Matrix form of given equations is AX = B {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3&3 \\ 1&{ - 2}&1 \\ 3&{ - 1}&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5 \\ { - 4} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Here {tex}\left| A \right| = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3&3 \\ 1&{ - 2}&1 \\ 3&{ - 1}&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right],X\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5 \\ { - 4} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3&3 \\ 1&{ - 2}&1 \\ 3&{ - 1}&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right|{/tex} = 2(4 + 1) - 3( - 2 - 3) + 3( - 1 + 6) {tex} = 10 + 15 + 15 = 40 \ne 0{/tex} Therefore, solution is unique and {tex}X = {A^{ - 1}}B = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}\left( {adj.A} \right)B{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{{40}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5&3&9 \\ 5&{ - 13}&1 \\ 5&{11}&{ - 7} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5 \\ { - 4} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{{40}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {25 - 12 + 27} \\ {25 + 52 + 3} \\ {25 - 44 - 21} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}= \frac{1}{{40}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {40} \\ {80} \\ { - 40} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ { - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Therefore, x = 1, y = 2 and z = - 1 .
solution_html:
Matrix form of given equations is AX = B

{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3&3 \\ 1&{ - 2}&1 \\ 3&{ - 1}&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5 \\ { - 4} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Here {tex}\left| A \right| = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3&3 \\ 1&{ - 2}&1 \\ 3&{ - 1}&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right],X\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5 \\ { - 4} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore \left| A \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3&3 \\ 1&{ - 2}&1 \\ 3&{ - 1}&{ - 2} \end{array}} \right|{/tex}

= 2(4 + 1) - 3( - 2 - 3) + 3( - 1 + 6)

{tex} = 10 + 15 + 15 = 40 \ne 0{/tex}

Therefore, solution is unique and {tex}X = {A^{ - 1}}B = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}\left( {adj.A} \right)B{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}} \right] = \frac{1}{{40}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5&3&9 \\ 5&{ - 13}&1 \\ 5&{11}&{ - 7} \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 5 \\ { - 4} \\ 3 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{{40}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {25 - 12 + 27} \\ {25 + 52 + 3} \\ {25 - 44 - 21} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}= \frac{1}{{40}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {40} \\ {80} \\ { - 40} \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1 \\ 2 \\ { - 1} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Therefore, x = 1, y = 2 and z = - 1 .

question_html: Let A be a square matrix of order 3 {tex}\times{/tex}3. Write the value of {tex}|2A|{/tex}, where {tex}|A|{/tex}= 4.
question_text: Let A be a square matrix of order 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3. Write the value of {tex}|2A|{/tex} , where {tex}|A|{/tex} = 4.
explanation: We are given a square matrix A of order 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3. We have to find the value of {tex}|2A|{/tex} , where {tex}{/tex} {tex}|A|{/tex} = 4. We know that, for a square matrix A of order n, {tex}| \ { k } A | = k ^ { n } .| A |{/tex} Here, {tex}| 2 A | = 2 ^ { 3 } | A |{/tex} [ {tex}\because{/tex} order of A is {tex}3 \times 3 {/tex} ] {tex}= 2 ^ { 3 } \times 4 = 8 \times 4 = 32{/tex} [Put |A| = 4]
solution: We are given a square matrix A of order 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3. We have to find the value of {tex}|2A|{/tex} , where {tex}{/tex} {tex}|A|{/tex} = 4. We know that, for a square matrix A of order n, {tex}| \ { k } A | = k ^ { n } .| A |{/tex} Here, {tex}| 2 A | = 2 ^ { 3 } | A |{/tex} [ {tex}\because{/tex} order of A is {tex}3 \times 3 {/tex} ] {tex}= 2 ^ { 3 } \times 4 = 8 \times 4 = 32{/tex} [Put |A| = 4]
solution_html:
We are given a  square matrix A of order 3 {tex}\times{/tex}3. We have to find the value of {tex}|2A|{/tex}, where {tex}{/tex}{tex}|A|{/tex}= 4.

We know that, for a square matrix A of order n,
{tex}| \ { k } A | = k ^ { n } .| A |{/tex}
Here, {tex}| 2 A | = 2 ^ { 3 } | A |{/tex} [{tex}\because{/tex} order of A is {tex}3 \times 3 {/tex}]
{tex}= 2 ^ { 3 } \times 4 = 8 \times 4 = 32{/tex} [Put |A| = 4]

solution_html:
Given, A= {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 1 } & { 2 } & { 1 } \\ { - 1 } & { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 1 } & { - 3 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Now, |A| = 1 (1 + 3) - 2 ( - 1 -1) + 1 ( 3-1)
= 4 + 4 + 2=10
{tex}\Rightarrow \quad | A | \neq 0{/tex}, hence A-1 exists.
Now, cofactors of elements of |A| are,
{tex}A _ { 11 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 2 } \left| \begin{array} { r r } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { - 3 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right| = ( 1 + 3 ) = 4{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 12 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 3 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { - 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right| = - ( - 1 - 1 ) = 2{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 13 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 4 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { - 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 1 } & { - 3 } \end{array} \right| = ( 3 - 1 ) = 2{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 21 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 3 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 2 } & { 1 } \\ { - 3 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right| = - ( 2 + 3 ) = - 5{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 22 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 4 } \left| \begin{array} { l l } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right| = ( 1 - 1 ) = 0{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 23 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 5 } \left| \begin{array} { r r } { 1 } & { 2 } \\ { 1 } & { - 3 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( - 3 - 2 ) = 5{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 31 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 4 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 2 } & { 1 } \\ { 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right| = ( 2 - 1 ) = 1{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 32 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 5 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { - 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right| = - ( 1 + 1 ) = - 2{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 33 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 6 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 2 } \\ { - 1 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right| = ( 1 + 2 ) = 3{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \operatorname { adj } ( A ) = \left[ \begin{array} { l l l } { A _ { 11 } } & { A _ { 12 } } & { A _ { 13 } } \\ { A _ { 21 } } & { A _ { 22 } } & { A _ { 23 } } \\ { A _ { 31 } } & { A _ { 32 } } & { A _ { 33 } } \end{array} \right] ^ { T }{/tex}
= {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 4 } & { 2 } & { 2 } \\ { - 5 } & { 0 } & { 5 } \\ { 1 } & { - 2 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right]^T = \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 4 } & { - 5 } & { 1 } \\ { 2 } & { 0 } & { - 2 } \\ { 2 } & { 5 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
and {tex}A ^ { - 1 } = \frac { 1 } { | A | } \operatorname { adj } ( A ){/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow A ^ { - 1 } = \frac { 1 } { 10 } \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 4 } & { - 5 } & { 1 } \\ { 2 } & { 0 } & { - 2 } \\ { 2 } & { 5 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Given system of equations can be written in matrix from as
{tex}\left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 1 } & { 2 } & { 1 } \\ { - 1 } & { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 1 } & { - 3 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { l } { x } \\ { y } \\ { z } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { l } { 4 } \\ { 0 } \\ { 4 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
i.e.  AX = B
where,
{tex}A = \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 1 } & { 2 } & { 1 } \\ { - 1 } & { 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 1 } & { - 3 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right] B = \left[ \begin{array} { l } { 4 } \\ { 0 } \\ { 4 } \end{array} \right] \text { and } X = \left[ \begin{array} { l } { x } \\ { y } \\ { z } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Clearly, {tex}X = A ^ { - 1 } B = \frac { 1 } { 10 } \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 4 } & { - 5 } & { 1 } \\ { 2 } & { 0 } & { - 2 } \\ { 2 } & { 5 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { l } { 4 } \\ { 0 } \\ { 4 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { l } { x } \\ { y } \\ { z } \end{array} \right] = \frac { 1 } { 10 } \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 16 + 0 + 4 } \\ { 8 + 0 + ( - 8 ) } \\ { 8 + 0 + 12 } \end{array} \right] = \frac { 1 } { 10 } \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 20 } \\ { 0 } \\ { 20 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { l } { 2 } \\ { 0 } \\ { 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Therefore,on comparing corresponding elements, we get x = 2, y = 0 and z = 0

2 } \\ { - 1 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Therefore,on comparing corresponding elements, we get x = 3, y = - 2 andz = - 1

According to the question,we have to determine the product of {tex} \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { - 4 } & { 4 } & { 4 } \\ { - 7 } & { 1 } & { 3 } \\ { 5 } & { - 3 } & { - 1 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}and {tex} \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 1 } & { - 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 1 } & { - 2 } & { - 2 } \\ { 2 } & { 1 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
and then using it we have to solve the system of equations x- y + z = 4, x- 2y- 2z = 9 and  2x + y + 3z = 1.

Let B {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { - 4 } & { 4 } & { 4 } \\ { - 7 } & { 1 } & { 3 } \\ { 5 } & { - 3 } & { - 1 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}and A = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 1 } & { - 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 1 } & { - 2 } & { - 2 } \\ { 2 } & { 1 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Now, BA ={tex}\left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { - 4 } & { 4 } & { 4 } \\ { - 7 } & { 1 } & { 3 } \\ { 5 } & { - 3 } & { - 1 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { 1 } & { - 1 } & { 1 } \\ { 1 } & { - 2 } & { - 2 } \\ { 2 } & { 1 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { c c c } { - 4 + 4 + 8 } & { 4 - 8 + 4 } & { - 4 - 8 + 12 } \\ { - 7 + 1 + 6 } & { 7 - 2 + 3 } & { - 7 - 2 + 9 } \\ { 5 - 3 - 2 } & { - 5 + 6 - 1 } & { 5 + 6 - 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { l l l } { 8 } & { 0 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 8 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 0 } & { 8 } \end{array} \right] = 8 \left[ \begin{array} { l l l } { 1 } & { 0 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 1 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 0 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right] = 8I{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}BA= 8I
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} BA(A-1) = 8lA-1
[post-multiplying both sides by A-1]
{tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} B(AA-1) = 8IA-1
{tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} B = 8A-1  {tex} \left[ \because A A ^ { - 1 } = I \right]{/tex}
{tex} \therefore A ^ { - 1 } = \frac { 1 } { 8 } \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { - 4 } & { 4 } & { 4 } \\ { - 7 } & { 1 } & { 3 } \\ { 5 } & { - 3 } & { - 1 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Hence, X = {tex} \frac { 1 } { 8 } \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { - 4 } & { 4 } & { 4 } \\ { - 7 } & { 1 } & { 3 } \\ { 5 } & { - 3 } & { - 1 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { l } { 4 } \\ { 9 } \\ { 1 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex} \Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { l } { x } \\ { y } \\ { z } \end{array} \right] = \frac { 1 } { 8 } \left[ \begin{array} { c } { - 16 + 36 + 4 } \\ { - 28 + 9 + 3 } \\ { 20 - 27 - 1 } \end{array} \right] = \frac { 1 } { 8 } \left[ \begin{array} { r } { 24 } \\ { - 16 } \\ { - 8 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { r } { 3 } \\ { - 2 } \\ { - 1 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Therefore,on comparing corresponding elements, we get x = 3, y = - 2 andz = - 1

explanation: Given: {tex}{A^{ - 1}} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&{ - 1}&1 \\ { - 15}&6&{ - 5} \\ 5&{ - 2}&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}{B^{ }} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2&{ - 2} \\ { - 1}&3&0 \\ 0&{ - 2}&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Since, (AB) -1 = B -1 A -1 [Reversal law] .....(i) Now {tex}\left| B \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2&{ - 2} \\ { - 1}&3&0 \\ 0&{ - 2}&1 \end{array}} \right|{/tex} = 1(3 - 0) - 2(-1 - 0) + (-2)(2 - 0) = 3 + 2 - 4 = 1 = {tex}\ne {/tex} 0 Therefore, B -1 exists. {tex}\therefore {/tex} B 11 = 3, B 12 = 1, B 13 = 2 and B 21 = 2, B 22 = 1, B 23 = 2 and B 31 = 6, B 22 = 2, B 33 = 5 {tex}\therefore adj.B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&1&2 \\ 2&1&2 \\ 6&2&5 \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&2&6 \\ 1&1&2 \\ 2&2&5 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore {B^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{\left| B \right|}}\left( {adj.B} \right) = \frac{1}{1} {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&2&6 \\ 1&1&2 \\ 2&2&5 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} From eq. (i), {tex}{\left( {AB} \right)^{ - 1}} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&2&6 \\ 1&1&2 \\ 2&2&5 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&{ - 1}&1 \\ { - 15}&6&{ - 5} \\ 5&{ - 2}&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {\left( {AB} \right)^{ - 1}} {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {9 - 30 + 30}&{ - 3 + 12 - 12}&{3 - 10 + 12} \\ {3 - 15 + 10}&{ - 1 + 6 - 4}&{1 - 5 + 4} \\ {6 - 30 + 25}&{ - 2 + 12 - 10}&{2 - 10 + 10} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 9&{ - 3}&5 \\ { - 2}&1&0 \\ 1&0&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
solution: Given: {tex}{A^{ - 1}} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&{ - 1}&1 \\ { - 15}&6&{ - 5} \\ 5&{ - 2}&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}{B^{ }} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2&{ - 2} \\ { - 1}&3&0 \\ 0&{ - 2}&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Since, (AB) -1 = B -1 A -1 [Reversal law] .....(i) Now {tex}\left| B \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2&{ - 2} \\ { - 1}&3&0 \\ 0&{ - 2}&1 \end{array}} \right|{/tex} = 1(3 - 0) - 2(-1 - 0) + (-2)(2 - 0) = 3 + 2 - 4 = 1 = {tex}\ne {/tex} 0 Therefore, B -1 exists. {tex}\therefore {/tex} B 11 = 3, B 12 = 1, B 13 = 2 and B 21 = 2, B 22 = 1, B 23 = 2 and B 31 = 6, B 22 = 2, B 33 = 5 {tex}\therefore adj.B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&1&2 \\ 2&1&2 \\ 6&2&5 \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&2&6 \\ 1&1&2 \\ 2&2&5 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore {B^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{\left| B \right|}}\left( {adj.B} \right) = \frac{1}{1} {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&2&6 \\ 1&1&2 \\ 2&2&5 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} From eq. (i), {tex}{\left( {AB} \right)^{ - 1}} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&2&6 \\ 1&1&2 \\ 2&2&5 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&{ - 1}&1 \\ { - 15}&6&{ - 5} \\ 5&{ - 2}&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow {\left( {AB} \right)^{ - 1}} {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {9 - 30 + 30}&{ - 3 + 12 - 12}&{3 - 10 + 12} \\ {3 - 15 + 10}&{ - 1 + 6 - 4}&{1 - 5 + 4} \\ {6 - 30 + 25}&{ - 2 + 12 - 10}&{2 - 10 + 10} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 9&{ - 3}&5 \\ { - 2}&1&0 \\ 1&0&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Given: {tex}{A^{ - 1}} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&{ - 1}&1 \\ { - 15}&6&{ - 5} \\ 5&{ - 2}&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} and {tex}{B^{ }} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2&{ - 2} \\ { - 1}&3&0 \\ 0&{ - 2}&1 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Since, (AB)-1 = B-1A-1 [Reversal law] .....(i)

Now {tex}\left| B \right| = \left| {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 1&2&{ - 2} \\ { - 1}&3&0 \\ 0&{ - 2}&1 \end{array}} \right|{/tex}

= 1(3 - 0) - 2(-1 - 0) + (-2)(2 - 0) = 3 + 2 - 4 = 1 = {tex}\ne {/tex} 0

Therefore, B-1 exists.

{tex}\therefore {/tex} B11 = 3, B12 = 1, B13 = 2 and B21 = 2, B22 = 1, B23 = 2 and B31 = 6, B22 = 2, B33 = 5

{tex}\therefore adj.B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&1&2 \\ 2&1&2 \\ 6&2&5 \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&2&6 \\ 1&1&2 \\ 2&2&5 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\therefore {B^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{\left| B \right|}}\left( {adj.B} \right) = \frac{1}{1} {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&2&6 \\ 1&1&2 \\ 2&2&5 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

From eq. (i), {tex}{\left( {AB} \right)^{ - 1}} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&2&6 \\ 1&1&2 \\ 2&2&5 \end{array}} \right]\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 3&{ - 1}&1 \\ { - 15}&6&{ - 5} \\ 5&{ - 2}&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex}\Rightarrow {\left( {AB} \right)^{ - 1}} {/tex} {tex}= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {9 - 30 + 30}&{ - 3 + 12 - 12}&{3 - 10 + 12} \\ {3 - 15 + 10}&{ - 1 + 6 - 4}&{1 - 5 + 4} \\ {6 - 30 + 25}&{ - 2 + 12 - 10}&{2 - 10 + 10} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 9&{ - 3}&5 \\ { - 2}&1&0 \\ 1&0&2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

question_text: Using matrices, solve the following system of equations. x-y+ 2z = 7 3x + 4y - 5z = - 5 and 2x- y + 3z = 12

Now, co-factors of elements of |A| are {tex}A _ { 11 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 2 } \left| \begin{array} { r r } { 4 } & { - 5 } \\ { - 1 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( 12 - 5 ) = 7{/tex} {tex}A _ { 12 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 3 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 3 } & { - 5 } \\ { 2 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( 9 + 10 ) = - 19{/tex} {tex}A _ { 13 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 4 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 3 } & { 4 } \\ { 2 } & { - 1 ) ^ { 4 } \left| \begin{array} { l l } { 1 } & { 2 } \\ { 2 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( 3 - 4 ) = - 1{/tex} {tex}A _ { 23 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 5 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { - 1 } \\ { 2 } & { - 1 ) ^ { 5 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 2 } \\ { 3 } & { - 5 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( - 5 - 6 ) = 11{/tex} {tex}A _ { 33 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 6 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { - 1 } \\ { 3 } & { 4 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( 4 + 3 ) = 7{/tex} {tex}\therefore \operatorname { adj } ( A ) = \left[ \begin{array} { l l l } { A _ { 11 } } & { A _ { 12 } } & { A _ { 13 } } \\ { A _ { 21 } } & { A _ { 22 } } & { A _ { 23 } } \\ { A _ { 31 } } & { A _ { 32 } } & { A _ { 33 } } \end{array} \right] ^ { T }{/tex} {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 7 } & { - 19 } & { - 11 } \\ { 1 } & { - 1 } & { - 1 } \\ { - 3 } & { 11 } & { 7 } \end{array} \right] ^ { T }{/tex} {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 7 } & { 1 } & { - 3 } \\ { - 19 } & { - 1 } & { 11 } \\ { - 11 } & { - 1 } & { 7 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} and {tex}A ^ { - 1 } = \frac { \operatorname { adj } ( A ) } { | A | }{/tex} ​​​​ {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 4 } \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 7 } & { 1 } & { - 3 } \\ { - 19 } & { - 1 } & { 11 } \\ { - 11 } & { - 1 } & { 7 } \end{array} \right] {/tex} Therefore, from Eq. (i), we get, X = A -1 B {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { l } { x } \\ { y } \\ { z } \end{array} \right] = \frac { 1 } { 4 } \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 7 } & { 1 } & { - 3 } \\ { - 19 } & { - 1 } & { 11 } \\ { - 11 } & { - 1 } & { 7 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 7 } \\ { - 5 } \\ { 12 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { l } { x } \\ { y } \\ { z } \end{array} \right] = \frac { 1 } { 4 } \left[ \begin{array} { r } { 49 - 5 - 36 } \\ { - 133 + 5 + 132 } \\ { - 77 + 5 + 84 } \end{array} \right] = \frac { 1 } { 4 } \left[ \begin{array} { l } { 8 } \\ { 4 } \\ { 12 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { l } { 2 } \\ { 1 } \\ { 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Therefore, comparing corresponding elements, we get x = 2, y = 1 and z = 3.

11 } & { - 1 } & { 7 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} and {tex}A ^ { - 1 } = \frac { \operatorname { adj } ( A ) } { | A | }{/tex} ​​​​ {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 4 } \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 7 } & { 1 } & { - 3 } \\ { - 19 } & { - 1 } & { 11 } \\ { - 11 } & { - 1 } & { 7 } \end{array} \right] {/tex} Therefore, from Eq. solution_html:
In matrix form ,the system of equations
x-y+2z=7
3x + 4y - 5z = - 5
and 2x - y+ 3z = 12
 can be written as,
AX=B ...(i)
where,
A  {tex}=\left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 1 } & { - 1 } & { 2 } \\ { 3 } & { 4 } & { - 5 } \\ { 2 } & { - 1 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right] , X = \left[ \begin{array} { l } { x } \\ { y } \\ { z } \end{array} \right] \text { and } B = \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 7 } \\ { - 5 } \\ { 12 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Here, |A| =1 (12- 5) + 1 (9 + 10) +2(-3- 8)
=1 (7) + 1 (19) +2(-11) (1)
= 7 + 19 - 22 = 4
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |A| {tex}\neq{/tex} 0
So, A is non-singular and its inverse exists.
Now, co-factors of elements of |A| are
{tex}A _ { 11 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 2 } \left| \begin{array} { r r } { 4 } & { - 5 } \\ { - 1 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( 12 - 5 ) = 7{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 12 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 3 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 3 } & { - 5 } \\ { 2 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( 9 + 10 ) = - 19{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 13 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 4 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 3 } & { 4 } \\ { 2 } & { - 1 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( - 3 - 8 ) = - 11{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 21 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 3 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { - 1 } & { 2 } \\ { - 1 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( - 3 + 2 ) = 1{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 22 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 4 } \left| \begin{array} { l l } { 1 } & { 2 } \\ { 2 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( 3 - 4 ) = - 1{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 23 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 5 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { - 1 } \\ { 2 } & { - 1 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( - 1 + 2 ) = - 1{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 31 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 4 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { - 1 } & { 2 } \\ { 4 } & { - 5 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( 5 - 8 ) = - 3{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 32 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 5 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { 2 } \\ { 3 } & { - 5 } \end{array} \right| = - 1 ( - 5 - 6 ) = 11{/tex}
{tex}A _ { 33 } = ( - 1 ) ^ { 6 } \left| \begin{array} { c c } { 1 } & { - 1 } \\ { 3 } & { 4 } \end{array} \right| = 1 ( 4 + 3 ) = 7{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \operatorname { adj } ( A ) = \left[ \begin{array} { l l l } { A _ { 11 } } & { A _ { 12 } } & { A _ { 13 } } \\ { A _ { 21 } } & { A _ { 22 } } & { A _ { 23 } } \\ { A _ { 31 } } & { A _ { 32 } } & { A _ { 33 } } \end{array} \right] ^ { T }{/tex}
{tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 7 } & { - 19 } & { - 11 } \\ { 1 } & { - 1 } & { - 1 } \\ { - 3 } & { 11 } & { 7 } \end{array} \right] ^ { T }{/tex}
{tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 7 } & { 1 } & { - 3 } \\ { - 19 } & { - 1 } & { 11 } \\ { - 11 } & { - 1 } & { 7 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
and {tex}A ^ { - 1 } = \frac { \operatorname { adj } ( A ) } { | A | }{/tex}
​​​​                  {tex}= \frac { 1 } { 4 } \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 7 } & { 1 } & { - 3 } \\ { - 19 } & { - 1 } & { 11 } \\ { - 11 } & { - 1 } & { 7 } \end{array} \right] {/tex}
Therefore, from Eq. (i), we get,
X = A-1B
{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { l } { x } \\ { y } \\ { z } \end{array} \right] = \frac { 1 } { 4 } \left[ \begin{array} { r r r } { 7 } & { 1 } & { - 3 } \\ { - 19 } & { - 1 } & { 11 } \\ { - 11 } & { - 1 } & { 7 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { c } { 7 } \\ { - 5 } \\ { 12 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow \left[ \begin{array} { l } { x } \\ { y } \\ { z } \end{array} \right] = \frac { 1 } { 4 } \left[ \begin{array} { r } { 49 - 5 - 36 } \\ { - 133 + 5 + 132 } \\ { - 77 + 5 + 84 } \end{array} \right] = \frac { 1 } { 4 } \left[ \begin{array} { l } { 8 } \\ { 4 } \\ { 12 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { l } { 2 } \\ { 1 } \\ { 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Therefore, comparing corresponding elements, we get x = 2, y = 1 and z = 3.

subject: Mathematics
question_html: For what value of x the matrix {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ll} {1+x} & {7} \\ {3-x} & {8} \end{array}\right]{/tex} is singular?
question_text: For what value of x the matrix {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ll} {1+x} & {7} \\ {3-x} & {8} \end{array}\right]{/tex} is singular?
explanation: Let, |A| = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow \left|\begin{array}{rr} {1+x} & {7} \\ {3-x} & {8} \end{array}\right|=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (1 + x) {tex}\times{/tex} 8 - 7 {tex}\times{/tex} (3 - x) = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 8 + 8x - 21 + 7x = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 15x - 13 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{x}=\frac{13}{15}{/tex}
solution: Let, |A| = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow \left|\begin{array}{rr} {1+x} & {7} \\ {3-x} & {8} \end{array}\right|=0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (1 + x) {tex}\times{/tex} 8 - 7 {tex}\times{/tex} (3 - x) = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 8 + 8x - 21 + 7x = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 15x - 13 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{x}=\frac{13}{15}{/tex}
solution_html:
Let, |A| = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow \left|\begin{array}{rr} {1+x} & {7} \\ {3-x} & {8} \end{array}\right|=0{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (1 + x) {tex}\times{/tex} 8 - 7 {tex}\times{/tex} (3 - x) = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 8 + 8x - 21 + 7x = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 15x - 13 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow \mathrm{x}=\frac{13}{15}{/tex}

question_html:
Find A-1 where A {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {1} & {2} & {-3} \\ {2} & {3} & {2} \\ {3} & {-3} & {-4} \end{array}\right]{/tex}. Hence solve the system of equations
x + 2y - 3z = - 4, 2x + 3y + 2z = 2, 3x - 3y - 4z = 11.
question_text: Find A -1 where A {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {1} & {2} & {-3} \\ {2} & {3} & {2} \\ {3} & {-3} & {-4} \end{array}\right]{/tex} . Hence solve the system of equations x + 2y - 3z = - 4, 2x + 3y + 2z = 2, 3x - 3y - 4z = 11.
explanation: We have, A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {1} & {2} & {-3} \\ {2} & {3} & {2} \\ {3} & {-3} & {-4} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} |A| = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{rrr} {1} & {2} & {-3} \\ {2} & {3} & {2} \\ {3} & {-3} & {-4} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = - 6 + 28 + 45 = 67 {tex}\ne{/tex} 0 So, A is invertible. Let C ij be the co-factors of any in A = [a ij ]. Let C ij be the co-factors of any in A = [a ij ]. Then, {tex}C_{11}=(-1)^{1+1}\left|\begin{array}{rr} {3} & {2} \\ {-3} & {-4} \end{array}\right|=-6{/tex} , {tex}C_{12}=(-1)^{1+2}\left|\begin{array}{rr} {2} & {2} \\ {3} & {-4} \end{array}\right|=14{/tex} {tex}C_{13}=(-1)^{1+3}\left|\begin{array}{rr} {2} & {3} \\ {3} & {-3} \end{array}\right|=-15{/tex} , {tex}C_{21}=(-1)^{2+1}\left|\begin{array}{rr} {2} & {-3} \\ {-3} & {-4} \end{array}\right|=17{/tex} , {tex}C_{22}=(-1)^{2+2}\left|\begin{array}{cc} {1} & {-3} \\ {3} & {-4} \end{array}\right|=5{/tex} , {tex}C_{23}=(-1)^{2+3}\left|\begin{array}{rr} {1} & {2} \\ {3} & {-3} \end{array}\right|=9{/tex} {tex}C_{31}=(-1)^{3+1}\left|\begin{array}{rr} {2} & {-3} \\ {3} & {2} \end{array}\right|=13{/tex} , {tex}C_{32}=(-1)^{3+2}\left|\begin{array}{rr} {1} & {-3} \\ {2} & {2} \end{array}\right|=-8{/tex} {tex}C_{33}=(-1)^{3+3}\left|\begin{array}{ll} {1} & {2} \\ {2} & {3} \end{array}\right|=-1{/tex} and, {tex}C_{33}=(-1)^{3+3}\left|\begin{array}{ll} {1} & {2} \\ {2} & {3} \end{array}\right|=-1{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-6} & {14} & {-15} \\ {17} & {5} & {9} \\ {13} & {-8} & {-1} \end{array}\right]^{T}{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {-6} & {17} & {13} \\ {14} & {5} & {-8} \\ {-15} & {9} & {-1} \end{array}\right]{/tex} So, A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{|A|} \text { adj } A{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{67}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {-6} & {17} & {13} \\ {14} & {5} & {-8} \\ {-15} & {9} & {-1} \end{array}\right]{/tex} ....(i) The given system of equations is x + 2y - 3z = - 4 2x+ 3y + 2z = - 2 3 x - 3 y - 4z = 11 or, AX = B, where A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {1} & {1} & {-3} \\ {2} & {3} & {2} \\ {3} & {-3} & {-4} \end{array}\right]{/tex} , X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right]{/tex} and B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{r} {-4} \\ {2} \\ {11} \end{array}\right]{/tex} As discussed above A is non-singular and so invertible. The inverse of A is given by (i). The solution of the given system of equations is given by x = A -1 B {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{67}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {-6} & {17} & {13} \\ {14} & {5} & {-8} \\ {-15} & {9} & {-1} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{r} {-4} \\ {2} \\ {11} \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{67}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {24} & {+34} & {+143} \\ {-56} & {+10} & {-88} \\ {60} & {+18} & {-11} \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{r} {3} \\ {-2} \\ {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 3, y = - 2 and z = 1 is the required solution
solution_html:
We have,
A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {1} & {2} & {-3} \\ {2} & {3} & {2} \\ {3} & {-3} & {-4} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} |A| = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{rrr} {1} & {2} & {-3} \\ {2} & {3} & {2} \\ {3} & {-3} & {-4} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = - 6 + 28 + 45 = 67 {tex}\ne{/tex} 0
So, A is invertible.
Let Cij be the co-factors of any in A = [aij]. Then,
{tex}C_{11}=(-1)^{1+1}\left|\begin{array}{rr} {3} & {2} \\ {-3} & {-4} \end{array}\right|=-6{/tex}, {tex}C_{12}=(-1)^{1+2}\left|\begin{array}{rr} {2} & {2} \\ {3} & {-4} \end{array}\right|=14{/tex}
{tex}C_{13}=(-1)^{1+3}\left|\begin{array}{rr} {2} & {3} \\ {3} & {-3} \end{array}\right|=-15{/tex}, {tex}C_{21}=(-1)^{2+1}\left|\begin{array}{rr} {2} & {-3} \\ {-3} & {-4} \end{array}\right|=17{/tex},
{tex}C_{22}=(-1)^{2+2}\left|\begin{array}{cc} {1} & {-3} \\ {3} & {-4} \end{array}\right|=5{/tex}, {tex}C_{23}=(-1)^{2+3}\left|\begin{array}{rr} {1} & {2} \\ {3} & {-3} \end{array}\right|=9{/tex}
{tex}C_{31}=(-1)^{3+1}\left|\begin{array}{rr} {2} & {-3} \\ {3} & {2} \end{array}\right|=13{/tex}, {tex}C_{32}=(-1)^{3+2}\left|\begin{array}{rr} {1} & {-3} \\ {2} & {2} \end{array}\right|=-8{/tex}
{tex}C_{33}=(-1)^{3+3}\left|\begin{array}{ll} {1} & {2} \\ {2} & {3} \end{array}\right|=-1{/tex}
and, {tex}C_{33}=(-1)^{3+3}\left|\begin{array}{ll} {1} & {2} \\ {2} & {3} \end{array}\right|=-1{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-6} & {14} & {-15} \\ {17} & {5} & {9} \\ {13} & {-8} & {-1} \end{array}\right]^{T}{/tex}= {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {-6} & {17} & {13} \\ {14} & {5} & {-8} \\ {-15} & {9} & {-1} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
So, A-1 = {tex}\frac{1}{|A|} \text { adj } A{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{67}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {-6} & {17} & {13} \\ {14} & {5} & {-8} \\ {-15} & {9} & {-1} \end{array}\right]{/tex}                 ....(i)
The given system of equations is
x + 2y - 3z = - 4
2x+ 3y + 2z = - 2
3 x - 3 y - 4z = 11
or, AX = B, where A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {1} & {1} & {-3} \\ {2} & {3} & {2} \\ {3} & {-3} & {-4} \end{array}\right]{/tex}, X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right]{/tex} and B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{r} {-4} \\ {2} \\ {11} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
As discussed above A is non-singular and so invertible. The inverse of A is given by (i).
The solution of the given system of equations is given by
x = A-1B
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{67}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {-6} & {17} & {13} \\ {14} & {5} & {-8} \\ {-15} & {9} & {-1} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{r} {-4} \\ {2} \\ {11} \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{67}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {24} & {+34} & {+143} \\ {-56} & {+10} & {-88} \\ {60} & {+18} & {-11} \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{r} {3} \\ {-2} \\ {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 3, y = - 2 and z = 1 is the required solution

subject: Mathematics
question_html: A matrix A of order 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 has determinant 5. What is the value of |3A|?
question_text: A matrix A of order 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 3 has determinant 5. What is the value of |3A|?
explanation: If the determinant of a matrix A of order m is, {tex}\Delta{/tex} then the determinant of matrix {tex}\lambda{/tex} A, where {tex}\lambda{/tex} is a scalar, is {tex}\lambda^m \Delta{/tex} . In this question, {tex}\Delta{/tex} = 5, {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 3 and m = 3. Since the scalar is taken from each row it is multiplied by the number of rows of the matrix, which is the property of the determinant. | {tex}\lambda{/tex} A| = 3 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = 135
solution: If the determinant of a matrix A of order m is, {tex}\Delta{/tex} then the determinant of matrix {tex}\lambda{/tex} A, where {tex}\lambda{/tex} is a scalar, is {tex}\lambda^m \Delta{/tex} . In this question, {tex}\Delta{/tex} = 5, {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 3 and m = 3. Since the scalar is taken from each row it is multiplied by the number of rows of the matrix, which is the property of the determinant. | {tex}\lambda{/tex} A| = 3 3 {tex}\times{/tex} 5 = 135
solution_html:
If the determinant of a matrix A of order m is,{tex}\Delta{/tex} then the determinant of matrix {tex}\lambda{/tex}A, where {tex}\lambda{/tex} is a scalar, is {tex}\lambda^m \Delta{/tex}.
In this question, {tex}\Delta{/tex} = 5, {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 3 and m = 3.
Since the scalar is taken from each row it is multiplied by the number of rows of the matrix, which is the property of the determinant.
|{tex}\lambda{/tex}A| = 33 {tex}\times{/tex} 5
= 135

question_html:
Using matrix method, solve the system of equations
x + 2y + z = 4;
-x + y + z = 0;
x - 3y + z = 4.
question_text: Using matrix method, solve the system of equations x + 2y + z = 4; -x + y + z = 0; x - 3y + z = 4.
explanation: Given set of equations are : - x + 2y + z = 4 -x + y + z = 0 x - 3y + z = 4 Converting the following set of equations in matrix form, we ha AX = B {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 2 & 1 \\ -1 & 1 & 1 \\ 1 & -3 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 4 \\ 0 \\ 4 \end{array}\right]{/tex} R 2 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} R 2 + R 1 R 3 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} R 3 - R1 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 2 & 1 \\ 0 & 3 & 2 \\ 0 & -5 & 0 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 4 \\ 4 \\ 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Again converting into equations we get X + 2y + z = 4 3y + 2z = 4 -5y = 0 Y = 0 0 + 2z = 4 Z = 2 X + 0 + 2 = 4 X = 2 {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 2, y = 0, z = 2
solution: Given set of equations are : - x + 2y + z = 4 -x + y + z = 0 x - 3y + z = 4 Converting the following set of equations in matrix form, we ha AX = B {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 2 & 1 \\ -1 & 1 & 1 \\ 1 & -3 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 4 \\ 0 \\ 4 \end{array}\right]{/tex} R 2 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} R 2 + R 1 R 3 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} R 3 - R1 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 2 & 1 \\ 0 & 3 & 2 \\ 0 & -5 & 0 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 4 \\ 4 \\ 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Again converting into equations we get X + 2y + z = 4 3y + 2z = 4 -5y = 0 Y = 0 0 + 2z = 4 Z = 2 X + 0 + 2 = 4 X = 2 {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 2, y = 0, z = 2
solution_html:
Given set of equations are : -
x + 2y + z = 4
-x + y + z = 0
x - 3y + z = 4
Converting the following set of equations in matrix form, we ha
AX = B
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 2 & 1 \\ -1 & 1 & 1 \\ 1 & -3 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 4 \\ 0 \\ 4 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
R2 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex}R2 + R1
R3 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex}R3 - R1
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 2 & 1 \\ 0 & 3 & 2 \\ 0 & -5 & 0 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} 4 \\ 4 \\ 0 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Again converting into equations we get
X + 2y + z = 4
3y + 2z = 4
-5y = 0
Y = 0
0 + 2z = 4
Z = 2
X + 0 + 2 = 4
X = 2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 2, y = 0, z = 2

question_html:
Using matrix method, solve the system of equations
3x + 4y + 7z = 4;
2x - y + 3z = -3;
x + 2y - 3z = 8.
question_text: Using matrix method, solve the system of equations 3x + 4y + 7z = 4; 2x - y + 3z = -3; x + 2y - 3z = 8.
explanation: Given set of lines are:- 3X + 4y + 7z = 4; 2x - y + 3z = - 3; x + 2y - 3z = 8 Converting the following equations in matrix form, AX = B {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & 4 & 7 \\ 2 & -1 & 3 \\ 1 & 2 & -3 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 4 \\ -3 \\ 8 \end{array}\right]{/tex} 3R 2 - 2R 1 3R 3 - R 1 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & 4 & 7 \\ 0 & -11 & -5 \\ 0 & 2 & -16 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 4 \\ -17 \\ 20 \end{array}\right]{/tex} 11R 3 + 2R 2 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & 4 & 7 \\ 0 & -11 & -5 \\ 0 & 0 & -186 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 4 \\ -17 \\ 186 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Again converting into equations we get, 3x + 4y + 7z = 4 -11y - 5z = -17 -186z = 186 Z = -1 -11y + 5 = - 17 -11y = - 22 Y = 2 3x + 4×2 + 7× - 1 = 4 3x = 4 - 8 + 7 X = 1 {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 1, y = 2, z = -1
solution: Given set of lines are:- 3X + 4y + 7z = 4; 2x - y + 3z = - 3; x + 2y - 3z = 8 Converting the following equations in matrix form, AX = B {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & 4 & 7 \\ 2 & -1 & 3 \\ 1 & 2 & -3 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 4 \\ -3 \\ 8 \end{array}\right]{/tex} 3R 2 - 2R 1 3R 3 - R 1 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & 4 & 7 \\ 0 & -11 & -5 \\ 0 & 2 & -16 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 4 \\ -17 \\ 20 \end{array}\right]{/tex} 11R 3 + 2R 2 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & 4 & 7 \\ 0 & -11 & -5 \\ 0 & 0 & -186 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 4 \\ -17 \\ 186 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Again converting into equations we get, 3x + 4y + 7z = 4 -11y - 5z = -17 -186z = 186 Z = -1 -11y + 5 = - 17 -11y = - 22 Y = 2 3x + 4×2 + 7× - 1 = 4 3x = 4 - 8 + 7 X = 1 {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 1, y = 2, z = -1
solution_html:
Given set of lines are:-
3X + 4y + 7z = 4;
2x - y + 3z = - 3;
x + 2y - 3z = 8
Converting the following equations in matrix form,
AX = B
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & 4 & 7 \\ 2 & -1 & 3 \\ 1 & 2 & -3 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 4 \\ -3 \\ 8 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
3R2 - 2R1
3R3 - R1
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & 4 & 7 \\ 0 & -11 & -5 \\ 0 & 2 & -16 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 4 \\ -17 \\ 20 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
11R3 + 2R2
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 3 & 4 & 7 \\ 0 & -11 & -5 \\ 0 & 0 & -186 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 4 \\ -17 \\ 186 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Again converting into equations we get,
3x + 4y + 7z = 4
-11y - 5z = -17
-186z = 186
Z = -1
-11y + 5 = - 17
-11y = - 22
Y = 2
3x + 4×2 + 7× - 1 = 4
3x = 4 - 8 + 7
X = 1
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 1, y = 2, z = -1

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}\left| \begin{array} { r r } { x } & { x } \\ { 1 } & { x } \end{array} \right| = \left| \begin{array} { l l } { 3 } & { 4 } \\ { 1 } & { 2 } \end{array} \right|{/tex}, then write the positive value of x.
question_text: If {tex}\left| \begin{array} { r r } { x } & { x } \\ { 1 } & { x } \end{array} \right| = \left| \begin{array} { l l } { 3 } & { 4 } \\ { 1 } & { 2 } \end{array} \right|{/tex} , then write the positive value of x.
explanation: Given, {tex}\left| \begin{array} { l l } { x } & { x } \\ { 1 } & { x } \end{array} \right| = \left| \begin{array} { l l } { 3 } & { 4 } \\ { 1 } & { 2 } \end{array} \right|{/tex} On expanding, we get x 2 - x = 6-4 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x 2 - x- 2= 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x - 2) (x + 1) = 0 {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 2 or -1 Hence, the positive value of x is 2.
solution: Given, {tex}\left| \begin{array} { l l } { x } & { x } \\ { 1 } & { x } \end{array} \right| = \left| \begin{array} { l l } { 3 } & { 4 } \\ { 1 } & { 2 } \end{array} \right|{/tex} On expanding, we get x 2 - x = 6-4 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x 2 - x- 2= 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x - 2) (x + 1) = 0 {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 2 or -1 Hence, the positive value of x is 2.
solution_html:
Given, {tex}\left| \begin{array} { l l } { x } & { x } \\ { 1 } & { x } \end{array} \right| = \left| \begin{array} { l l } { 3 } & { 4 } \\ { 1 } & { 2 } \end{array} \right|{/tex}
On expanding, we get
x 2 - x = 6-4 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x 2 - x- 2= 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} (x - 2) (x + 1) = 0
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 2 or -1
Hence, the positive value of x is 2.

question_html:
Solve the following system of equations, using matrix method:
x + 2y + z = 7,
x + 3z = 11,
2x - 3y = 1
question_text: Solve the following system of equations, using matrix method: x + 2y + z = 7, x + 3z = 11, 2x - 3y = 1
explanation: The given system of equations is x + 2y + 2 = 7 x + 0y + 3z = 11 2x - 3 y + 0z = 1 or, {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {1} & {2} & {1} \\ {1} & {0} & {3} \\ {2} & {-3} & {0} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{r} {7} \\ {11} \\ {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex} or, AX = B, where A = {tex} \left[\begin{array}{rrr} {1} & {2} & {1} \\ {1} & {0} & {3} \\ {2} & {-3} & {0} \end{array}\right] {/tex} , X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right]{/tex} and B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c} {7} \\ {11} \\ {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex} Now, |A| = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{rrr} {1} & {2} & {1} \\ {1} & {0} & {3} \\ {2} & {-3} & {0} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 1(0 + 9) - 2 (0 - 6) + 1 (-3 - 0) = 9 + 12 - 3 = 18 {tex}\ne{/tex} 0 So, the given system of equations has a unique solution given by X = A -1 B Let C ij be the co-factors of elements a ij - in A = [a ij ]. Then, C 11 = (-1) 1+1 {tex}\left|\begin{array}{rr} {0} & {3} \\ {-3} & {0} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 9, C 12 = (-1) 1+2 {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ll} {1} & {3} \\ {2} & {0} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 6, C 13 = (-1) 1+3 {tex}\left|\begin{array}{rr} {1} & {0} \\ {2} & {-3} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = -3, C 21 = (-1) 2+1 {tex}\left|\begin{array}{rr} {2} & {1} \\ {-3} & {0} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = -3, C 22 = (-1) 2+2 {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ll} {1} & {1} \\ {2} & {0} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = -2, C 23 = (-1) 2+3 {tex}\left|\begin{array}{rr} {1} & {2} \\ {2} & {-3} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 7, C 31 = (-1) 3+1 {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ll} {2} & {1} \\ {0} & {3} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 6, C 32 = (-1) 3+2 {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ll} {1} & {1} \\ {1} & {3} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = -2, and, C 33 = (-1) 3+3 {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ll} {1} & {2} \\ {1} & {0} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = -2 adjA = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {9} & {6} & {-3} \\ {-3} & {-2} & {7} \\ {6} & {-2} & {-2} \end{array}\right]^{T}{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {9} & {-3} & {6} \\ {6} & {-2} & {-2} \\ {-3} & {7} & {-2} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{|A|} \text { adj } A{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{18}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {9} & {-3} & {6} \\ {6} & {-2} & {-2} \\ {-3} & {7} & {-2} \end{array}\right]{/tex} Now, X = A -1 B {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} X = {tex}\frac{1}{18}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {9} & {-3} & {6} \\ {6} & {-2} & {-2} \\ {-3} & {7} & {-2} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{r} {7} \\ {11} \\ {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{18}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {63} & {-33} & {+6} \\ {42} & {-22} & {-2} \\ {-21} & {+77} & {-2} \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{18}\left[\begin{array}{c} {36} \\ {18} \\ {54} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} {2} \\ {1} \\ {3} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} {2} \\ {1} \\ {3} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2, y = 1 and 2 = 3 Hence, x = 2, y = 1 and z = 3 is the required solution

Then, C 11 = (-1) 1+1 {tex}\left|\begin{array}{rr} {0} & {3} \\ {-3} & {0} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 9, C 12 = (-1) 1+2 {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ll} {1} & {3} \\ {2} & {0} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 6, C 13 = (-1) 1+3 {tex}\left|\begin{array}{rr} {1} & {0} \\ {2} & {-3} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = -3, C 21 = (-1) 2+1 {tex}\left|\begin{array}{rr} {2} & {1} \\ {-3} & {0} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = -3, C 22 = (-1) 2+2 {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ll} {1} & {1} \\ {2} & {0} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = -2, C 23 = (-1) 2+3 {tex}\left|\begin{array}{rr} {1} & {2} \\ {2} & {-3} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 7, C 31 = (-1) 3+1 {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ll} {2} & {1} \\ {0} & {3} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 6, C 32 = (-1) 3+2 {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ll} {1} & {1} \\ {1} & {3} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = -2, and, C 33 = (-1) 3+3 {tex}\left|\begin{array}{ll} {1} & {2} \\ {1} & {0} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = -2 adjA = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {9} & {6} & {-3} \\ {-3} & {-2} & {7} \\ {6} & {-2} & {-2} \end{array}\right]^{T}{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {9} & {-3} & {6} \\ {6} & {-2} & {-2} \\ {-3} & {7} & {-2} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A -1 = {tex}\frac{1}{|A|} \text { adj } A{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{18}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {9} & {-3} & {6} \\ {6} & {-2} & {-2} \\ {-3} & {7} & {-2} \end{array}\right]{/tex} Now, X = A -1 B {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} X = {tex}\frac{1}{18}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {9} & {-3} & {6} \\ {6} & {-2} & {-2} \\ {-3} & {7} & {-2} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{r} {7} \\ {11} \\ {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{18}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {63} & {-33} & {+6} \\ {42} & {-22} & {-2} \\ {-21} & {+77} & {-2} \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{18}\left[\begin{array}{c} {36} \\ {18} \\ {54} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} {2} \\ {1} \\ {3} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} {2} \\ {1} \\ {3} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2, y = 1 and 2 = 3 Hence, x = 2, y = 1 and z = 3 is the required solution
solution_html:
The given system of equations is 
x + 2y + 2 = 7
x + 0y + 3z = 11
2x - 3 y + 0z = 1
or, {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {1} & {2} & {1} \\ {1} & {0} & {3} \\ {2} & {-3} & {0} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{r} {7} \\ {11} \\ {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
or, AX = B, where A = {tex} \left[\begin{array}{rrr} {1} & {2} & {1} \\ {1} & {0} & {3} \\ {2} & {-3} & {0} \end{array}\right] {/tex}, X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right]{/tex} and B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c} {7} \\ {11} \\ {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Now, |A| = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{rrr} {1} & {2} & {1} \\ {1} & {0} & {3} \\ {2} & {-3} & {0} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 1(0 + 9) - 2 (0 - 6) + 1 (-3 - 0) = 9 + 12 - 3 = 18 {tex}\ne{/tex} 0
So, the given system of equations has a unique solution given by X = A-1B
Let Cij be the co-factors of elements aij - in A = [aij]. Then,
C11 = (-1)1+1{tex}\left|\begin{array}{rr} {0} & {3} \\ {-3} & {0} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 9, C12 = (-1)1+2{tex}\left|\begin{array}{ll} {1} & {3} \\ {2} & {0} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 6, 
C13 = (-1)1+3{tex}\left|\begin{array}{rr} {1} & {0} \\ {2} & {-3} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = -3, C21 = (-1)2+1{tex}\left|\begin{array}{rr} {2} & {1} \\ {-3} & {0} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = -3,
C22 = (-1)2+2{tex}\left|\begin{array}{ll} {1} & {1} \\ {2} & {0} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = -2, C23 = (-1)2+3{tex}\left|\begin{array}{rr} {1} & {2} \\ {2} & {-3} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 7,
C31 = (-1)3+1{tex}\left|\begin{array}{ll} {2} & {1} \\ {0} & {3} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 6, C32 = (-1)3+2{tex}\left|\begin{array}{ll} {1} & {1} \\ {1} & {3} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = -2, 
and, C33 = (-1)3+3{tex}\left|\begin{array}{ll} {1} & {2} \\ {1} & {0} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = -2
adjA = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {9} & {6} & {-3} \\ {-3} & {-2} & {7} \\ {6} & {-2} & {-2} \end{array}\right]^{T}{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {9} & {-3} & {6} \\ {6} & {-2} & {-2} \\ {-3} & {7} & {-2} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A-1 = {tex}\frac{1}{|A|} \text { adj } A{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{18}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {9} & {-3} & {6} \\ {6} & {-2} & {-2} \\ {-3} & {7} & {-2} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Now, X = A-1B 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} X = {tex}\frac{1}{18}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {9} & {-3} & {6} \\ {6} & {-2} & {-2} \\ {-3} & {7} & {-2} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{r} {7} \\ {11} \\ {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{18}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} {63} & {-33} & {+6} \\ {42} & {-22} & {-2} \\ {-21} & {+77} & {-2} \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\frac{1}{18}\left[\begin{array}{c} {36} \\ {18} \\ {54} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} {2} \\ {1} \\ {3} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} {2} \\ {1} \\ {3} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2, y = 1 and 2 = 3
Hence, x = 2, y = 1 and z = 3 is the required solution

question_html: Write A-1 for A = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 2 } & { 5 } \\ { 1 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}.
question_text: Write A -1 for A = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 2 } & { 5 } \\ { 1 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} .
explanation: We have, A = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 2 } & { 5 } \\ { 1 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Clearly, adj A = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 3 } & { - 5 } \\ { - 1 } & { 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} and {tex}| A | = \left| \begin{array} { l l } { 2 } & { 5 } \\ { 1 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right| = 6 - 5 = 1{/tex} {tex}\therefore \quad A ^ { - 1 } = \frac { 1 } { | A | } \text { adj } ( A ) = \left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 3 } & { - 5 } \\ { - 1 } & { 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
solution: We have, A = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 2 } & { 5 } \\ { 1 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Clearly, adj A = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 3 } & { - 5 } \\ { - 1 } & { 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} and {tex}| A | = \left| \begin{array} { l l } { 2 } & { 5 } \\ { 1 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right| = 6 - 5 = 1{/tex} {tex}\therefore \quad A ^ { - 1 } = \frac { 1 } { | A | } \text { adj } ( A ) = \left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 3 } & { - 5 } \\ { - 1 } & { 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}

We have, A = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 2 } & { 5 } \\ { 1 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Clearly, adj A = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 3 } & { - 5 } \\ { - 1 } & { 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
and {tex}| A | = \left| \begin{array} { l l } { 2 } & { 5 } \\ { 1 } & { 3 } \end{array} \right| = 6 - 5 = 1{/tex}

question_html: If A = {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 2 } & { 3 } \\ { 5 } & { - 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}then write A{tex}^{-1}{/tex} in terms of A.
question_text: If A = {tex}= \left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 2 } & { 3 } \\ { 5 } & { - 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} then write A {tex}^{-1}{/tex} in terms of A.
explanation: We have, {tex}A = \left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 2 } & { 3 } \\ { 5 } & { - 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Clearly, adj A = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { r r } { - 2 } & { - 3 } \\ { - 5 } & { 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} and {tex}| A | = \left| \begin{array} { r r } { 2 } & { 3 } \\ { 5 } & { - 2 } \end{array} \right| = - 4 - 15 = - 19{/tex} {tex}\therefore \quad A ^ { - 1 } = \frac { 1 } { | A | } \text { adj } A = \frac { - 1 } { 19 } \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { - 2 } & { - 3 } \\ { - 5 } & { 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}\equiv \frac { 1 } { 19 } \left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 2 } & { 3 } \\ { 5 } & { - 2 } \end{array} \right] = \frac { 1 } { 19 } A{/tex}
solution: We have, {tex}A = \left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 2 } & { 3 } \\ { 5 } & { - 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} Clearly, adj A = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { r r } { - 2 } & { - 3 } \\ { - 5 } & { 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} and {tex}| A | = \left| \begin{array} { r r } { 2 } & { 3 } \\ { 5 } & { - 2 } \end{array} \right| = - 4 - 15 = - 19{/tex} {tex}\therefore \quad A ^ { - 1 } = \frac { 1 } { | A | } \text { adj } A = \frac { - 1 } { 19 } \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { - 2 } & { - 3 } \\ { - 5 } & { 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}\equiv \frac { 1 } { 19 } \left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 2 } & { 3 } \\ { 5 } & { - 2 } \end{array} \right] = \frac { 1 } { 19 } A{/tex}
solution_html:
We have, {tex}A = \left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 2 } & { 3 } \\ { 5 } & { - 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
Clearly, adj A = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { r r } { - 2 } & { - 3 } \\ { - 5 } & { 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
and {tex}| A | = \left| \begin{array} { r r } { 2 } & { 3 } \\ { 5 } & { - 2 } \end{array} \right| = - 4 - 15 = - 19{/tex}
{tex}\therefore \quad A ^ { - 1 } = \frac { 1 } { | A | } \text { adj } A = \frac { - 1 } { 19 } \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { - 2 } & { - 3 } \\ { - 5 } & { 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\equiv \frac { 1 } { 19 } \left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 2 } & { 3 } \\ { 5 } & { - 2 } \end{array} \right] = \frac { 1 } { 19 } A{/tex}

Solve the system of equations
{tex} \frac{2}{x} + \frac{3}{y} + \frac{{10}}{z} = 4{/tex}
{tex} \frac{4}{x} - \frac{6}{y} + \frac{5}{z} = 1{/tex}
{tex} \frac{6}{x} + \frac{9}{y} - \frac{{ 20}}{z} = 2{/tex}
question_text: Solve the system of equations {tex} \frac{2}{x} + \frac{3}{y} + \frac{{10}}{z} = 4{/tex} {tex} \frac{4}{x} - \frac{6}{y} + \frac{5}{z} = 1{/tex} {tex} \frac{6}{x} + \frac{9}{y} - \frac{{ 20}}{z} = 2{/tex}
explanation: Let {tex} \frac{1}{x} = u,\frac{1}{y} = v{/tex} and {tex} \frac{1}{z} = w{/tex} 2u + 3v + 10w = 4 4u – 6v+ 5w = 1 6u + 9v – 20w = 2 {tex} A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3&{10} \\ 4&{ - 6}&5 \\ 6&9&{ - 20} \end{array}} \right],X= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} u \\ v \\ w \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Now, {tex} \begin{array}{l}\vert A\vert=\begin{vmatrix}2&3&10\\4&-6&5\\6&9&-20\end{vmatrix}\\\end{array}{/tex} = 2[120 - 45] -3[-80 - 30] +10[36 + 36] = 150 + 330 + 720 = 1200 {tex} \neq0{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} A is non-singular and hence A -1 exists. Now, A 11 = 75, A 12 = 110, A 13 = 72 A 21 = 150, A 22 = -100, A 23 = 0 A 31 = 75, A 32 = 30, A 33 = -2 {tex} \therefore adjA = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {75}&{150}&{75} \\ {110}&{ - 100}&{30} \\ {72}&0&{ - 24} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}(adjA) = \frac{1}{{1200}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {75}&{150}&{75} \\ {110}&{ - 100}&{30} \\ {72}&0&{ - 24} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} X= {A^{ - 1}}B{/tex} {tex} = \frac{1}{{1200}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {600} \\ {400} \\ {240} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{1}{2}} \\ {\frac{1}{3}} \\ {\frac{1}{5}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} y \\ v \\ w \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{1}{2}} \\ {\frac{1}{3}} \\ {\frac{1}{5}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} u = \frac{1}{2},v = \frac{1}{3},w = \frac{1}{5}{/tex} {tex} \frac{1}{x} = \frac{1}{2},\frac{1}{y} = \frac{1}{3},\frac{1}{z} = \frac{1}{5}{/tex} x = 2, y = 3, z = 5
solution: Let {tex} \frac{1}{x} = u,\frac{1}{y} = v{/tex} and {tex} \frac{1}{z} = w{/tex} 2u + 3v + 10w = 4 4u – 6v+ 5w = 1 6u + 9v – 20w = 2 {tex} A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3&{10} \\ 4&{ - 6}&5 \\ 6&9&{ - 20} \end{array}} \right],X= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} u \\ v \\ w \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex} Now, {tex} \begin{array}{l}\vert A\vert=\begin{vmatrix}2&3&10\\4&-6&5\\6&9&-20\end{vmatrix}\\\end{array}{/tex} = 2[120 - 45] -3[-80 - 30] +10[36 + 36] = 150 + 330 + 720 = 1200 {tex} \neq0{/tex} {tex} \Rightarrow{/tex} A is non-singular and hence A -1 exists. Now, A 11 = 75, A 12 = 110, A 13 = 72 A 21 = 150, A 22 = -100, A 23 = 0 A 31 = 75, A 32 = 30, A 33 = -2 {tex} \therefore adjA = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {75}&{150}&{75} \\ {110}&{ - 100}&{30} \\ {72}&0&{ - 24} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}(adjA) = \frac{1}{{1200}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {75}&{150}&{75} \\ {110}&{ - 100}&{30} \\ {72}&0&{ - 24} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} X= {A^{ - 1}}B{/tex} {tex} = \frac{1}{{1200}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {600} \\ {400} \\ {240} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{1}{2}} \\ {\frac{1}{3}} \\ {\frac{1}{5}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} y \\ v \\ w \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{1}{2}} \\ {\frac{1}{3}} \\ {\frac{1}{5}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex} {tex} u = \frac{1}{2},v = \frac{1}{3},w = \frac{1}{5}{/tex} {tex} \frac{1}{x} = \frac{1}{2},\frac{1}{y} = \frac{1}{3},\frac{1}{z} = \frac{1}{5}{/tex} x = 2, y = 3, z = 5
solution_html:
Let {tex} \frac{1}{x} = u,\frac{1}{y} = v{/tex} and {tex} \frac{1}{z} = w{/tex}
2u + 3v + 10w = 4
4u – 6v+ 5w = 1
6u + 9v – 20w = 2
{tex} A = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 2&3&{10} \\ 4&{ - 6}&5 \\ 6&9&{ - 20} \end{array}} \right],X= \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} u \\ v \\ w \end{array}} \right]{/tex}{tex} B = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} 4 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}} \right]{/tex}

Now, {tex} \begin{array}{l}\vert A\vert=\begin{vmatrix}2&3&10\\4&-6&5\\6&9&-20\end{vmatrix}\\\end{array}{/tex}

= 2[120 - 45] -3[-80 - 30] +10[36 + 36]

= 150 + 330 + 720 = 1200{tex} \neq0{/tex}

{tex} \Rightarrow{/tex}A is non-singular and hence A-1 exists.

Now, A11 = 75, A12 = 110, A13 = 72
A21 = 150, A22 = -100, A23 = 0
A31 = 75, A32 = 30, A33 = -2

{tex} \therefore adjA = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {75}&{150}&{75} \\ {110}&{ - 100}&{30} \\ {72}&0&{ - 24} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} {A^{ - 1}} = \frac{1}{{\left| A \right|}}(adjA) = \frac{1}{{1200}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {75}&{150}&{75} \\ {110}&{ - 100}&{30} \\ {72}&0&{ - 24} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} X= {A^{ - 1}}B{/tex}
{tex} = \frac{1}{{1200}}\left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {600} \\ {400} \\ {240} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{1}{2}} \\ {\frac{1}{3}} \\ {\frac{1}{5}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} y \\ v \\ w \end{array}} \right] = \left[ {\begin{array}{*{20}{c}} {\frac{1}{2}} \\ {\frac{1}{3}} \\ {\frac{1}{5}} \end{array}} \right]{/tex}
{tex} u = \frac{1}{2},v = \frac{1}{3},w = \frac{1}{5}{/tex}

{tex} \frac{1}{x} = \frac{1}{2},\frac{1}{y} = \frac{1}{3},\frac{1}{z} = \frac{1}{5}{/tex}

x = 2, y = 3, z = 5
