question_html: If A is an invertible matrix of order 3 and |A| = 5, then find | adj A|.
question_text: If A is an invertible matrix of order 3 and |A| = 5, then find | adj A|.
explanation: We know that, for a non-singular square matrix of order n |adj(A)l=|A| n-1 Here, n = 3 and |A|=5 {tex}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} |adj A|= |A| 3-1 = |A| 2 = 5 2 = 25
solution: We know that, for a non-singular square matrix of order n |adj(A)l=|A| n-1 Here, n = 3 and |A|=5 {tex}{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} |adj A|= |A| 3-1 = |A| 2 = 5 2 = 25
solution_html:
We know that, for a non-singular square matrix of order n
|adj(A)l=|A|n-1
Here, n = 3 and |A|=5 {tex}{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} |adj A|= |A| 3-1 = |A| 2 = 52 = 25

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
Solve the system of equations by matrix method
x + y + z = 6 
x + 2z = 7
3 x + y + z = 12
question_text: Solve the system of equations by matrix method x + y + z = 6 x + 2z = 7 3 x + y + z = 12
explanation: The given system can be written in matrix form as: {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 0 & 2 \\ 3 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} \mathrm{x} \\ \mathrm{y} \\ \mathrm{z} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 6 \\ 17 \\ 12 \end{array}\right]{/tex} A X = B Now, |A| = {tex}1\left|\begin{array}{ll} 0 & 2 \\ 1 & 1 \end{array}\right|-1\left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 2 \\ 3 & 1 \end{array}\right|+1\left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 0 \\ 3 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 1(- 2) - 1(1 - 6) + 1(1) = - 2 + 5 + 1 = 4 So, the above system has a unique solution, given by X = A -1 B Cofactors of A are: C 11 = (- 1) 1 + 1 0 - 2 = - 2 C 21 = (- 1) 2 + 1 1 - 1 = 0 C 31 = (- 1) 3 + 1 2 - 0 = 2 C 12 = (- 1) 1 + 2 1 - 6 = 5 C 22 = (- 1) 2 + 1 1 - 3 = - 2 C 32 = (- 1) 3 + 1 2 - 1 = - 1 C 13 = (- 1) 1 + 2 1 - 0 = 1 C 23 = (- 1) 2 + 1 1 - 3 = 2 C 33 = (- 1) 3 + 1 0 - 1 = - 1 {tex}\operatorname{adj} A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & 5 & 1 \\ 0 & -2 & 2 \\ 2 & -1 & -1 \end{array}\right]^{T}{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & 0 & 2 \\ 5 & -2 & -1 \\ 1 & 2 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}A^{-1}=\frac{1}{|A|} \operatorname{adj} A{/tex} Now, X = A –1 B = {tex}\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & 0 & 2 \\ 5 & -2 & -1 \\ 1 & 2 & -1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 6 \\ 7 \\ 12 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}x=\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{c} -12+0+24 \\ 30-14-12 \\ 6+14-12 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}x=\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{l} 12 \\ 4 \\ 8 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}x=\left[\begin{array}{l} 3 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Hence, X = 3, Y = 1 and Z = 2
solution: The given system can be written in matrix form as: {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 0 & 2 \\ 3 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} \mathrm{x} \\ \mathrm{y} \\ \mathrm{z} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 6 \\ 17 \\ 12 \end{array}\right]{/tex} A X = B Now, |A| = {tex}1\left|\begin{array}{ll} 0 & 2 \\ 1 & 1 \end{array}\right|-1\left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 2 \\ 3 & 1 \end{array}\right|+1\left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 0 \\ 3 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 1(- 2) - 1(1 - 6) + 1(1) = - 2 + 5 + 1 = 4 So, the above system has a unique solution, given by X = A -1 B Cofactors of A are: C 11 = (- 1) 1 + 1 0 - 2 = - 2 C 21 = (- 1) 2 + 1 1 - 1 = 0 C 31 = (- 1) 3 + 1 2 - 0 = 2 C 12 = (- 1) 1 + 2 1 - 6 = 5 C 22 = (- 1) 2 + 1 1 - 3 = - 2 C 32 = (- 1) 3 + 1 2 - 1 = - 1 C 13 = (- 1) 1 + 2 1 - 0 = 1 C 23 = (- 1) 2 + 1 1 - 3 = 2 C 33 = (- 1) 3 + 1 0 - 1 = - 1 {tex}\operatorname{adj} A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & 5 & 1 \\ 0 & -2 & 2 \\ 2 & -1 & -1 \end{array}\right]^{T}{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & 0 & 2 \\ 5 & -2 & -1 \\ 1 & 2 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}A^{-1}=\frac{1}{|A|} \operatorname{adj} A{/tex} Now, X = A –1 B = {tex}\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & 0 & 2 \\ 5 & -2 & -1 \\ 1 & 2 & -1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 6 \\ 7 \\ 12 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}x=\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{c} -12+0+24 \\ 30-14-12 \\ 6+14-12 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}x=\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{l} 12 \\ 4 \\ 8 \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}x=\left[\begin{array}{l} 3 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Hence, X = 3, Y = 1 and Z = 2
solution_html:
The given system can be written in matrix form as:
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 0 & 2 \\ 3 & 1 & 1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} \mathrm{x} \\ \mathrm{y} \\ \mathrm{z} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 6 \\ 17 \\ 12 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
A X = B
Now, |A| = {tex}1\left|\begin{array}{ll} 0 & 2 \\ 1 & 1 \end{array}\right|-1\left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 2 \\ 3 & 1 \end{array}\right|+1\left|\begin{array}{ll} 1 & 0 \\ 3 & 1 \end{array}\right|{/tex}
= 1(- 2) - 1(1 - 6) + 1(1)
= - 2 + 5 + 1
= 4
So, the above system has a unique solution, given by
X = A-1B
Cofactors of A are:
C11 = (- 1)1 + 1 0 - 2 = - 2
C21 = (- 1)2 + 1 1 - 1 = 0
C31 = (- 1)3 + 1 2 - 0 = 2
C12 = (- 1)1 + 2 1 - 6 = 5
C22 = (- 1)2 + 1 1 - 3 = - 2
C32 = (- 1)3 + 1 2 - 1 = - 1
C13 = (- 1)1 + 2 1 - 0 = 1
C23 = (- 1)2 + 1 1 - 3 = 2
C33 = (- 1)3 + 1 0 - 1 = - 1
{tex}\operatorname{adj} A=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & 5 & 1 \\ 0 & -2 & 2 \\ 2 & -1 & -1 \end{array}\right]^{T}{/tex}
{tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & 0 & 2 \\ 5 & -2 & -1 \\ 1 & 2 & -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}A^{-1}=\frac{1}{|A|} \operatorname{adj} A{/tex}
Now, X = A–1B = {tex}\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} -2 & 0 & 2 \\ 5 & -2 & -1 \\ 1 & 2 & -1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} 6 \\ 7 \\ 12 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}x=\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{c} -12+0+24 \\ 30-14-12 \\ 6+14-12 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}x=\frac{1}{4}\left[\begin{array}{l} 12 \\ 4 \\ 8 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}x=\left[\begin{array}{l} 3 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Hence, X = 3, Y = 1 and Z = 2

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
Using matrix method, solve the system of equations
2x + 8y + 5z = 5;
x + y + z = -2;
x + 2y - z = 2
question_text: Using matrix method, solve the system of equations 2x + 8y + 5z = 5; x + y + z = -2; x + 2y - z = 2
explanation: Given set of lines are:- 2x + 8y + 5z = 5; x + y + z = -2 x + 2y - z = 2 Converting the following equations in matrix form, AX = B {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 8 & 5 \\ 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 2 & -1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 5 \\ -2 \\ 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} 2R 2 - R 1 2R 3 - R 1 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 8 & 5 \\ 0 & -6 & -3 \\ 0 & -4 & -7 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 5 \\ -9 \\ -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} 3R 3 - 2R 2 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 8 & 5 \\ 0 & -6 & -3 \\ 0 & 0 & -15 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 5 \\ -9 \\ 15 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Again converting into equations, we get 2x + 8y + 5z = 5 -6y - 3z = -9 -15z = 15 Z = -1 -6y - 3 {tex}\times{/tex} -1 = -9 -6y = -9 - 3 Y = 2 2x + 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 + 5 {tex}\times{/tex} -1 = 5 2x = 5 - 16 + 5 x = -3 {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = -3, y = 2, z = -1.
solution: Given set of lines are:- 2x + 8y + 5z = 5; x + y + z = -2 x + 2y - z = 2 Converting the following equations in matrix form, AX = B {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 8 & 5 \\ 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 2 & -1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 5 \\ -2 \\ 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} 2R 2 - R 1 2R 3 - R 1 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 8 & 5 \\ 0 & -6 & -3 \\ 0 & -4 & -7 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 5 \\ -9 \\ -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} 3R 3 - 2R 2 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 8 & 5 \\ 0 & -6 & -3 \\ 0 & 0 & -15 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 5 \\ -9 \\ 15 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Again converting into equations, we get 2x + 8y + 5z = 5 -6y - 3z = -9 -15z = 15 Z = -1 -6y - 3 {tex}\times{/tex} -1 = -9 -6y = -9 - 3 Y = 2 2x + 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 + 5 {tex}\times{/tex} -1 = 5 2x = 5 - 16 + 5 x = -3 {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = -3, y = 2, z = -1.
solution_html:
Given set of lines are:-
2x + 8y + 5z = 5;
x + y + z = -2
x + 2y - z = 2
Converting the following equations in matrix form,
AX = B
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 8 & 5 \\ 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 1 & 2 & -1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 5 \\ -2 \\ 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
2R2 - R1
2R3 - R1
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 8 & 5 \\ 0 & -6 & -3 \\ 0 & -4 & -7 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 5 \\ -9 \\ -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
3R3 - 2R2
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 8 & 5 \\ 0 & -6 & -3 \\ 0 & 0 & -15 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 5 \\ -9 \\ 15 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Again converting into equations, we get
2x + 8y + 5z = 5
-6y - 3z = -9
-15z = 15
Z = -1
-6y - 3 {tex}\times{/tex} -1 = -9
-6y = -9 - 3
Y = 2
2x + 8 {tex}\times{/tex} 2 + 5 {tex}\times{/tex} -1 = 5
2x = 5 - 16 + 5
x = -3
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = -3, y = 2, z = -1.

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
Using matrix method, solve the system of equations
2x - 3y + 5z = 11;
3x + 2y - 4z = -5;
x + y - 2z = -3.
question_text: Using matrix method, solve the system of equations 2x - 3y + 5z = 11; 3x + 2y - 4z = -5; x + y - 2z = -3.
explanation: Given set of lines are : - 2x - 3y + 5z = 11; 3x + 2y - 4z = - 5; x + y - 2z = - 3. Converting the following equations in matrix form, AX = B {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & -3 & 5 \\ 3 & 2 & -4 \\ 1 & 1 & -2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 11 \\ -5 \\ -3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} 2R 2 - 3R 1 2R 3 - R 1 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & -3 & 5 \\ 0 & 13 & -23 \\ 0 & 5 & -9 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 11 \\ -43 \\ -17 \end{array}\right]{/tex} 13R 3 - 5R 2 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & -3 & 5 \\ 0 & 13 & -23 \\ 0 & 0 & -2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 11 \\ -43 \\ -6 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Again converting into equations we get, 2x - 3y + 5z = 11 13y - 23 z = - 43 -2z = - 6 z = 3 13y - 23×3 = - 43 13y = - 43 + 69 13y = 26 y = 2 2x - 3×2 + 5×3 = 11 2x = 11 + 6 - 15 x = 1 {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 1, y = 2, z = 3.
solution: Given set of lines are : - 2x - 3y + 5z = 11; 3x + 2y - 4z = - 5; x + y - 2z = - 3. Converting the following equations in matrix form, AX = B {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & -3 & 5 \\ 3 & 2 & -4 \\ 1 & 1 & -2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 11 \\ -5 \\ -3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} 2R 2 - 3R 1 2R 3 - R 1 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & -3 & 5 \\ 0 & 13 & -23 \\ 0 & 5 & -9 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 11 \\ -43 \\ -17 \end{array}\right]{/tex} 13R 3 - 5R 2 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & -3 & 5 \\ 0 & 13 & -23 \\ 0 & 0 & -2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 11 \\ -43 \\ -6 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Again converting into equations we get, 2x - 3y + 5z = 11 13y - 23 z = - 43 -2z = - 6 z = 3 13y - 23×3 = - 43 13y = - 43 + 69 13y = 26 y = 2 2x - 3×2 + 5×3 = 11 2x = 11 + 6 - 15 x = 1 {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 1, y = 2, z = 3.
solution_html:
Given set of lines are : -
2x - 3y + 5z = 11;
3x + 2y - 4z = - 5;
x + y - 2z = - 3.
Converting the following equations in matrix form,
AX = B
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & -3 & 5 \\ 3 & 2 & -4 \\ 1 & 1 & -2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 11 \\ -5 \\ -3 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
2R2 - 3R1
2R3 - R1
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & -3 & 5 \\ 0 & 13 & -23 \\ 0 & 5 & -9 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 11 \\ -43 \\ -17 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
13R3 - 5R2
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & -3 & 5 \\ 0 & 13 & -23 \\ 0 & 0 & -2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 11 \\ -43 \\ -6 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Again converting into equations we get,
2x - 3y + 5z = 11
13y - 23 z = - 43
-2z = - 6
z = 3
13y - 23×3 = - 43
13y = - 43 + 69
13y = 26
y = 2
2x - 3×2 + 5×3 = 11
2x = 11 + 6 - 15
x = 1
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 1, y = 2, z = 3.

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Evaluate the determinant {tex}\left|\begin{array}{rr} {a+i b} & {c+i d} \\ {-c+i d} & {a-i b} \end{array}\right|{/tex}
question_text: Evaluate the determinant {tex}\left|\begin{array}{rr} {a+i b} & {c+i d} \\ {-c+i d} & {a-i b} \end{array}\right|{/tex}
explanation: Let A = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{cc} {a+i b} & {c+i d} \\ {-c+i d} & {a-i b} \end{array}\right|{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |A| = (a + ib)(a - ib) - (c + id)(-c + id) = (a + ib)(a - ib) + (c + id)(c - id) = a 2 - i 2 b 2 + c 2 - i 2 d 2 = a 2 - (-1)b 2 + c 2 - (-1)d 2 = a 2 + b 2 + c 2 + d 2 Thus, |A| = a 2 + b 2 + c 2 + d 2
solution: Let A = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{cc} {a+i b} & {c+i d} \\ {-c+i d} & {a-i b} \end{array}\right|{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |A| = (a + ib)(a - ib) - (c + id)(-c + id) = (a + ib)(a - ib) + (c + id)(c - id) = a 2 - i 2 b 2 + c 2 - i 2 d 2 = a 2 - (-1)b 2 + c 2 - (-1)d 2 = a 2 + b 2 + c 2 + d 2 Thus, |A| = a 2 + b 2 + c 2 + d 2
solution_html:
Let A = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{cc} {a+i b} & {c+i d} \\ {-c+i d} & {a-i b} \end{array}\right|{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} |A| = (a + ib)(a - ib) - (c + id)(-c + id)
= (a + ib)(a - ib) + (c + id)(c - id)
= a2 - i2 b2 + c2 - i2 d2
= a2 - (-1)b2 + c2 - (-1)d2
= a2 + b2 + c2 + d2
Thus, |A| =  a2 + b2 + c2 + d2

subject: Mathematics
question_html: Write the cofactor of a12 in the matrix {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 2 & -3 & 5 \\ 6 & 0 & 4 \\ 1 & 5 & -7 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
question_text: Write the cofactor of a 12 in the matrix {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 2 & -3 & 5 \\ 6 & 0 & 4 \\ 1 & 5 & -7 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
explanation: Given the matrix is, {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 2 & -3 & 5 \\ 6 & 0 & 4 \\ 1 & 5 & -7 \end{array}\right]{/tex} We need to find the cofactor of a 12 in the matrix {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & -3 & 5 \\ 6 & 0 & 4 \\ 1 & 5 & -7 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Firstly we know what the element at position a 12 in the matrix is. a 12 = -3 And as discussed above, the sign at a 12 is (-). For cofactor of -3, eliminate first row and second column in the matrix. Cofactor of -3 = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{cc} 6 & 4 \\ 1 & -7 \end{array}\right|{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Cofactor of -3 = (6 {tex}\times{/tex} -7) – (4 {tex}\times{/tex} 1) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Cofactor of -3 = -42 – 4 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Cofactor of -3 = -46 Since, the sign of cofactor of -3 is (-), then Cofactor of -3 = -(-46) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Cofactor of -3 = 46 Thus, the cofactor of -3 is 46.
solution: Given the matrix is, {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 2 & -3 & 5 \\ 6 & 0 & 4 \\ 1 & 5 & -7 \end{array}\right]{/tex} We need to find the cofactor of a 12 in the matrix {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & -3 & 5 \\ 6 & 0 & 4 \\ 1 & 5 & -7 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Firstly we know what the element at position a 12 in the matrix is. a 12 = -3 And as discussed above, the sign at a 12 is (-). For cofactor of -3, eliminate first row and second column in the matrix. Cofactor of -3 = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{cc} 6 & 4 \\ 1 & -7 \end{array}\right|{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Cofactor of -3 = (6 {tex}\times{/tex} -7) – (4 {tex}\times{/tex} 1) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Cofactor of -3 = -42 – 4 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Cofactor of -3 = -46 Since, the sign of cofactor of -3 is (-), then Cofactor of -3 = -(-46) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Cofactor of -3 = 46 Thus, the cofactor of -3 is 46.
solution_html:
Given the matrix is, {tex}\left[\begin{array}{rrr} 2 & -3 & 5 \\ 6 & 0 & 4 \\ 1 & 5 & -7 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
We need to find the cofactor of a12 in the matrix
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & -3 & 5 \\ 6 & 0 & 4 \\ 1 & 5 & -7 \end{array}\right]{/tex} 
Firstly we know what the element at position a12 in the matrix is. 
a12 = -3
And as discussed above, the sign at a12 is (-).
For cofactor of -3, eliminate first row and second column in the matrix.
Cofactor of -3 = {tex}\left|\begin{array}{cc} 6 & 4 \\ 1 & -7 \end{array}\right|{/tex} 
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Cofactor of -3 = (6 {tex}\times{/tex} -7) – (4 {tex}\times{/tex} 1)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Cofactor of -3 = -42 – 4
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Cofactor of -3 = -46
Since, the sign of cofactor of -3 is (-), then
Cofactor of -3 = -(-46)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} Cofactor of -3 = 46
Thus, the cofactor of -3 is 46.

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}\left|\begin{array}{cc} 2 x+5 & 3 \\ 5 x+2 & 9 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 0, find x.
question_text: If {tex}\left|\begin{array}{cc} 2 x+5 & 3 \\ 5 x+2 & 9 \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 0, find x.
explanation: Given that; {tex}\left|\begin{array}{cc} 2 x+5 & 3 \\ 5 x+2 & 9 \end{array}\right| = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 9(2x + 5) – 3(5x + 2) = 0 by expanding the determinent, we get, {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 18x + 45 – 15x – 6 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x + 39 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x = - 39 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = -13
solution: Given that; {tex}\left|\begin{array}{cc} 2 x+5 & 3 \\ 5 x+2 & 9 \end{array}\right| = 0{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 9(2x + 5) – 3(5x + 2) = 0 by expanding the determinent, we get, {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 18x + 45 – 15x – 6 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x + 39 = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x = - 39 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = -13
solution_html:
Given that;
{tex}\left|\begin{array}{cc} 2 x+5 & 3 \\ 5 x+2 & 9 \end{array}\right| = 0{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 9(2x + 5) – 3(5x + 2) = 0 by expanding the determinent, we get,
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 18x + 45 – 15x – 6 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x + 39 = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} 3x = - 39
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = -13

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
Using matrix method, solve the system of equations
x - y = 3;
2x + 3y + 4z = 17;
y + 2z = 7.
question_text: Using matrix method, solve the system of equations x - y = 3; 2x + 3y + 4z = 17; y + 2z = 7.
explanation: To find:- x, y, z Given set of equations are:- x - y = 3 2x + 3y + 4z = 17 y + 2z = 7 Converting the following set of equations in matrix form, we have AX = B {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & -1 & 0 \\ 2 & 3 & 4 \\ 0 & 1 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 3 \\ 17 \\ 7 \end{array}\right]{/tex} R 2 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} R 2 - 2R 1 {tex}\begin{aligned} &\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & -1 & 0 \\ 0 & 5 & 4 \\ 0 & 1 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 3 \\ 11 \\ 7 \end{array}\right]\\ \end{aligned}{/tex} R 3 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 2R 3 - R 2 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & -1 & 0 \\ 0 & 5 & 4 \\ 0 & -3 & 0 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 3 \\ 11 \\ 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Again converting into equations we get x - y = 3 5y + 4z = 11 -3y = 3 y = - 1 5× - 1 + 4z = 11 4z = 16 z = 4 x + 1 = 3 x = 2 {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 2, y = -1, z = 4
solution: To find:- x, y, z Given set of equations are:- x - y = 3 2x + 3y + 4z = 17 y + 2z = 7 Converting the following set of equations in matrix form, we have AX = B {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & -1 & 0 \\ 2 & 3 & 4 \\ 0 & 1 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 3 \\ 17 \\ 7 \end{array}\right]{/tex} R 2 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} R 2 - 2R 1 {tex}\begin{aligned} &\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & -1 & 0 \\ 0 & 5 & 4 \\ 0 & 1 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 3 \\ 11 \\ 7 \end{array}\right]\\ \end{aligned}{/tex} R 3 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 2R 3 - R 2 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & -1 & 0 \\ 0 & 5 & 4 \\ 0 & -3 & 0 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 3 \\ 11 \\ 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Again converting into equations we get x - y = 3 5y + 4z = 11 -3y = 3 y = - 1 5× - 1 + 4z = 11 4z = 16 z = 4 x + 1 = 3 x = 2 {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 2, y = -1, z = 4
solution_html:
To find:- x, y, z
Given set of equations are:-
x - y = 3
2x + 3y + 4z = 17
y + 2z = 7
Converting the following set of  equations in matrix form, we have
AX = B
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & -1 & 0 \\ 2 & 3 & 4 \\ 0 & 1 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 3 \\ 17 \\ 7 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
R2 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex}R2 - 2R1
{tex}\begin{aligned} &\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & -1 & 0 \\ 0 & 5 & 4 \\ 0 & 1 & 2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 3 \\ 11 \\ 7 \end{array}\right]\\ \end{aligned}{/tex}
R3 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex}2R3 - R2
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & -1 & 0 \\ 0 & 5 & 4 \\ 0 & -3 & 0 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 3 \\ 11 \\ 3 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Again converting into equations we get
x - y = 3
5y + 4z = 11
-3y = 3
y = - 1
5× - 1 + 4z = 11
4z = 16
z = 4
x + 1 = 3
x = 2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 2, y = -1, z = 4

subject: Mathematics
question_html: If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ll} {3} & {-2} \\ {4} & {-2} \end{array}\right]{/tex}, find the value of A, so that A2 = {tex}\lambda{/tex}A - 2l. Hence, find A-1
question_text: If {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ll} {3} & {-2} \\ {4} & {-2} \end{array}\right]{/tex} , find the value of A, so that A 2 = {tex}\lambda{/tex} A - 2l. Hence, find A -1
explanation: Given: {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ll} {3} & {-2} \\ {4} & {-2} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}A^{2}=\left[\begin{array}{cc} {3} & {-2} \\ {4} & {-2} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} {3} & {-2} \\ {4} & {-2} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} {9-8} & {-6+4} \\ {12-8} & {-8+4} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} {1} & {-2} \\ {4} & {-4} \end{array}\right]{/tex} Now, A 2 = {tex}\lambda{/tex} A – 2I {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\lambda{/tex} A = A 2 + 2I {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{cc} {1} & {-2} \\ {4} & {-4} \end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{ll} {2} & {0} \\ {0} & {2} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} {3} & {-2} \\ {4} & {-2} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\lambda{\left[\begin{array}{ll} {3} & {-2} \\ {4} & {-2} \end{array}\right]}=\left[\begin{array}{ll} {3} & {-2} \\ {4} & {-2} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ll} {3 \lambda} & {-2 \lambda} \\ {4 \lambda} & {-2 \lambda} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll} {3} & {-2} \\ {4} & {-2} \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}3\lambda{/tex} = 3 or {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 1 So, A 2 = A – 2I Multiply by A – 1 both sides = A.A. A – 1 = A. A – 1 – 2I. A – 1 = 0 = 2A – 1 = {tex}I-A=\left[\begin{array}{ll} {1} & {0} \\ {0} & {1} \end{array}\right]-\left[\begin{array}{ll} {3} & {-2} \\ {4} & {-2} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll} {-2} & {2} \\ {-4} & {3} \end{array}\right]{/tex} Hence, A – 1 = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \cdot\left[\begin{array}{cc} {-2} & {2} \\ {-4} & {3} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
solution: Given: {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ll} {3} & {-2} \\ {4} & {-2} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}A^{2}=\left[\begin{array}{cc} {3} & {-2} \\ {4} & {-2} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} {3} & {-2} \\ {4} & {-2} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} {9-8} & {-6+4} \\ {12-8} & {-8+4} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} {1} & {-2} \\ {4} & {-4} \end{array}\right]{/tex} Now, A 2 = {tex}\lambda{/tex} A – 2I {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} {tex}\lambda{/tex} A = A 2 + 2I {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{cc} {1} & {-2} \\ {4} & {-4} \end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{ll} {2} & {0} \\ {0} & {2} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} {3} & {-2} \\ {4} & {-2} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\lambda{\left[\begin{array}{ll} {3} & {-2} \\ {4} & {-2} \end{array}\right]}=\left[\begin{array}{ll} {3} & {-2} \\ {4} & {-2} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ll} {3 \lambda} & {-2 \lambda} \\ {4 \lambda} & {-2 \lambda} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll} {3} & {-2} \\ {4} & {-2} \end{array}\right]{/tex} = {tex}3\lambda{/tex} = 3 or {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 1 So, A 2 = A – 2I Multiply by A – 1 both sides = A.A. A – 1 = A. A – 1 – 2I. A – 1 = 0 = 2A – 1 = {tex}I-A=\left[\begin{array}{ll} {1} & {0} \\ {0} & {1} \end{array}\right]-\left[\begin{array}{ll} {3} & {-2} \\ {4} & {-2} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll} {-2} & {2} \\ {-4} & {3} \end{array}\right]{/tex} Hence, A – 1 = {tex}\frac{1}{2} \cdot\left[\begin{array}{cc} {-2} & {2} \\ {-4} & {3} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
solution_html:
Given: {tex}A=\left[\begin{array}{ll} {3} & {-2} \\ {4} & {-2} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}A^{2}=\left[\begin{array}{cc} {3} & {-2} \\ {4} & {-2} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{cc} {3} & {-2} \\ {4} & {-2} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} {9-8} & {-6+4} \\ {12-8} & {-8+4} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} {1} & {-2} \\ {4} & {-4} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Now, A2 = {tex}\lambda{/tex}A – 2I
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}{tex}\lambda{/tex}A = A2 + 2I
{tex}=\left[\begin{array}{cc} {1} & {-2} \\ {4} & {-4} \end{array}\right]+\left[\begin{array}{ll} {2} & {0} \\ {0} & {2} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{cc} {3} & {-2} \\ {4} & {-2} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}=\lambda{\left[\begin{array}{ll} {3} & {-2} \\ {4} & {-2} \end{array}\right]}=\left[\begin{array}{ll} {3} & {-2} \\ {4} & {-2} \end{array}\right]{/tex}

{tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ll} {3 \lambda} & {-2 \lambda} \\ {4 \lambda} & {-2 \lambda} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll} {3} & {-2} \\ {4} & {-2} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
= {tex}3\lambda{/tex} = 3 or {tex}\lambda{/tex} = 1
So, A2 = A – 2I
Multiply by A – 1 both sides
= A.A. A – 1 = A. A – 1 – 2I. A – 1 = 0
= 2A – 1 = {tex}I-A=\left[\begin{array}{ll} {1} & {0} \\ {0} & {1} \end{array}\right]-\left[\begin{array}{ll} {3} & {-2} \\ {4} & {-2} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{ll} {-2} & {2} \\ {-4} & {3} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Hence, A – 1 =  {tex}\frac{1}{2} \cdot\left[\begin{array}{cc} {-2} & {2} \\ {-4} & {3} \end{array}\right]{/tex}

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
Solve the system of equations by matrix method:
{tex}\frac{2}{x}-\frac{3}{y}+\frac{3}{z}=10{/tex}
{tex}\frac{1}{x}+\frac{1}{y}+\frac{1}{z}=10{/tex}
{tex}\frac{3}{x}-\frac{1}{y}+\frac{2}{z}=13{/tex}
question_text: Solve the system of equations by matrix method: {tex}\frac{2}{x}-\frac{3}{y}+\frac{3}{z}=10{/tex} {tex}\frac{1}{x}+\frac{1}{y}+\frac{1}{z}=10{/tex} {tex}\frac{3}{x}-\frac{1}{y}+\frac{2}{z}=13{/tex}
explanation: The given system can be written in matrix form as: {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} {5} & {3} & {1} \\ {2} & {1} & {3} \\ {1} & {2} & {4} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} {16} \\ {19} \\ {25} \end{array}\right]{/tex} or AX = B A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} {5} & {3} & {1} \\ {2} & {1} & {3} \\ {1} & {2} & {4} \end{array}\right]{/tex} , X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} {\mathrm{X}} \\ {\mathrm{y}} \\ {\mathrm{z}} \end{array}\right] {/tex} and B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} {16} \\ {19} \\ {25} \end{array}\right]{/tex} Now, |A| = {tex}5\left|\begin{array}{ll} {1} & {3} \\ {2} & {4} \end{array}\right|-3\left|\begin{array}{ll} {2} & {3} \\ {1} & {4} \end{array}\right|+1\left|\begin{array}{ll} {2} & {1} \\ {1} & {2} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 5(4 - 6) - 3(8 - 3) + 1(4 - 1) = - 10 - 15 + 3 = - 22 So, the above system has a unique solution, given by X = A -1 B Cofactors of A are: C 11 = (-1) 1 + 1 (4 - 6) = - 2 C 21 = (-1) 2 + 1 (12 - 2) = - 10 C 31 = (-1) 3 + 1 (9 - 1) = 8 C 12 = (-1) 1 + 2 (8 - 3) = - 5 C 22 = (-1) 2 + 1 (20 - 1) = 19 C 32 = (-1) 3 + 1 (15 - 2) = - 13 C 13 = (-1) 1 + 2 (4 - 2) = 2 C 23 = (-1) 2 + 1 (10 - 3) = - 7 C 33 = (-1) 3 + 1 (5 - 6) = - 1 adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-2} & {-5} & {-3} \\ {-10} & {19} & {-7} \\ {8} & {-13} & {-1} \end{array}\right]^{T}{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-2} & {-10} & {8} \\ {-5} & {19} & {13} \\ {3} & {-7} & {-1} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}A^{-1}=\frac{1}{|A|} \text { adj } A{/tex} Now, {tex}X=A^{-1} B=\frac{1}{-22}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-2} & {-10} & {8} \\ {-5} & {19} & {-13} \\ {3} & {-7} & {-1} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} {16} \\ {19} \\ {25} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}x=\frac{1}{-22}\left[\begin{array}{c} {-32-190+200} \\ {-80+361-325} \\ {48-133-25} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}x=\frac{1}{-22}\left[\begin{array}{l} {-22} \\ {-44} \\ {-110} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}x=\left[\begin{array}{l} {1} \\ {2} \\ {5} \end{array}\right]{/tex} Hence, X = 1,Y = 2 and Z = 5
solution: The given system can be written in matrix form as: {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} {5} & {3} & {1} \\ {2} & {1} & {3} \\ {1} & {2} & {4} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} {16} \\ {19} \\ {25} \end{array}\right]{/tex} or AX = B A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} {5} & {3} & {1} \\ {2} & {1} & {3} \\ {1} & {2} & {4} \end{array}\right]{/tex} , X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} {\mathrm{X}} \\ {\mathrm{y}} \\ {\mathrm{z}} \end{array}\right] {/tex} and B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} {16} \\ {19} \\ {25} \end{array}\right]{/tex} Now, |A| = {tex}5\left|\begin{array}{ll} {1} & {3} \\ {2} & {4} \end{array}\right|-3\left|\begin{array}{ll} {2} & {3} \\ {1} & {4} \end{array}\right|+1\left|\begin{array}{ll} {2} & {1} \\ {1} & {2} \end{array}\right|{/tex} = 5(4 - 6) - 3(8 - 3) + 1(4 - 1) = - 10 - 15 + 3 = - 22 So, the above system has a unique solution, given by X = A -1 B Cofactors of A are: C 11 = (-1) 1 + 1 (4 - 6) = - 2 C 21 = (-1) 2 + 1 (12 - 2) = - 10 C 31 = (-1) 3 + 1 (9 - 1) = 8 C 12 = (-1) 1 + 2 (8 - 3) = - 5 C 22 = (-1) 2 + 1 (20 - 1) = 19 C 32 = (-1) 3 + 1 (15 - 2) = - 13 C 13 = (-1) 1 + 2 (4 - 2) = 2 C 23 = (-1) 2 + 1 (10 - 3) = - 7 C 33 = (-1) 3 + 1 (5 - 6) = - 1 adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-2} & {-5} & {-3} \\ {-10} & {19} & {-7} \\ {8} & {-13} & {-1} \end{array}\right]^{T}{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-2} & {-10} & {8} \\ {-5} & {19} & {13} \\ {3} & {-7} & {-1} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}A^{-1}=\frac{1}{|A|} \text { adj } A{/tex} Now, {tex}X=A^{-1} B=\frac{1}{-22}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-2} & {-10} & {8} \\ {-5} & {19} & {-13} \\ {3} & {-7} & {-1} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} {16} \\ {19} \\ {25} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}x=\frac{1}{-22}\left[\begin{array}{c} {-32-190+200} \\ {-80+361-325} \\ {48-133-25} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}x=\frac{1}{-22}\left[\begin{array}{l} {-22} \\ {-44} \\ {-110} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}x=\left[\begin{array}{l} {1} \\ {2} \\ {5} \end{array}\right]{/tex} Hence, X = 1,Y = 2 and Z = 5
solution_html:
The given system can be written in matrix form as:
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} {5} & {3} & {1} \\ {2} & {1} & {3} \\ {1} & {2} & {4} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{l} {16} \\ {19} \\ {25} \end{array}\right]{/tex} or AX = B 
A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{lll} {5} & {3} & {1} \\ {2} & {1} & {3} \\ {1} & {2} & {4} \end{array}\right]{/tex}, X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} {\mathrm{X}} \\ {\mathrm{y}} \\ {\mathrm{z}} \end{array}\right] {/tex} and B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} {16} \\ {19} \\ {25} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Now, |A| = {tex}5\left|\begin{array}{ll} {1} & {3} \\ {2} & {4} \end{array}\right|-3\left|\begin{array}{ll} {2} & {3} \\ {1} & {4} \end{array}\right|+1\left|\begin{array}{ll} {2} & {1} \\ {1} & {2} \end{array}\right|{/tex}
= 5(4 - 6) - 3(8 - 3) + 1(4 - 1)
= - 10 - 15 + 3
= - 22
So, the above system has a unique solution, given by
X = A-1B
Cofactors of A are:
C11 = (-1)1 + 1 (4 - 6) = - 2
C21 = (-1)2 + 1(12 - 2) = - 10
C31 = (-1)3 + 1(9 - 1) = 8
C12 = (-1)1 + 2 (8 - 3) = - 5
C22 = (-1)2 + 1 (20 - 1) = 19
C32 = (-1)3 + 1 (15 - 2) = - 13
C13 = (-1)1 + 2 (4 - 2) = 2
C23 = (-1)2 + 1 (10 - 3) = - 7
C33 = (-1)3 + 1 (5 - 6) = - 1
adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-2} & {-5} & {-3} \\ {-10} & {19} & {-7} \\ {8} & {-13} & {-1} \end{array}\right]^{T}{/tex}
{tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-2} & {-10} & {8} \\ {-5} & {19} & {13} \\ {3} & {-7} & {-1} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}A^{-1}=\frac{1}{|A|} \text { adj } A{/tex}
Now, {tex}X=A^{-1} B=\frac{1}{-22}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {-2} & {-10} & {8} \\ {-5} & {19} & {-13} \\ {3} & {-7} & {-1} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} {16} \\ {19} \\ {25} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}x=\frac{1}{-22}\left[\begin{array}{c} {-32-190+200} \\ {-80+361-325} \\ {48-133-25} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}x=\frac{1}{-22}\left[\begin{array}{l} {-22} \\ {-44} \\ {-110} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}x=\left[\begin{array}{l} {1} \\ {2} \\ {5} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Hence, X = 1,Y = 2 and Z = 5

question_html:
Using matrix method, solve the system of equations
4x - 5y - 11z = 12;
x - 3y + z = 1;
2x + 3y - 7z = 2.
question_text: Using matrix method, solve the system of equations 4x - 5y - 11z = 12; x - 3y + z = 1; 2x + 3y - 7z = 2.
explanation: Given set of lines are : - 4x - 5y - 11z = 12 X - 3y + z = 1; 2x + 3y - 7z = 2 Converting the following equations in matrix form, AX = B {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & -5 & -11 \\ 1 & -3 & 1 \\ 2 & 3 & -7 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 12 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} 4R 2 - R 1 2R 3 - R 1 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & -5 & -11 \\ 0 & -7 & 15 \\ 0 & 11 & -3 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 12 \\ -8 \\ -8 \end{array}\right]{/tex} 5R 3 + R 2 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & -5 & -11 \\ 0 & -7 & 15 \\ 0 & 48 & 0 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 12 \\ -8 \\ -48 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Again converting into equations we get 4x - 5y - 11z = 12 -7y + 15z = -8 48 y = -48 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = -1 7 + 15z = -8 15z = -15 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} z = -1 4x + 5 + 11 = 12 4x = 12 - 5 - 11 4x = -4 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = - 1 {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = -1, y = -1, z = -1
solution: Given set of lines are : - 4x - 5y - 11z = 12 X - 3y + z = 1; 2x + 3y - 7z = 2 Converting the following equations in matrix form, AX = B {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & -5 & -11 \\ 1 & -3 & 1 \\ 2 & 3 & -7 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 12 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} 4R 2 - R 1 2R 3 - R 1 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & -5 & -11 \\ 0 & -7 & 15 \\ 0 & 11 & -3 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 12 \\ -8 \\ -8 \end{array}\right]{/tex} 5R 3 + R 2 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & -5 & -11 \\ 0 & -7 & 15 \\ 0 & 48 & 0 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 12 \\ -8 \\ -48 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Again converting into equations we get 4x - 5y - 11z = 12 -7y + 15z = -8 48 y = -48 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = -1 7 + 15z = -8 15z = -15 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} z = -1 4x + 5 + 11 = 12 4x = 12 - 5 - 11 4x = -4 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = - 1 {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = -1, y = -1, z = -1
solution_html:
Given set of lines are : -
4x - 5y - 11z = 12
X - 3y + z = 1;
2x + 3y - 7z = 2
Converting the following equations in matrix form,
AX = B
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & -5 & -11 \\ 1 & -3 & 1 \\ 2 & 3 & -7 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 12 \\ 1 \\ 2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
4R2 - R1
2R3 - R1
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & -5 & -11 \\ 0 & -7 & 15 \\ 0 & 11 & -3 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 12 \\ -8 \\ -8 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
5R3 + R2
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 4 & -5 & -11 \\ 0 & -7 & 15 \\ 0 & 48 & 0 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 12 \\ -8 \\ -48 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Again converting into equations we get
4x - 5y - 11z = 12
-7y + 15z = -8
48 y = -48
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = -1
7 + 15z = -8
15z = -15
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} z = -1
4x + 5 + 11 = 12
4x = 12 - 5 - 11
4x = -4
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}x = - 1
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = -1, y = -1, z = -1

question_html: Show that A = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 2 } & { - 3 } \\ { 3 } & { 4 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} satisfies the equation x2 - 6x + 17 = 0. Hence, find A-1.
question_text: Show that A = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 2 } & { - 3 } \\ { 3 } & { 4 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} satisfies the equation x 2 - 6x + 17 = 0. Hence, find A -1 .
explanation: We have, A =​ {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 2 } & { - 3 } \\ { 3 } & { 4 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} A 2 = AA = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 2 } & { - 3 } \\ { 3 } & { 4 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 2 } & { - 3 } \\ { 3 } & { 4 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 4 - 9 } & { - 6 - 12 } \\ { 6 + 12 } & { - 9 + 16 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { r r } { - 5 } & { - 18 } \\ { 18 } & { 7 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} - 6 A = (- 6) {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 2 } & { - 3 } \\ { 3 } & { 4 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { r r } { - 12 } & { 18 } \\ { - 18 } & { - 24 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} and, 17 I = 17 {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 1 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 17 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 17 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} A 2 - 6A + 17 I 2 = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { r r } { - 5 } & { - 18 } \\ { 18 } & { 7 } \end{array} \right] + \left[ \begin{array} { r r } { - 12 } & { 18 } \\ { - 18 } & { - 24 } \end{array} \right] + \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 17 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 17 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A 2 - 6A + 17 I 2 = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { r r } { - 5 - 12 + 17 } & { - 18 + 18 + 0 } \\ { 18 - 18 + 0 } & { 7 - 24 + 17 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 0 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} = O Hence, the matrix A satisfies the equation x 2 - 6x +17 = 0. Now, A 2 - 6A + 17 I 2 = O {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A 2 - 6A = - 17 I 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A -1 (A 2 - 6A) = A -1 (- 17 I 2 ) [Pre-multiplying both sides by A -1 ] {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A -1 A 2 - 6A -1 A = - 17 (A -1 I 2 ) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A - 6 I 2 = - 17 A -1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A -1 = - {tex}\frac { 1 } { 17 }{/tex} (A - 6 I 2 ) = {tex}\frac { 1 } { 17 }{/tex} (6 I 2 - A) = {tex}\frac { 1 } { 17 }{/tex} {tex}\left\{ \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 6 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 6 } \end{array} \right] - \left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 2 } & { - 3 } \\ { 3 } & { 4 } \end{array} \right] \right\} = \frac { 1 } { 17 } \left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 4 } & { 3 } \\ { - 3 } & { 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
solution: We have, A =​ {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 2 } & { - 3 } \\ { 3 } & { 4 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} A 2 = AA = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 2 } & { - 3 } \\ { 3 } & { 4 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 2 } & { - 3 } \\ { 3 } & { 4 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 4 - 9 } & { - 6 - 12 } \\ { 6 + 12 } & { - 9 + 16 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { r r } { - 5 } & { - 18 } \\ { 18 } & { 7 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} - 6 A = (- 6) {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 2 } & { - 3 } \\ { 3 } & { 4 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { r r } { - 12 } & { 18 } \\ { - 18 } & { - 24 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} and, 17 I = 17 {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 1 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 17 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 17 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}\therefore{/tex} A 2 - 6A + 17 I 2 = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { r r } { - 5 } & { - 18 } \\ { 18 } & { 7 } \end{array} \right] + \left[ \begin{array} { r r } { - 12 } & { 18 } \\ { - 18 } & { - 24 } \end{array} \right] + \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 17 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 17 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A 2 - 6A + 17 I 2 = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { r r } { - 5 - 12 + 17 } & { - 18 + 18 + 0 } \\ { 18 - 18 + 0 } & { 7 - 24 + 17 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 0 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex} = O Hence, the matrix A satisfies the equation x 2 - 6x +17 = 0. Now, A 2 - 6A + 17 I 2 = O {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A 2 - 6A = - 17 I 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A -1 (A 2 - 6A) = A -1 (- 17 I 2 ) [Pre-multiplying both sides by A -1 ] {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A -1 A 2 - 6A -1 A = - 17 (A -1 I 2 ) {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A - 6 I 2 = - 17 A -1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A -1 = - {tex}\frac { 1 } { 17 }{/tex} (A - 6 I 2 ) = {tex}\frac { 1 } { 17 }{/tex} (6 I 2 - A) = {tex}\frac { 1 } { 17 }{/tex} {tex}\left\{ \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 6 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 6 } \end{array} \right] - \left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 2 } & { - 3 } \\ { 3 } & { 4 } \end{array} \right] \right\} = \frac { 1 } { 17 } \left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 4 } & { 3 } \\ { - 3 } & { 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
solution_html:
We have, A =​ {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 2 } & { - 3 } \\ { 3 } & { 4 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} A2 = AA = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 2 } & { - 3 } \\ { 3 } & { 4 } \end{array} \right] \left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 2 } & { - 3 } \\ { 3 } & { 4 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 4 - 9 } & { - 6 - 12 } \\ { 6 + 12 } & { - 9 + 16 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { r r } { - 5 } & { - 18 } \\ { 18 } & { 7 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
- 6 A = (- 6) {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 2 } & { - 3 } \\ { 3 } & { 4 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { r r } { - 12 } & { 18 } \\ { - 18 } & { - 24 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}and, 17 I = 17 {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 1 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 1 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 17 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 17 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\therefore{/tex} A2 - 6A + 17 I2 = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { r r } { - 5 } & { - 18 } \\ { 18 } & { 7 } \end{array} \right] + \left[ \begin{array} { r r } { - 12 } & { 18 } \\ { - 18 } & { - 24 } \end{array} \right] + \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 17 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 17 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A2 - 6A + 17 I2 = {tex}\left[ \begin{array} { r r } { - 5 - 12 + 17 } & { - 18 + 18 + 0 } \\ { 18 - 18 + 0 } & { 7 - 24 + 17 } \end{array} \right] = \left[ \begin{array} { l l } { 0 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 0 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}= O
Hence, the matrix A satisfies the equation x2 - 6x +17 = 0.
Now, A2 - 6A + 17 I2 = O
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A2 - 6A = - 17 I2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A-1 (A2 - 6A) = A-1 (- 17 I2) [Pre-multiplying both sides by A-1]
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A-1 A2 - 6A-1 A = - 17 (A-1 I2)
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A - 6 I2 = - 17 A-1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} A-1 = - {tex}\frac { 1 } { 17 }{/tex} (A - 6 I2) = {tex}\frac { 1 } { 17 }{/tex} (6 I2 - A) = {tex}\frac { 1 } { 17 }{/tex} {tex}\left\{ \left[ \begin{array} { c c } { 6 } & { 0 } \\ { 0 } & { 6 } \end{array} \right] - \left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 2 } & { - 3 } \\ { 3 } & { 4 } \end{array} \right] \right\} = \frac { 1 } { 17 } \left[ \begin{array} { r r } { 4 } & { 3 } \\ { - 3 } & { 2 } \end{array} \right]{/tex}

question_html:
Solve the system of equations by matrix method:
3x + 4y + 2z = 8
2y - 3z = 3
x - 2y + 6z = -2
question_text: Solve the system of equations by matrix method: 3x + 4y + 2z = 8 2y - 3z = 3 x - 2y + 6z = -2
explanation: The given system can be written in matrix form as: {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {3} & {4} & {2} \\ {0} & {2} & {-3} \\ {1} & {-2} & {6} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} {8} \\ {3} \\ {-2} \end{array}\right]{/tex} or AX = B A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {3} & {4} & {2} \\ {0} & {2} & {-3} \\ {1} & {-2} & {6} \end{array}\right]{/tex} , X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} {\mathrm{x}} \\ {\mathrm{y}} \\ {\mathrm{z}} \end{array}\right]{/tex} and B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c} {8} \\ {3} \\ {-2} \end{array}\right]{/tex} Now, |A| = {tex}\begin{array}{ll} {3\left|\begin{array}{cc} {2} & {-3} \\ {-2} & {6} \end{array}\right|-4\left|\begin{array}{cc} {0} & {-3} \\ {1} & {6} \end{array}\right|+2\left|\begin{array}{cc} {0} & {2} \\ {1} & {-2} \end{array}\right|} \end{array}{/tex} = 3(12 - 6) - 4(0 + 3) + 2(0 - 2) = 18 - 12 - 4 = 2 So, the above system has a unique solution, given by X = A -1 B Cofactors of A are: C 11 = (-1) 1 + 1 (12 - 6) = 6 C 21 = (-1) 2 + 1 (24 + 4) = -28 C 31 = (-1) 3 + 1 (-12 - 4) = -16 C 12 = (-1) 1 + 2 (0 + 3) = -3 C 22 = (-1) 2 + 1 18 - 2 = 16 C 32 = (-1) 3 + 1 -9 - 0 = 9 C 13 = (-1) 1 + 2 (0 - 2) = -2 C 23 = (-1) 2 + 1 (-6 - 4) = 10 C 33 = (-1) 3 + 1 6 - 0 = 6 adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {6} & {-3} & {-2} \\ {-28} & {16} & {10} \\ {-16} & {9} & {6} \end{array}\right]^{\mathrm{T}}{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {6} & {-28} & {-16} \\ {-3} & {16} & {9} \\ {-2} & {10} & {6} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}A^{-1}=\frac{1}{|A|} \text { adj } A{/tex} Now, X = A -1 B = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {6} & {-28} & {-16} \\ {-3} & {16} & {9} \\ {-2} & {10} & {6} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} {8} \\ {3} \\ {-2} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}x=\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{c} {48-84+32} \\ {-24+48-18} \\ {-16+30-12} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}X=\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{c} {-4} \\ {6} \\ {2} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}x=\left[\begin{array}{c} {-2} \\ {3} \\ {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex} Hence, X = -2, Y = 3 and Z = 1
solution: The given system can be written in matrix form as: {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {3} & {4} & {2} \\ {0} & {2} & {-3} \\ {1} & {-2} & {6} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} {8} \\ {3} \\ {-2} \end{array}\right]{/tex} or AX = B A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {3} & {4} & {2} \\ {0} & {2} & {-3} \\ {1} & {-2} & {6} \end{array}\right]{/tex} , X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} {\mathrm{x}} \\ {\mathrm{y}} \\ {\mathrm{z}} \end{array}\right]{/tex} and B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c} {8} \\ {3} \\ {-2} \end{array}\right]{/tex} Now, |A| = {tex}\begin{array}{ll} {3\left|\begin{array}{cc} {2} & {-3} \\ {-2} & {6} \end{array}\right|-4\left|\begin{array}{cc} {0} & {-3} \\ {1} & {6} \end{array}\right|+2\left|\begin{array}{cc} {0} & {2} \\ {1} & {-2} \end{array}\right|} \end{array}{/tex} = 3(12 - 6) - 4(0 + 3) + 2(0 - 2) = 18 - 12 - 4 = 2 So, the above system has a unique solution, given by X = A -1 B Cofactors of A are: C 11 = (-1) 1 + 1 (12 - 6) = 6 C 21 = (-1) 2 + 1 (24 + 4) = -28 C 31 = (-1) 3 + 1 (-12 - 4) = -16 C 12 = (-1) 1 + 2 (0 + 3) = -3 C 22 = (-1) 2 + 1 18 - 2 = 16 C 32 = (-1) 3 + 1 -9 - 0 = 9 C 13 = (-1) 1 + 2 (0 - 2) = -2 C 23 = (-1) 2 + 1 (-6 - 4) = 10 C 33 = (-1) 3 + 1 6 - 0 = 6 adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {6} & {-3} & {-2} \\ {-28} & {16} & {10} \\ {-16} & {9} & {6} \end{array}\right]^{\mathrm{T}}{/tex} {tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {6} & {-28} & {-16} \\ {-3} & {16} & {9} \\ {-2} & {10} & {6} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}A^{-1}=\frac{1}{|A|} \text { adj } A{/tex} Now, X = A -1 B = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {6} & {-28} & {-16} \\ {-3} & {16} & {9} \\ {-2} & {10} & {6} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} {8} \\ {3} \\ {-2} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}x=\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{c} {48-84+32} \\ {-24+48-18} \\ {-16+30-12} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}X=\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{c} {-4} \\ {6} \\ {2} \end{array}\right]{/tex} {tex}x=\left[\begin{array}{c} {-2} \\ {3} \\ {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex} Hence, X = -2, Y = 3 and Z = 1
solution_html:
The given system can be written in matrix form as:
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {3} & {4} & {2} \\ {0} & {2} & {-3} \\ {1} & {-2} & {6} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} {x} \\ {y} \\ {z} \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} {8} \\ {3} \\ {-2} \end{array}\right]{/tex} or AX = B
A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {3} & {4} & {2} \\ {0} & {2} & {-3} \\ {1} & {-2} & {6} \end{array}\right]{/tex}, X = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{l} {\mathrm{x}} \\ {\mathrm{y}} \\ {\mathrm{z}} \end{array}\right]{/tex} and B = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{c} {8} \\ {3} \\ {-2} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Now, |A| = {tex}\begin{array}{ll} {3\left|\begin{array}{cc} {2} & {-3} \\ {-2} & {6} \end{array}\right|-4\left|\begin{array}{cc} {0} & {-3} \\ {1} & {6} \end{array}\right|+2\left|\begin{array}{cc} {0} & {2} \\ {1} & {-2} \end{array}\right|} \end{array}{/tex}
= 3(12 - 6) - 4(0 + 3) + 2(0 - 2)
= 18 - 12 - 4
= 2
So, the above system has a unique solution, given by
X = A -1B
Cofactors of A are:
C11 = (-1)1 + 1 (12 - 6) = 6
C21 = (-1)2 + 1(24 + 4) = -28
C31 = (-1)3 + 1(-12 - 4) = -16
C12 = (-1)1 + 2 (0 + 3) = -3
C22 = (-1)2 + 1 18 - 2 = 16
C32 = (-1)3 + 1 -9 - 0 = 9
C13 = (-1)1 + 2 (0 - 2) = -2
C23 = (-1)2 + 1 (-6 - 4) = 10
C33 = (-1)3 + 1 6 - 0 = 6
adj A = {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {6} & {-3} & {-2} \\ {-28} & {16} & {10} \\ {-16} & {9} & {6} \end{array}\right]^{\mathrm{T}}{/tex}
{tex}=\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {6} & {-28} & {-16} \\ {-3} & {16} & {9} \\ {-2} & {10} & {6} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}A^{-1}=\frac{1}{|A|} \text { adj } A{/tex}
Now, X = A -1B = {tex}\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} {6} & {-28} & {-16} \\ {-3} & {16} & {9} \\ {-2} & {10} & {6} \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} {8} \\ {3} \\ {-2} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}x=\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{c} {48-84+32} \\ {-24+48-18} \\ {-16+30-12} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}X=\frac{1}{2}\left[\begin{array}{c} {-4} \\ {6} \\ {2} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
{tex}x=\left[\begin{array}{c} {-2} \\ {3} \\ {1} \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Hence, X = -2, Y = 3 and Z = 1

question_html:
Using matrix method, solve the system of equations
x + y + z = 4;
2x - y + z = -1;
2x + y - 3z = -9.
question_text: Using matrix method, solve the system of equations x + y + z = 4; 2x - y + z = -1; 2x + y - 3z = -9.
explanation: To find:- x, y, z Given set of equations are ; x + y + z = 4 2x - y + z = -1 2x + y - 3z = -9 Converting the following set of equations in matrix form, we have. AX = B {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 2 & -1 & 1 \\ 2 & 1 & -3 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 4 \\ -1 \\ -9 \end{array}\right]{/tex} R 2 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} R 2 - 2R 1 R 3 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} R 3 - 2R 1 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 0 & -3 & -1 \\ 0 & -1 & -5 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 4 \\ -9 \\ -13 \end{array}\right]{/tex} R 3 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 3R 3 - R 2 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 0 & -3 & -1 \\ 0 & 0 & -14 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 4 \\ -9 \\ -42 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Again converting into equations, we get x + y + z = 4 -3y - z = - 9 -14z = - 42 z = 3 -3y - 3 = - 9 -3y = - 6 y = 2 x + 2 + 3 = 4 x = 4 - 5 x = - 1 {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = -1, y = 2, z = 3
solution: To find:- x, y, z Given set of equations are ; x + y + z = 4 2x - y + z = -1 2x + y - 3z = -9 Converting the following set of equations in matrix form, we have. AX = B {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 2 & -1 & 1 \\ 2 & 1 & -3 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 4 \\ -1 \\ -9 \end{array}\right]{/tex} R 2 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} R 2 - 2R 1 R 3 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} R 3 - 2R 1 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 0 & -3 & -1 \\ 0 & -1 & -5 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 4 \\ -9 \\ -13 \end{array}\right]{/tex} R 3 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 3R 3 - R 2 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 0 & -3 & -1 \\ 0 & 0 & -14 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 4 \\ -9 \\ -42 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Again converting into equations, we get x + y + z = 4 -3y - z = - 9 -14z = - 42 z = 3 -3y - 3 = - 9 -3y = - 6 y = 2 x + 2 + 3 = 4 x = 4 - 5 x = - 1 {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = -1, y = 2, z = 3
solution_html:
To find:- x, y, z
Given set of  equations are ;
x + y + z = 4
2x - y + z = -1
2x + y - 3z = -9
Converting the following set of equations in matrix form, we have.
AX = B
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 2 & -1 & 1 \\ 2 & 1 & -3 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 4 \\ -1 \\ -9 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
R2 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} R2 - 2R1
R3 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} R3 - 2R1
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 0 & -3 & -1 \\ 0 & -1 & -5 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 4 \\ -9 \\ -13 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
R3 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 3R3 - R2
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 1 & 1 \\ 0 & -3 & -1 \\ 0 & 0 & -14 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 4 \\ -9 \\ -42 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Again converting into equations, we get
x + y + z = 4
-3y - z = - 9
-14z = - 42
z = 3
-3y - 3 = - 9
-3y = - 6
y = 2
x + 2 + 3 = 4
x = 4 - 5
x = - 1
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = -1, y = 2, z = 3

question_html:
Using matrix method, solve the system of equations
2x - 3y + 5z = 16;
3x + 2y - 4z = -4;
x + y - 2z = -3.
question_text: Using matrix method, solve the system of equations 2x - 3y + 5z = 16; 3x + 2y - 4z = -4; x + y - 2z = -3.
explanation: Given set of lines are : - 2x - 3y + 5z = 16; 3x + 2y - 4z = - 4 x + y - 2z = -3 Converting the following equations in matrix form, AX = B {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & -3 & 5 \\ 3 & 2 & -4 \\ 1 & 1 & -2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 16 \\ -4 \\ -3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} 2R 2 - 3R 1 2R 3 - R 1 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & -3 & 5 \\ 0 & 13 & -23 \\ 0 & 5 & -9 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 16 \\ -56 \\ -22 \end{array}\right]{/tex} 13R 3 - 5R 2 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & -3 & 5 \\ 0 & 13 & -23 \\ 0 & 0 & -2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 16 \\ -56 \\ -6 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Again converting into equations, we get 2x - 3y + 5z = 16 13y - 23z = - 56 -2z = -6 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} z = 3 13y - 23 × 3 = -56 13y = - 56 + 69 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = 1 2x - 3 × 1 + 5 × 3 = 16 2x = 16 + 3 - 15 2x = 4 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2 {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 2, y = 1, z = 3
solution: Given set of lines are : - 2x - 3y + 5z = 16; 3x + 2y - 4z = - 4 x + y - 2z = -3 Converting the following equations in matrix form, AX = B {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & -3 & 5 \\ 3 & 2 & -4 \\ 1 & 1 & -2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 16 \\ -4 \\ -3 \end{array}\right]{/tex} 2R 2 - 3R 1 2R 3 - R 1 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & -3 & 5 \\ 0 & 13 & -23 \\ 0 & 5 & -9 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 16 \\ -56 \\ -22 \end{array}\right]{/tex} 13R 3 - 5R 2 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & -3 & 5 \\ 0 & 13 & -23 \\ 0 & 0 & -2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 16 \\ -56 \\ -6 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Again converting into equations, we get 2x - 3y + 5z = 16 13y - 23z = - 56 -2z = -6 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} z = 3 13y - 23 × 3 = -56 13y = - 56 + 69 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = 1 2x - 3 × 1 + 5 × 3 = 16 2x = 16 + 3 - 15 2x = 4 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2 {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 2, y = 1, z = 3
solution_html:
Given set of lines are : -
2x - 3y + 5z = 16;
3x + 2y - 4z = - 4
x + y - 2z = -3
Converting the following equations in matrix form,
AX = B
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & -3 & 5 \\ 3 & 2 & -4 \\ 1 & 1 & -2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 16 \\ -4 \\ -3 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
2R2 - 3R1
2R3 - R1
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & -3 & 5 \\ 0 & 13 & -23 \\ 0 & 5 & -9 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 16 \\ -56 \\ -22 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
13R3 - 5R2
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & -3 & 5 \\ 0 & 13 & -23 \\ 0 & 0 & -2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 16 \\ -56 \\ -6 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Again converting into equations, we get
2x - 3y + 5z = 16
13y - 23z = - 56
-2z = -6
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}z = 3
13y - 23 × 3 = -56
13y = - 56 + 69
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = 1
2x - 3 × 1 + 5 × 3 = 16
2x = 16 + 3 - 15
2x = 4
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 2, y = 1, z = 3

question_html:
Using matrix method, solve the system of equations
2x + y - z = -1;
x + y + z = 2;
3x + y - 2z = -1.
question_text: Using matrix method, solve the system of equations 2x + y - z = -1; x + y + z = 2; 3x + y - 2z = -1.
explanation: To find:- x, y, z Given set of equations are : - 2x + y - z = 1 x - y + z = 2 3x + y - 2z = -1 Converting the following set of equations in matrix form, we have AX = B {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 1 & -1 \\ 1 & -1 & 1 \\ 3 & 1 & -2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 1 \\ 2 \\ -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} R 2 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 2R 2 - R 1 R 3 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 2R 3 - 3R1 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 1 & -1 \\ 0 & -3 & 3 \\ 0 & -1 & -1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 1 \\ 3 \\ -5 \end{array}\right]{/tex} R 3 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 3R 3 - R 2 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 1 & -1 \\ 0 & -3 & 3 \\ 0 & 0 & -6 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 1 \\ 3 \\ -18 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Again converting into equations we get 2x + y - z = 1 -3y + 3z = 3 -6z = - 18 z = 3 -3y + 3×3 = 3 -3y = 3 - 9 -3y = - 6 y = 2 2x + 2 - 3 = 1 2x = 1 + 1 x = 1 {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 1 , y = 2 , z = 3
solution: To find:- x, y, z Given set of equations are : - 2x + y - z = 1 x - y + z = 2 3x + y - 2z = -1 Converting the following set of equations in matrix form, we have AX = B {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 1 & -1 \\ 1 & -1 & 1 \\ 3 & 1 & -2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 1 \\ 2 \\ -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} R 2 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 2R 2 - R 1 R 3 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 2R 3 - 3R1 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 1 & -1 \\ 0 & -3 & 3 \\ 0 & -1 & -1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 1 \\ 3 \\ -5 \end{array}\right]{/tex} R 3 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex} 3R 3 - R 2 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 1 & -1 \\ 0 & -3 & 3 \\ 0 & 0 & -6 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 1 \\ 3 \\ -18 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Again converting into equations we get 2x + y - z = 1 -3y + 3z = 3 -6z = - 18 z = 3 -3y + 3×3 = 3 -3y = 3 - 9 -3y = - 6 y = 2 2x + 2 - 3 = 1 2x = 1 + 1 x = 1 {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 1 , y = 2 , z = 3
solution_html:
To find:- x, y, z
Given set of equations are : -
2x + y - z = 1
x - y + z = 2
3x + y - 2z = -1
Converting the following set of equations in matrix form, we have
AX = B
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 1 & -1 \\ 1 & -1 & 1 \\ 3 & 1 & -2 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 1 \\ 2 \\ -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
R2 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex}2R2 - R1
R3 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex}2R3 - 3R1
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 1 & -1 \\ 0 & -3 & 3 \\ 0 & -1 & -1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 1 \\ 3 \\ -5 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
R3 {tex}\rightarrow{/tex}3R3 - R2
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 2 & 1 & -1 \\ 0 & -3 & 3 \\ 0 & 0 & -6 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 1 \\ 3 \\ -18 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Again converting into equations we get
2x + y - z = 1
-3y + 3z = 3
-6z = - 18
z = 3
-3y + 3×3 = 3
-3y = 3 - 9
-3y = - 6
y = 2
2x + 2 - 3 = 1
2x = 1 + 1
x = 1
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 1 , y = 2 , z = 3

subject: Mathematics
question_html:
Using matrix method, solve the system of equations
x + y - z = 1;
3x + y - 2z = 3;
x - y - z = -1.
question_text: Using matrix method, solve the system of equations x + y - z = 1; 3x + y - 2z = 3; x - y - z = -1.
explanation: Given set of lines are:- x + y - z = 1 3x + y - 2z = 3 x - y - z = - 1 Converting the following equations in matrix form, AX = B {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 1 & -1 \\ 3 & 1 & -2 \\ 1 & -1 & -1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 1 \\ 3 \\ -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} R 2 - 3R 1 R 3 - R 1 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 1 & -1 \\ 0 & -2 & 1 \\ 0 & -2 & 0 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 1 \\ 0 \\ -2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Again converting into equations we get x + y - z = 1 -2y + z = 0 -2y = - 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = 1 -2 + z = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} z = 2 x + 1 - 2 = 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2 {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 2, y = 1, z = 2
solution: Given set of lines are:- x + y - z = 1 3x + y - 2z = 3 x - y - z = - 1 Converting the following equations in matrix form, AX = B {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 1 & -1 \\ 3 & 1 & -2 \\ 1 & -1 & -1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 1 \\ 3 \\ -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex} R 2 - 3R 1 R 3 - R 1 {tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 1 & -1 \\ 0 & -2 & 1 \\ 0 & -2 & 0 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 1 \\ 0 \\ -2 \end{array}\right]{/tex} Again converting into equations we get x + y - z = 1 -2y + z = 0 -2y = - 2 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = 1 -2 + z = 0 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} z = 2 x + 1 - 2 = 1 {tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2 {tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 2, y = 1, z = 2
solution_html:
Given set of lines are:-
x + y - z = 1
3x + y - 2z = 3
x - y - z = - 1
Converting the following equations in matrix form,
AX = B
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 1 & -1 \\ 3 & 1 & -2 \\ 1 & -1 & -1 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 1 \\ 3 \\ -1 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
R2 - 3R1
R3 - R1
{tex}\left[\begin{array}{ccc} 1 & 1 & -1 \\ 0 & -2 & 1 \\ 0 & -2 & 0 \end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{l} x \\ y \\ z \end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c} 1 \\ 0 \\ -2 \end{array}\right]{/tex}
Again converting into equations we get
x + y - z = 1
-2y + z = 0
-2y = - 2
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} y = 1
-2 + z = 0
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex}z = 2
x + 1 - 2 = 1
{tex}\Rightarrow{/tex} x = 2
{tex}\therefore{/tex} x = 2, y = 1, z = 2
